title: TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SUPPORT when Amazon sends you a broken one!
body: DO NOT BUY FIRE TV DEVICES!!! CHOOSE ANOTHER STREAMING PLAYER BRAND!!!Worst customer service experience in my life from Amazon!!!! Amazon sent me a completely broken Fire TV 4k stick! From day one the audio cuts out entirely after a few mins of playing a video, and does not return until stick is physically unplugged from power. Then it cuts out again. Problem occurs in all video apps, and I tried full software restore, plugging into multiple different TVs, as well as different audio connections (direct to TV through HDMI, Through receiver, through bluetooth on multiple devices.) And my other Fire TV 4k of the same model works fine in this same TV, so... THE STICK IS BROKEN! So I contact Amazon through their chat interface and Parmish is a total disaster at helping me. I tell him that I have done a full software restore on it, at which point he responds that I need to do a full software restore. Ya know, the thing I just told him that I did already. Then I tell him I have already tried plugging the fire TV directly into the TV with no sound receiver, and he says "well than what I need you to do is plug the fire TV directly into the TV with no sound receiver." This happened over and over. It was like a bad joke. But I wasn't laughing. Finally after about an hour of this nonsense he decides that my Fire TV stick needs to be replaced. Then he says I "Have no card on file", what ever that means. So after about 10 more unanswered and ignored questions to him about just what he's talking about I finally figure out he means my Amazon account has no credit card on file. But it does have a card on file. I log in and verify this. The card is up to date and working, and amazon had just charged it that day even. So I tell him this, and he responds that I need a credit card on file in order to send out a replacement, even though it won't be charged. I tell him again that the card is on file and working, and he says it need to be updated. Even though it does not. So I hit "edit" on my card info on file, and resubmit the same working card. Parmish sees this on his end and says thank you. Then he says I need to update my OTHER CARD on file. I look and there is an old credit card from 6 years ago on the account from a bank I no longer use, and its not even selected as the active/favored card, so It doesn't even matter that it there, but I delete it anyways just to be sure. Then Parmish says I need to reenter and update that old card (that I no longer bank with) in order to get a replacement sent out. At this point I'm done with Parmish. The whole conversation was absolutely ridiculous! I say "thank you for the help but I need a supervisor. Please transfer me to a supervisor."So Parmish transfers me to Vikas. I ask Vikas if he is a supervisor and he says "no." (Are you surprised by this? I wasn't.) So I explain that I was suppose to be transferred to a supervisor and ask that Vikas transfer me directly to a supervisor. And I'm transferred to Annal.I ask Annal if they are a supervisor, and they answer... get ready for it... are you ready... they answer ..."No" !!!!!!!!! They &^^&%&$^^%^ answer "No."So I close the chat window I just wasted over an hour on and I call Amazon. This is where the real nightmare begins. I start off with a regular customer service rep and I ask her to transfer me to a supervisor right away because this will be my 4th attempt to get a supervisor. She is very polite and gets me a supervisor on the line write away. I explain the entire issue with the Fire Tv 4k Stick and everything I have done to troubleshoot it already YET AGIAN, and the supervisor apologizes for all the insane trouble I've been having. She sounds like she really wants to help me, asked some more questions, and then tells me that she can't replace this particular model because its out of stock. I had just checked the stock, so I knew it wasn't out. I checked it again, and then told her it appears to have plenty of stock. She verified what model it was again, then said "oh yes, I guess we do have it, let my put you on hold while I try to send you one." She comes back a while later and says "the system won't let me send you one. I have to transfer you to a Fire TV Specialist, buy I'll get you a manager there as well.So then I have to go through the whole thing again with this new Fire TV Specialist person. She keeps putting me on hold to check things over and over again. And she has me do crazy things - For example, she insists I go to my TV, unplug the other working Fire TV I now have in there, and plug this broken one back in, then load it up and read the serial number off of it to her from the info screen. Why? Doesn't she already have that info? She should. And WHY AM I PLUGGING A $25 DEFECTIVE BROKEN DEVICE BACK INTO MY $2,000 FLATSCREEN??!!! I don't want to do that. I shouldn't be asked to do that. What the hell? Then she keeps checking to see if I'm still in warranty. REALL???!!! I JUST got this stick in the mail. I JUST GOT IT. How long is the warranty period that she has to keep checking on.. 5 hours after purchase. Also, this is a brand new model that just came out. HOW COULD ANY OF THEM POSSIBLY BE OUT OF WARRANTY??!!Finally after 3 HOURS of talking to people about this and a final, particularly long hold while the Fire TV Specialist person tries to figure out if I can be sent a replacement - I give up. I tell her I'll just return it instead - just so I can just get off the phone. But I'm NOT returning it - That too would probably turn into another huge nightmare. It's not worth my $25 for all this aggravation. So... I'M DONE. This is ridiculous! After speaking with a total of SIX people for over THREE HOURS.. My brand new Fire TV 4K stick in now in my trash can and I'm never buying a Fire TV product again. All Amazon need to do was have me send in my broken one and then send out a replacement. That's It. They could have sorted out all their serial numbers and insane data requirements after they got my stick back. AS a customer, I simply got something in the mail that arrived broken and I wanted it replaced soon after I got it. How simple of a transition is that? But instead I got this torrent of poor customer service and bizarre data mining. SO.. Officially.. I'm no longer a Fire TV user.I'm currently switching my entire smart TV ecosystem for the entire house over to Google's Android TV now, I've had it with Fire TV, and I suggest you do the same. Android TV systems don't suffer from the same app limitations that Amazon does (IE with Fire TV you get access got about 12% of the Android apps available to the rest of the Android TV world, Even though Fire TV runs on Android, and you can install what ever apps you want on your Android TV device without having to TRICK IT into installing it), it still works and runs apps (like playing a game or looking at photos) even if your internet connection goes down (Fire TV becomes a useless brick without constant internet), and the whole thing just seems to run better. I don't have time to deal with Amazon's nightmare anymore. All I needed was for Amazon to replace the broken Fire TV stick they JUST sent me, and they can't even do that. So, ummmm... BYEEEEEEEEEE!!!!!

title: Wrong product
body: I love Amazon products but this is a failure. No you can't Stream for free. You need to pay for your shows subscriptions.REMOTE DOESN'T WORK PROPERLY!I agree with other reviewer. My story is the same. Tried almost everything he mentioned.So you’ve heard about cutting the cord and you’re excited to save a little money, or you remember when you could stream just about any movie on Netflix, and you think a Fire TV Stick is the answer. The Amazon Fire TV Stick isn’t very expensive, but if you don’t wish to deal with the stress or disappointment, do your research first.After getting sick of paying for a whole cable subscription to watch nothing but local news, I installed a $600 antenna, only to find out the only channels available over the air in my area are PBS. After hearing about the Fire TV Stick, I thought I had found the answer. I was wrong.NOTHING on the Firestick is free. You will still have to create accounts through secondary companies. Most require a subscription which also risks identity and personal security. Based upon my significant other’s three favorite shows and my three favorite shows, the fees and subscriptions would pay our cable bill, and we’re unable to view everything on Firestick that we can with our basic cable subscription.Even worse, all the Fire TV Stick does is function as a hardware device which combines your choice of apps for viewing on a television. All my phones and all my laptops do that with most of my televisions already. The Fire TV Stick doesn’t magically let you watch anything for free.It’s Christmas time and we want to watch Christmas Vacation. Even with a Netflix subscription, I’m still expected to pay a rental fee. I thought I could find it and stream it for free, somewhere. Nope. The Fire Stick must have it somewhere. Nope. I finally got mad and jail broke my Firestick and installed Kodi. Still no! I can find it and I can stream it, but I still must pay a rental fee. This was the final straw and my Firestick just moved to its final resting place in the dumpster.If not being able to cut the cord and watch similar programming for free isn’t an issue, there’s more. You want to watch a specific movie or TV program? There’s no search function. Select your category or genre and click, click, click………..it’s been thirty minutes and I still haven’t found Christmas Vacation, AND IT’S CHRISTMAS TIME!!!Local TV? Not on Firestick! Local broadcasts and over the air TV are necessary to aid in keeping the population informed. If you cut the cord and rely on Firestick for entertainment, you and the rest of the Firestick population will certainly be so uninformed that certain politicians may or may not make it into office. Yet, at least you’ll be shielded from the stress of the campaign. Forest fires are heading in your direction and it’s time to evacuate. You won’t have local programming so at least you won’t have to pack your belongings since you don’t know your house will burn down. Amazon needs to realize what laws now apply to them and they need to make local programming and over the air broadcasts available on the Fire TV Stick FOR FREE! That’s why it’s free. Amazon’s technology has exceeded current systems which identifies a change in infrastructure. If the Firestick is reaching a broader population, Amazons structure must change as well.Enjoy watching a specific TV show? If you find it and pay to watch it, you must select it by the season and episode. For a device which is marketed for its ease of use, there are excessive steps necessary just to view the latest episode of your favorite show. I downloaded other apps and even when using the search bar in the apps, if I try to use the voice control it reverts to the Firestick and not the app I’m using. It will kick me out and take me back to the home screen with basically a Netflix search, nothing else. It’s not easy to use, as marketed.Even worse, I heard I had to install Kodi to make it work the way I want. I did and as I previously explained, everything still requires a subscription. I’m not done yet, I didn’t realize what I was doing is called “jail breaking” and now I’m at risk of my IP address being tracked and if I’m caught illegally viewing TV or movies, I will lose my internet service and I can be liable for royalties and fees. I can’t risk losing what I’ve worked so hard to gain, hence why my Firestick went in the trash can. If you think you can cut the cord and view TV or movies for free, you will be taking a legal risk of liability.This is actually my second review. After my first review an Amazon representative called me and asked what and why and all kinds of other questions; not interested in my true opinion, but simply to argue with me over the valid points I was making. In the end, when I explained my problem is not with Amazon, my complaint is that the Firestick does not operate as I expected it to, based upon the marketing and advertising intended to deceive the public. The Firestick functions as Amazon intends it to, but the deception Amazon has created is where my disappointment stems. The Amazon representative deleted my review…yet another deception. The Amazon Fire TV Stick is a big deception, disappointment, and legal liability.--

title: Fire TV Stick 4K Does Not Work With (my) LG TV
body: The Fire TV Stick 4K does not work properly with my LG TV. I have tried 3 different Fire TV Stick 4K’s, I have contacted Amazon Support several times and the product simply does not work regardless of the connection configuration I use. Amazon Support tries to tell me it’s my LG TV, it’s is not. It is their Fire TV Stick 4K. The problem I had is a known issue. Here is an article on HelpDeskGeek.com (https://helpdeskgeek.com/help-desk/a-detailed-guide-on-fixing-black-screen-issues-on-fire-tv-stick/) that talks all about it and suggested changes. I have tried all of these and none of them worked. What DID WORK was replacing my Fire Stick TV 4K with a Fire TV Cube. Amazon Support refused to make this swap for me so now I am out the (significant) difference in price.Here are the details on my failed Fire TV Stick 4K experience…About 2 ½ months ago I bought my first Fire TV Stick 4K at a local warehouse club. I already had a Fire TV Cube on my main TV in the family room and this was for a 2nd TV in the bedroom.Please note, the Fire TV Cube has performed exceptionally well on the main TV all this time. The software on the Fire TV Stick 4K presents exactly the same interface as the Cube so I was looking for equally good performance. The issue with the Fire TV Stick 4K is that when you turn it on, sometimes it comes up with the home screen and sometimes my TV is just a black screen with the message “NO SIGNAL” bouncing around. The only way to correct the problem is to remove power from the Fire TV Stick 4K and then restore it, letting it restart the device. The longest the device ever went without requiring rebooting was about a week days, but most times it won’t even go an entire day.I started experimenting with different configurations. My TV has 2 HDMI ports. I have the ability to plug the adapter into a power strip or directly into the wall. And I have 2.4Ghz Wi-Fi, 5.0Ghz Wi-Fi and (using the adapter that came with my Fire TV Cube), wired internet. I tried every combination of HDMI ports, A/C power source (wall or power strip) and internet connection. All with the same results - Fire TV Stick 4K works great for a while but after a day or so, it won’t turn back on again.At this point I contacted Amazon for support. It happened to be Prime Days and the Fire TV Stick 4K was on sale for $25. The rep could not come up with a solution and was getting ready to send out a replacement. I suggested returning the Fire TV Stick 4K to the local warehouse club and buying a new one through Amazon on Prime Days which she thought was the best approach.The 2nd Fire TV Stick 4K came from Amazon. I set it up and immediately having EXACTLY the same issue. I tried a handful of combinations of HDMI ports, A/C power source and internet connection to no avail. So back on the phone with Amazon support. At this point they started pointing fingers at my LG TV as the problem. I don’t disagree that the TV was part of the issue, but I explained to them that prior to connecting the Fire TV Stick 4K, I had a FiOS set top box on HDMI 1 for two years and prior to that I had a DirecTV DVR on HDMI 1 for more than five years. Neither of these had any issue communicating with my LG TV, not once, ever.Since my Fire TV Cube in the other room was working so well, I asked Amazon support if they could possibly send out a Fire TV Cube as a replacement. They said this was impossible and said since the devices ran the same software, the Cube would probably act the same. They instead sent out a 3rd Fire TV Stick 4K. The new Stick came, and while I wasn’t expecting better results, I set it up. As expected, it performed just like the 1st & 2nd Sticks.During the next call to support, I pushed the Fire TV Cube replacement even harder. I said I would pay the $55 price difference that existed on Prime Days. They said that was impossible. In fact, since they already replaced the device once, all they could offer was a return for a refund of the $25 I paid during Prime Days. I told them to go ahead and set up the return & refund while I tried to decide what to do. Of course I could go with another streaming device like Roku or NVIDIA Shield, but then the interface would be different than my Fire TV Cube on the main TV.While I was trying to figure out my next move, I came across a sale for the Fire TV Cube at Best Buy. They had it for $80, matching the Prime Days price. So I picked one up from Best Buy, set it up on my LG TV and it is working flawlessly, just like the Fire TV Cube on my main TV. In fact today marks ONE MONTH without a single black screen using the Fire TV Cube. With the return/refund on my Fire TV Stick 4K, I have effectively paid the additional $55 to get a working set-up in my bedroom. I’m not happy that I had to spend the extra money when I need NONE of the features the Cube offers over the Stick 4K. I would have preferred if Amazon would have either paid or helped pay the $55 difference since it was their product not working that forced me to upgrade, but at least I have a working TV again.

title: Slow, Forgetful, Updates every week, 50% Failure rate, Keep your Roku
body: I have used Roku since version 1 and have several around the house. In addition as an Amazon fan I purchased two of the original Fire TV sticks and immediately recognized that they were inferior to Roku so I shelved them. Then this year Amazon released Fire TV Recast for antenna TV and I wanted to implement that platform for my OTA channels. To do so I needed updated Fire TV Sticks and ended up buying four of the Fire TV Stick 4K version. This is my experience dealing with the subpar Fire TV Stick 4K devices.Installing the devices is a challenge due to the 5 foot USB cord which means buying a 6 foot cord, using a USB extender, or adding an extension cord for power. Amazon could charge me 23 cents more for a cord that works rather than their standard 5 foot USB cord.Once connected to power and WiFi each of the Fire TV Stick devices requires multiple updates. This process can take up to an hour per stick including the app updates. While Amazon offers to store your WiFi password you must configure every app for every service with your user name and password. If you are doing this one time for one Fire TV stick, then no big deal, you sort of expect this. However doing it four times for four Fire TV stick devices to configure 6 apps on each one, that process takes a day.Once setup, the Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K devices are much slower than a Roku. Even Gen 3 Roku devices are 4 to 6 times faster to wake etc. There is a measurable time between pressing a button on the Amazon Fire TV Stick remote and actions on the screen. The delay is long enough that it seems like your remote commands may be going out through the Internet, being read by Amazon servers, then sent back to the Fire TV Stick device. Seriously you can count between pressing the button and the screen action occurring. This all sort of makes sense if the buttons are just sending pre-programmed Amazon Alexa commands, which require Amazon servers to interpret.With all the - not as good as Roku - tolerance and acceptance I committed and began using the Fire TV Stick 4K devices along with the Fire Recast TV device. Then 22 days in the first Fire TV Stick failed. The TV screen went blank in the middle of a show and five hours of troubleshooting later I figured out the thing is dead.With one down and three to go I moved another unit to replace the dead Fire TV Stick only to find that the second Fire TV Stick 4K with Alexa remote will not remember that it is connected to a 2 year old Samsung TV which means the volume and power buttons stop working after about 3 hours. After hours of troubleshooting that issue I decided to swap remotes since I had a spare from the dead Fire TV device. However, the problem remains which means the Fire TV Stick 4K device itself will not remember the TV.Now I have a 50% failure rate with Amazon Fire TV devices and have spent more than a dozen hours just trying to make the things work or keep working. In total I have spent more time working on Amazon Fire TV Stick devices in the last month than I spent working on the 12 Roku devices I have owned over the last 8 years or so. This is a sad statement about the quality of Amazon Fire TV Stick devices.I plan to use the last two Fire TV Stick 4K devices until they die, which I expect will be by the end of this year. Then I will call the whole thing a bad and expensive experiment, and give up on the Fire Recast TV which is a shame because that device has been rock solid, once you figure out the setup hurdles.As the Fire TV Stick devices fail I am just plugging my Roku devices back in and am pleasantly reminded of how well Roku devices work.

title: Lags, buggy, crashes, disconnects. More trouble than it is worth.
body: UPDATED 4/27/19:After about a year and a half, this thing has proved to be more trouble than it’s worth. Constant lagging in loading apps and video thumbnails, regular crashing, locking up, having to restart regularly and even having to unplug it completely.Almost every time I use it, it will spin and say the connection is down. A quick test will show the signal strength as strong and all my other devices are still connected to my WiFi and working.Ultimately there is never a problem-free evening with the firestick. And even when it is working, it takes way too long to refresh your list via the Prime video page. Ironically Netflix seems to load and operate smoother than Prime. But just trying to browse titles and previews is a headache. I keep my phone beside of me to browse and add titles from the iPhone Prime app because it’s such a mess to use the firestick to perform those functions.Sometimes it just freezes up completely, the thumbnails eventually reload and you start all over.I write this as an honest and genuine longtime customer (and longtime Prime member). The Roku is just a much better device all around for every function whether you’re watching Prime movies, Netflix, Hulu, mirroring your phone, even streaming YouTube.I imagine the issue is the operating system but the regular glitches, crashes, slow/buggy operations, constant refreshes when browsing make this device way more trouble than it is worth and almost unusable at times. I cannot for the life of me figure out how they have not designed a streaming device that doesn’t even operate with their own streaming service. I was never a huge Roku fan but compared to the firestick, even my old/outdated Roku outperforms firestick by miles.My gut tells me that problems are much more common with the firestick than these reviews reflect. I received mine as a gift and even though the price is right, it’s not really a bargain at any price if it doesn’t function.Addendum: 10/02/2020An update to assure everyone that after getting a new 4K Fire Stick, I’m still having all of the same issues with the unbearable lag in loading eveything from thumbnail to having to wait upwards of 2 minutes for a Prime movie to load. Even navigating through the settings and browsing apps is a process that is frustrating due to how slow it is. Sometimes the whole home page will simply reload itself. But as I mentioned last year, the most puzzling part is that after all this time Amazon isn’t even able to manage and process their own streaming service with any real efficiency.I stand by my claim that Roku processes Prime movies much quicker and without the bugs, lags and crashes.Go figure. I used the Fire Stick tonight because I had some issues with my other TV and surprise, I had issues just adding movies to my queue. After they were added, they wouldn’t show up on my queue (or as Amazon calls it, ‘your movies’).Anyone have an idea of why Roku can seamlessly manage/play Prime movies but the Fire Stick at the highest processing speed available can’t load my queue or stream those movies without a significant lag?Just to confirm, this isn’t an flaw with my specific device. Between gifts/replacements have 3 of the 4K sticks at this point. I actually keep one connected just use one to turn my TV on and control the volume. It’s great for that, ironically.Just don’t try to stream anything with it except Netflix.

title: They eat up your GB with garbage so less space for apps you actually want.
body: It 'SAYS' you can cast, like from your phone TO the Fire stick. Ive had 3 different brands of phone since my first fire stick. Mysteriously, Not one of them has been able to connect. You're better off with Chromecast or a room if you want this feature because Amazon is straight up lying that it works on their proprietary fire stick. Can't get Google anything on it so forget playing any Google purchased videos. Won't even work thru the fire stick browser OR a downloaded Firefox browser. AMazon has balls charging $40 for a product that doesnt even work as ADVERTISED. Spent 5min on the phone with a customer service rep and after a couple of questions, her go-to solution is right to factory reset. Seriously? Yeah, no big deal for her to have to re-login to everything! The service on this thing, all around, is shotty. Streaming, video quality, connection. I'm pissed that I spent money to upgrade. To This.It advertises at 8gb. But after their requirements to operate, youre down to 5.64gb of space. But Then you have to update it so you actually go down to just over 4gb of avail space. Remeber the old days of small phones and comouters? Have to delete 5 apps so you have this one other one in the aoo store? Dleting FB so you can use a browser then delete the browser so you out fb back on it? Welcome back to that philosophy. Then, As soon as you drop below 1gb, you'll get constant alerts that you're out of space and need to delete. Because Amazon eats up so much of the left over space with forced crap apps. I don't use IMDB. Can't remove it. Amazon FreeTime (for kids)? No kids but Can't remove it. And several others, that SHOULD BE A CHOICE!!!!!!!! I had my basic firestick for years and never had to delete anything, at any point. I got this 4K version in Nov 2019, and by April, my live TV option had been removed. and by july, with 3 apps, i was getting errors left & right that i was running out space. Even tho my apps amounted to not even 1gb. Customer service says the only fix is to reset to default. Now, without adding anything new, Other than what I always had on it, I'm suddenly getting errors that I'm out of space and must delete. I barely have half the apps I once did yet I'm constantly being told to remove. Nothing worked and they sent me a new one. Thats when i discovered that they are using up even more of your avail space for all the forced garbage. Those apps should be a choice. With only 8gb to start with? And the operating system already coming out of that, on top of the increased amount used to update it, when setup? The forced apps are just blindly robbing us of More space and they Still think its worth $40 freaking dollars? When your stuck missing out on so many android apps and the ability to actually cast from another device? (Unsure of just many side apps i tried, as well but conveniently and mysteriously, not one of them worked.) Its a rip off. Amazon as a whole is a complete and greedy joke. It's turned into merely an upgraded version of wish.com or picnsave! And I'm now looking for a Roku or Chromecast. JB needs to be kicked in the face with a reality check for thinking this acceptable to be ripping people off like this. For $40, there should be access to way more than 8 GB on this thing! ANd not so much of it used up by Amazon proprietary garbage that will never get used but every customer.

title: Amazon is Failing in the Technology Space
body: Amazon seems to be rushing to put new technology out and everything I've gotten from them in the past 4 years still isn't working properly and hasn't been fixed. I have the first generation Echo Dot and Fire TV and purchased the 4th Generation Echo and this 4th Gen Fire TV 4k stick. There are no combinations of devices I have that will accurately communicate to complete a simple task to play a live channel and sometimes not movie channels in Netflix, and the only devices involved are Amazon made, aside from a dumb TV. I can instruct Alexa to play any news channel and she will know where to go but not always able to get all the way to opening the channel. Sometimes she does, but it's less than 50% unless you catch it again or change channels and come back soon after. It sometimes opens the app, like Netflix, and gets to the destination Series show, but doesn't open the episode, or whatever is at the destination. It will finally complete the task and play the selection if you give Alexa the exact same command a second time after it's gotten almost there, or you can pick up the remote and click enter. Mind you, BOTH of these devices are Amazon made and they can't even effectively speak to each other. Other problems from 4 years ago still exist. Sadly, my first generation of both devices works much better than the 4th gen of both in any combination, and better in all categories. Further, THIS Fire TV 4K device has a weird problem where it makes the voice sound higher pitched than it is, like it is sped up (like a chipmunk, as some complained on the internet, so it's a known problem, too). It doesn't seem that the video is sped up, only the voice. This doesn't happen on the 1st gen and I'm still quite happy with most of how it operates, only the software is still a problem there. But, you can't expect Amazon devices to interact well, and this is a problem with ALL their products. It's only exacerbated by their poor tech support who doesn't keep track of past problems, so you keep starting at square 1 when you have to call more than once about repetitive or persistent issues. Their engineers don't know how to fix anything, and their software menu system recently changed for the worse, as they just keep cranking out and creating more new messes to add to old ones! I don't like any of their products any more and I almost can't communicate with Alexa any more. It also pesters customers with nonsense like failed "Recommendations" based on a failed assumption of what it thinks it heard me say. Never should this device give ANY recommendations if the first thing it says is, "I didn't quite hear that"... but I have some recommendations that might help you. NO, THEY NEVER DO!! This is severely broken as a working device. UPDATE: The remote for this broke after 4 months, the battery door was loose when I got it, now it doesn't stay on. This TONE-DEAF Amazonians send me a remote with DISNEY, HULU, NETFLIX AND PRIME silkscreened on the buttons of the remote!!! WTF is wrong with these tone-deaf people??????? NO ONE likes DISNEY any more and doesn't want to see their name on their devices, especially when most people are boycotting them! I'm so done with the moronic way this place is operated and where it's headed. They need to just go ahead and move to China and get out of this Country they hate so much!!!

title: Don’t have a 4K tv? Still the better choice! Until now.
body: I use this fire stick with my Toshiba 32” HD(not 4K) tv. And I couldn’t be happier!I’ll set this up into pros and cons based on my personal experience.Pros:-They set it up to your account before sending it to you! (It’s the little things that count!!)-works with any tv (as far as I know) that has an hdmi port-no lengthy setup requiring you to program remote. It figured out my tv and the volume controls worked from the get go! (No idea how that works but I love it!)-Alexa!-Alexa!-Alexa! I never thought I’d use this assistant, as my echo dot sits and does literally nothing. (I actually don’t know if it’s plugged in haha), but I can tell her right through the remote “watch Archer on Hulu” and she goes there right away. Or if I want to switch it up mid episode I can say “watch The Crown on Netflix” and it switches no problem! Love Alexa for its only useful application for me personally!-Volume Controls!!! My goodness. Having 8 remotes just to make a tv work sucks. So an extra obviously necessary touch to the remote is a great idea!-Fast. Haven’t had any point of lagging yet!Cons:-I have issues with connectivity on stick. It says “connection error” every once in a while and will stop playing a video. I don’t have issues with any other device or even my other streaming devices in the same location. If I clear the notification it brings me back to the home screen and works fine and is connected to the internet. Makes you lose your mind during a movie. When it cuts out. This could probably be fixed with an update as, once again I don’t have issues with other streaming devices in the same location.-I only want one more button on my remote. Input! I love watching Netflix and such but having to switch to like a gaming system or DVD player has now become a hassle. It’s nice to have volume but I could completely pack up the tv remote if I was able to switch inputs right on the fire remote!It’s a great device!Let me tell you why you specifically want this over the not 4K version even if you don’t have a 4K tv as I own both types of fire sticks and no 4K TVs!-volume controls! (This is a big one for me!)-faster. The normal HD one is a little low on processing power and occasionally lags through menus. The 4K one flys through like a breeze.-And obviously the 4K flexibility. If I buy a 4K tv sometime I can use this in it no problem.-when I bought this it was only $10 more than the non 4K one and its worth it for the volume control remote alone.Bottom line:I would recommend this device! The 4K stick is the better option for all users 4K tv or not.Update: March 30th 2021The recent software update has created issues with the device. I’ll often lose sound until I either unplug either of my 2 fire sticks power source or restart them in the settings. And I also often, but less so, lose visuals but maintain audio until I unplug them.Good job ruining your product with a hasty update. Patiently waiting for a fix. If not it’s time to move on. And I suggest anybody looking around the date of my update stays away from this software bricked device.

title: Quit working in less than a year - REALLY AMAZON????!!!!!!
body: 3trd Update 7/23/2020: If you do decide to buy a fire stick make sure to buy the extended warranty so you don't totally waste your money!!! The rep tells me this is a known issue with the fire tv stick 4k and they are working on it. They have been working on it for months, so basically they are selling defective devises and hoping people just give up when they quit. I believe Amazon should be standing behind the products they sell when there are these types of issues, but it seems that is not the case - you are just out of luck if you happen to be one that this is an issue with. I realize it is not a lot of money, but at this stage it is the point that it is happening to multiple devises. Maybe they should stop selling them until they get the problem fixed or offer a longer warranty since it is the update that is forced on you that causes them to quit working2nd Update: Back to a brick - another fire stick bites the dust. I have 3 sticks sitting here that are all doing the same thing - black screen white logo - never boots. I called amazon because 2 of them are just out of warranty date barely a year old. 1 is only 5 months old. They are supposed to be sending a refurbished replacement stick and for some reason they can't manage to get it sent. 2 days late now and says to wait 1-2 days more - wated and it still did not arrive. Had to waste my time and call Amazon again, just to be told they will send another replacement. I received an email that they updated to 2-day free shipping at no additional cost to me - seriously!!!Amazon has gone downhill and the customer service is really lacking now. REALLY REALLY not happy with Amazon - especially since I spend so much money with them every month. I guess that will be changing.If you do decide to buy a fire stick make sure to buy the extended warranty so you don't totally waste your money!!! The rep tells me this is a known issue with the fire tv stick 4k and they are working on it. They have been working on it for months, so basically they are selling defective devises and hoping people just give up when they quit. I believe Amazon should be standing behind the products they sell when there are these types of issues, but it seems that is not the case - you are just out of luck if you happen to be one that this is an issue with. I realize it is not a lot of money, but at this stage it is the point that it is happening to multiple devises. Maybe they should stop selling them until they get the problem fixed or offer a longer warranty since it is the update that is forced on you that causes them to quit working.Update: amazon replaced my fire stick and I am back watching Prime :).Original: I bought 3 Fire TV 4k sticks on November 26. 2018. One has completely quit - stuck on the red Fire Logo and won't go past it. And now 2 of the remotes have stopped working completely. This is completely unacceptable. What poor quality if they won't even last a year. Sad indeed because I do all of my online shopping on Amazon. I have many Alexa devises and so on. Will be thinking hard before I purchase another Alexa or Amazon devise if they are of such poor quality that they don't last for at least a year.

title: Update: DOUBLE Shame on you Amazon
body: UPDATE 8/6/2020: Amazon has called to follow up based on my negative review, and once again is trying to get me to spend my time to try to correct this corrupt device. I told the rep (this is my 5th request!) that I want the device REPLACED. The fire stick has already proven to be corruptable. Why a BILLION dollar industry like Amazon can’t simply send me a replacement device that costs them $2 to produce, I have no idea. I’m shocked that Amazon is being so unreasonable and cares so little for my peace of mind. I DON’T WANT THEIR HIJACKED INSECURE DEVICE HOOKED UP YO MY WIFI. REPLACE IT, AMAZON.Original post 8/3/2020: Device arrived, set up (not my first Fire Stick. Replacing the older, slower model). Worked great for 2 days. On the 3rd day it stopped working and required that we log back in to identify the account. But when we did, it stated our Amazon account doesn’t exist.Called customer service, who informed me the fire stick is now “assigned to a different account.” How in the world could that happen??!? She even said she couldn’t solve the problem unless we knew the “new” account information (how would we know that)?!Then I got put on hold for 45 minutes. Then I was told there is nothing that can be done, since I don’t know the account information that HIJACKED MY FIRE STICK. I now own $50 of useless digital junk. (It worked great for 2 days, remember?!) so somehow, Amazon permitted someone else to hack into my device (how, I have no idea!) and now I can’t use it! So I asked for a replacement device. End call.25 minutes later, the Cust service rep calls me back and says she might be able to solve the problem, but I have to talk to technology. Great. I already wasted over an hour on the phone to be told it couldn’t be fixed, and now I’m being asked to spend more time on the phone to MAYBE fix what MIGHT be wrong?Plus, she can’t identify what happened in the first place. If this device has a weakness, how do I know this won’t happen again 2 days from now? Nope. Send me a new fire stick.It’s now been 3 days since that call, and I don’t have a new device. So I called the same number just now and explained that I still need a replacement fire stick. I was told by the representative that “I only work on kindles” and I needed to hold for tech support.I explained I don’t want tech support, I want a new, replacement device (it is impossible to ask for a replacement Online, because the only choice is to be connected to Cust service for a fire stick issue). So I am stuck in a customer service loop that doesn’t end.And now Amazon has me screwed. I can’t get the answers I need, nor the replacement device I’ve requested. Amazon allowed my perfectly working device to be hijacked by another account, I’m locked out of it, and somehow that means I need to spend more hours being kicked around in their system while I listen to people telling me they don’t know what is wrong.I don’t care what’s wrong. Send me a new device, Amazon. I’ve been a Prime Member since year 1, and this is not the kind of customer service Amazon used to give. Get with the program, Amazon. If you can’t handle the digital world, step aside.

title: Best Remote - Poor GUI - Auto-Preview Trailers In Prime Video A Horror Show ** Addendum *** UPDATE!
body: UPDATE 2021: The party's over. Bought newest version of Apple TV even though I cringed at the price. The last system upgrade of Firestick finally pushed me over the edge. It started out passable then devolved into one solid advertisement ... one never endings blob of icons. There is no continuity and the promises of making everything more efficient proved to be false.Fire Sticks remote is the best I have ever used bar none. That said, I am extremely frustrated by the GUI. It's just too busy. It's like writing on paper with no lines. Poor delineation and too much work to scroll horizontally.Now I don't mind Amazon tooting their own horn on the Home screen like others do. I don't even mind the trailers in front of their originals. But what I do mind is the Auto-Preview trailers screaming at me in Prime Video without giving me the option to turn them off. Tacky, useless, data eaters and inefficient. A real turn-off ..literally. I stopped watching Netflix because of this so I guess Prime Video and the Fire Stick are next on the list.As an adult I am capable of choosing what trailers I want to watch and as a grandmother I don't want my grandkids exposed to content in this way. And these trailers just don't play once per title ... they play over and over and over every time you browse the channel.I'll tell you what Mr. Bezos, I want to come into your home and yell in your face every time you look at me ... "Hey look at me ... I'm over here ... I have something I want to tell you ... something I want to show you ... look at my pretty pictures and listen to my playlist". How many times would you be willing to let me do that? So you and Netflix can for some financially induced reasoning, regardless of repeated complaints/reviews, continue to be obnoxious while Disney + allows users to turn that function off. Funny thing happened when somebody listened to consumers. Disney + is doing pretty well for itself. Instead of forced bombardment of advertising they concluded customers enjoying their GUI would end up being more profitable.People do get tired of being harassed and they will move on regardless of how popular a service is. Nothing is too big to fail. The Titanic was labeled as unsinkable too.As a longtime Amazon customers I hope you recognize the error and correct it.7/10/2020 UpdateI have gone back to using a Roku Ultra. I am sick of the Autoplay the Firestick Apps forces on me. Amazon has no respect for people with Data Caps or empathy for people who want peace and quiet to browse. I no longer have to deal with Netflix starting to play an episode before I get to choose. I no longer have to worry about my husband falling asleep watching Apple TV +. I also don't have a screen full of Apps all bunched together in repetition on my Home screen. I love the remote and its ability to easily FF and Rewind but I seldom need that function. All you would have to do to get me back Amazon is offer me the choice to toggle Autoplay off in the Apps in your service. All you would have to do is clean up your collage of a home screen. Roku is number one for a very good reason .... simplicity.

title: Great - but with one issue  *Updated 5/15/19*
body: bought it to replace an old roku 3 that quit on me. got it for the cyber monday price of $35.i also have a fire stick (last year's version) and this new one is much faster and overall a really good streaming device. except for one issue i'm having. i use my tv both as a computer monitor as well as watching streaming content, and when switching inputs, more often than not i have to unplug and reboot the fire stick in order for it to work.i never had this problem with the roku. i have checked all the settings (it recognizes the tv it's hooked up to) and cannot determine why it does this. maybe just got a lemon? hopefully amazon customer service will have a work around or else i'll just send it in for a replacementUpdated review on 5/15/19So the issue listed above persisted and eventually got to the point I had to reboot this thing every time I wanted to watch. In addition another one problem popped up. When starting the Fire Stick I would get a message "Home is not available, check your network settings". Well, checking my network settings would always show that the connection is excellent. I would have to do this little cycle 3 or 4 times before i could get to the Home page and start watching. I finally decided that enough was enough and contacted Amazon for a replacement. That's where the fun begins. I have never had such a bizarre customer service experience with any company in my life.A long chat session, and two phone calls lead to some ridiculous troubleshooting suggestions, including:Use my Fire stick only on a device that has an HDMI port (lol)Disable all security on my network (seriously)Use only the g or n bands on my router instead of the ac band that every other device in my home uses. (why pay for gigabit if you don't need it to use a defective Fire Stick)And the old standby, contact my ISP. (Never mind that I'm chatting and exchanging emails just fine with my service provider)I was then informed that all of my information and the useless steps I went through would be submitted to the 'developers' and after analyzing it, they would get back to me in a couple of days to see where I go from there.I realize that some of these are troubleshooting steps, but why? Can't Amazon take my word that this device is defective, UNDER WARRANTY, and i'm not trying to beat them out of a $35 piece of electronics?Today i receive an email stating that due to 'intermittent issue' - did not specify if the issue was theirs or mine - a replacement could not be made. Instead a promo credit would be issued to my account in order to purchase another Fire Stick. Well the credit does not appear - who knew!Also I am to ship the defective unit back in the original packaging (I purchased it six months ago but miraculously still have the box), insured, with tracking, at MY EXPENSE. Again this is for a defective item that is still under warranty.I have never written such a long review but my hope is someone from Amazon will read his that can make the simple decision to please issue me a replacement or properly get the credit I was promised into my account.

title: Do Not Buy The Fire Stick
body: Amazon stopped supporting Sony, so we needed something else to keep watching.  We bought 2 of these and converted our home theater set up from RGB to HDMI using a new Yamaha receiver to switch between devices. (We planned to add the second unit to an older TV)  There is not enough room between inputs to plug the Fire Stick in directly, so we use the extension cable that comes with the unit.  I have an extensive electronics and computer background, so I know that everything is connected correctly.  We have a solid 60 mbs internet speed through a wireless gigabit router.  Using other wireless devices does affect the Fire Stick one way or another.The Amazon Fire Sticks are a huge disappointment.  First and foremost, the picture keeps cutting in and out identically on both units, flickering between the picture and a black screen.  Rebooting sometimes solves the problem for a couple of hours, but it is not unusual have to reboot 2 to 5 times during a movie.  We have Cable and Blueray running through the same receiver and the problem is only with the Amazon Fire Sticks.  A web search suggested a hard reboot on the stick to clear it's cache, but that did nothing.  We've switched the Fire Stick to all of the different HDMI inputs with no improvement.  There is also a digital noise like static that shows up from time to time only in the Fire Stick video.  Again, we no issues with any of our other devices.In addition, the screen is jammed full of different apps that you most likely don't need and there is no apparent way to delete them.  When you open Amazon video app, is absolutely flooded with lists of movies they want to sell you, and you have to scroll way down to finally find your watch list, which only shows you a few movies at a time instead of an entire page of movies in your watch list like NetFlix does.  And, often times when you add a movie to the watch list, the next time you turn it on the same movie is there 3-4 times with different icons is in your list and you have to delete each one separately after you have watched the movie.  When it works, the picture quality is good, and it responds well to voice commands.  You need to press and hold the Alexa button for this, but there is no way to tell if Alexa is listening all the time, even when the button is not pushed.  It would be nice to have some input and reassurance from Amazon on this.  We do not use voice command devices because of the incredible intrusiveness inherent in these devices.As I mentioned earlier, all of our devices run through a new Yamaha receiver.  All of our devices work flawlessly on all inputs, except for the Fire SticksSo, bottom line? Do not buy this.  With 2 units doing the same thing, and many, many web posts about the issue, it is obviously a problem that Amazon is, or at least should be, aware of and should have fixed long ago.  You would well advised to skip the Fire Stick and go with another device.  And, if you read these reviews Amazon, we would love to hear what you have to say about these units.

title: Not compatible with Amazon's own services
body: First, full disclosure: this is a punitive review. I'm sure this device works fine for some of its intended purposes, but I won't be keeping it because it doesn't do what I want it to.How can someone write a punitive review based on their disappointment in what the product does not, and does not claim, to do? I'm glad you asked.I bought this for one reason only: I wanted to cast music from the amazon prime music app on my iphone, etc to my home stereo. I have been a huge fan of Prime for a long time, and one thing I love about it is the free music streaming service. Being able to get much of the music I want, without paying for an extra subscription through Spotify, Apple, Amazon, etc is one of the main reasons I buy the Prime membership every year.Although I own a chromecast, to which I can cast a variety of things (including spotify, my own local files, Google music, etc), I can't cast Amazon music. Fair enough, I thought: Amazon wants to sell their products, and so they don't want people streaming their service to someone else's device. I'm happy to buy Amazon's dongle, I thought, if it means I can cast from Amazon prime music and still save money on a subscription.I didn't bother to check whether one could, in fact, cast from the Amazon music app to Amazon's newest, super-powerful 4k fire stick. I saw that Amazon music app could cast from the iphone; and that they sold a fancy streaming device that looks like a chromecast. Why would you bother to check on this? Technologically, all that is required for this to work is for Amazon to set up the app so that it points the FireTV stick 4k towards the appropriate streaming location of prime music on the web. It's not a technical feat at all: it's exactly what the device is designed for, and apparently can do very well with 4k tv services, for example. So I didn't check compatibility.I was so wrong!!! Amazon does not, in fact, support casting to any FireTV device, only to their Alexa-enabled devices (but NOT Alexa-enabled devices that are also FireTV devices. Go figure!). This, despite the fact that the Amazon Music App on Android can apparently now cast to chromecast (but still no iOS casting. bummer). This feature is so standard on music streaming apps and tv dongles that Amazon even supports it for their competitors' streaming devices, but not their own.In some ways, this experience really fits with my overall impression of Amazon. A hodgepodge of semi-unrelated services, designed around keeping customers within their ecosystem, without necessarily thinking about overall user experience. Very different from, for example, Apple.It turns out it wouldn't have mattered if I had checked more carefully for compatibility. The page for the FireTV Stick mentions that the device runs Amazon music, but (of course) doesn't mention that it can't receive casts from computers, phones, tablets, etc. I literally had to buy the stupid stick to figure out this ridiculous limitation. Back it goes to Amazon.

title: Still a buggy proprietary piece of trash
body: I got this because there was a discount for turning in my crappy 1st gen firestick that had loads of problems with audio sync, it seemed like a no brainer to upgrade the old tech for the new and purchase a wired adapter.Alexa is annoying and can't do what I ask like continuing to play a show in other apps, netflix/hulu, the firestick keeps exiting apps like netflix/hulu/disney+/etc after I pause for several minutes. It stays in the app longer if you disable screensaver but it still exits the app, I don't know how long 5-10? minutes back to the amazon home screen, the remote doesn't respond to the arrow keys when it is in screen saver you have to press back/home and then you are lagging as the firestick jumps around to places you didn't want to go.It is better than the old 1st gen firestick I had but only slightly, I screwed myself by buying a firestick in the first place, a roku box is better but more expensive, and I now know it is worth the price compared the artificially low price of the fire stick products. It also never seems to turn itself off, even when I press the power button, it will turn my tv off and leave a 'held' state screen on the firestick, black but not off, on my roku it will turn off after a time so if I turn the tv on and the roku turned off there is no signal from the device/hdmi, firestick always has a signal but blank as it probably is waiting or actively listening to you.The quality of streaming/video is excellent but as I mentioned I got the wired ethernet adapted because I found better video results with a wired connection despite having excellent wifi with my old 1st gen firestick that was within 10 feet of the router.Avoid this piece of garbage, it is slower than a roku, wastes your time, forces 'information/feature' notices on you, essentially advertising for prime or other partners they sell content through, I only encountered this situation once but I wanted to use my device to watch shows/movies and I was confronted with an 'ad' I couldn't move past without acknowledging, back, home, etc did nothing. They often do nothing when amazon is pushing some content they want you to see, their shows, addons they want you to buy and so on.The good thing about my old firestick is it was so problematic I didn't use it much and this one works technically better but has shown me how garbage the platform and device are compared to something like roku that just does what you want without bothering or pushing their services on you. The worst roku does is 'advertise' stuff as you select the "app"/service you want to use, once you choose it is gone and only on the selection menu where it quickly vanished once you enter/select the app unlike amazon they don't exit you from the app after a few minutes to shove ads in your face.

title: Read this before you waste your money.
body: I bought 3 of these around black friday time so thankfully paid only $25 for them. I have been so frustrated after living with them for 4 months that I just bought a Roku 3810R and the difference is night and day. I trusted Amazon because I do purchase a lot of products from them but these 4K firesticks suck. I have 30 to 35 meg download internet and 15 to 20 meg upload. I do like the remotes and the long range that they have and the simple design of the remote but that's where it ends. I have 2 dual band Ubiquiti 2.4 and 5 GHZ access points setup in different locations of my house on different channels and have excellent wifi as I have no problems with wireless device connectivity. Here are the problems I experienced before the Roku.1. All are sluggish with latency on just navigating with the remote to the stick itself.2. I loaded up about 25 extra apps on one and it literally gave me a message that I needed to delete some apps because I was running out of memory resources. What???3. If you are on certain news apps and it disconnects or you stop streaming and try to go back to the same app you were watching it will never connect to that channel. You have to exit back to the home screen and literally load another app then exit that app, then you will be able to load that original channel again....very frustrating. All while you deal with the latency with the remote.4. I installed an inline hdmi audio extractor so I was able to get my subwoofer audio input to work with my hdmi distribution to 8 TV's in my house. It worked fine but I ended up with a low ground loop (hum) in the subwoofer. (annoying)5. Randomly buffers and I understand that can happen with internet connections on occasion.However !!!!! I bought the 3810R Roku 3 days ago and every problem described above has been completely eliminated !!No latency with the remote, I loaded 42 apps and no memory resource warnings, I can leave a channel and reload the same one instantly, the audio hum (ground loop) was totally eliminated, and has not buffered one time at all in 3 days which has never been the case with the firestick.So this has been my experience. I have an old non 4K firestick in my RV and it has always performed on a Verizon cell modem very well but this 4K one I have my reservations with. I'll never purchase another firestick. Expected more out of amazon. The Roku 3810R is by far superior in performance. I don't like the Roku remote layout as well but I can easily live with it since the performance is so much better in every way.I have been in electronics for a living all my life with audio and video so I can write this review in confidence. Trying to save everyone the grief I went through.

title: Incredibly disappointed with Alexa integration across the product line
body: Incredibly disappointed. This was one of EIGHT Fire TV and/or Echo devices that I purchased on Black Friday, hoping to switch from Roku TV and have a seamless smarthome experience. Oh boy, was I wrong.Whether I rename the the Fire TV devices or use their original names, the echo devices CANNOT control or recognize the tv devices, even after properly pairing them under the TV & Video setting in the Alexa app. I've tried to unpair, repair, restart, unregister, reregister, you name it. I've wasted days. Generally, Alexa responds with 'I cannot find a device named ...' I consider myself very technically savy and I'm positive this is not something that I'm doing wrong. I should've read more into this before buying. TONS of people all over the internet are having this problem. It's infuriating. Plain and simple, until problems like this are fixed, Amazon is blaintantly false advertising the way that their devices work.Other cons to note:-Multiple video apps stutter and sometimes even randomly pause without ever resuming unless you physically resume them.-Alexa drastically lowers the volume of the TV when you are voicing a command. This in-and-of-itself is not a huge problem. However, it becomes a problem often when after the command is complete, Alexa does not resume the original volume of the content. When this has happened, I've had to ask Alexa to turn the volume back up to the specific number it was at.-I have two third generation echos paired as a stereo system for the TV. Often, even though I have my fire TV set as the preferred device, only the echos hear my voice, and once again, because they cannot control the TV, they say that they cannot recognize the device and then I just have to pick up the controller anyway.-The Fire TV will often think that it hears an 'Alexa' when absolutely no where near that word has been stated. Because the volume lowers drastically when Alexa is listening, it creates an obnoxious volume dip in content to listen for nothing when this happens.-You cannot set a sleep timerHonestly I could go on and on about the cons and about just how unhappy I am about these products. There are even more problems than what I listed here, but I'm not wasting too much breath, as after doing days and days of research on these topics, I've seen that Amazon has absolutely no answers to these problems, even after thousands of people have reported the same problems over and over. Unless there is some revalatory software updated coming, I'll be returning hundreds of dollars of equipment.

title: Buyer beware.  Do your research before buying this product!
body: I wasted an entire evening trying to set up this "Fire TV Stick 4k" and I never did get it working! The stick connected successfully to my wi-fi (500 Mbps) but I could not get past the "Unable to update" screen. I rebooted over 20 times, I reset the factory settings, I used the extra dongle, I plugged the stick into a wall outlet, I plugged the stick into my 1 amp USB port, and unfortunately I ended up having to call Support. After telling the Support technician the DSN number on the box, she told me that the stick was defective and that I needed to talk to someone else to return the item. This second person asked me the same exact questions that I had already answered with the first person. After multiple times on hold, a third person came onto the line and started asking me the exact same questions! I told him that my answers to the two previous Support people were still valid and nothing changed since I last answered them. He put me on hold a few times and then my phone battery finally died (on my landline)! I was irate that I wasted so much time and got so little in return. All I wanted was a streaming stick that worked out-of-the-box but apparently I have to get a Roku, instead.The Fire TV Stick 4K was brand new, still in the original packaging when I got it. What kind of quality control exists, if any, at Amazon??? Amazon would rather apologize than do something right........oh, wait......I didn't even get an apology from anyone!Before I called Support, I took Amazon's suggestion and searched their website for help...needless to say, that didn't do a thing as it was utterly useless! I couldn't find anything on their site about the error message that I was receiving. HOWEVER, when I did a simple search on the internet, I got hundreds of results from people with the same problem. Why can't Amazon own up to it and admit that there is a problem like this with their product??? Apologize and then FIX it!Great, now I've lost ANOTHER hour trying to write a legible review after my blood pressure finally dropped back to normal!From reading other articles on the web, it sounds like Roku is definitely the way to go if you want a reliable streaming option. SIDE NOTE: The only reason that I want a streaming device is that Samsung is discontinuing support for Netflix on my "smart" Samsung television......oh, that, and I can't even download the Disney+ app from the Samsung store.Now I'm off to waste more of my time packaging up the Fire Stick and returning it.....
