title: Alexa features are not so good
body: I bought this for the Alexa integration but learned that it's incapable of the most basic function--turning the tv on and off. Alexa customer service informed me that I would need to buy the much more expensive fire cube for that.I bought a Chromecast/Google home mini combo for the bedroom and love the voice commands. So I thought that feature would be a good upgrade for the living room but Chromecast's lack of screen interface (and the fact that I already have an echo in the living room) made me go for the fire stick 4k.Mind you, I have a smart TV with minimal Alexa compatibility, but wanted the ability to say "Alexa play... show" Or "look for..." and all that. My Vizio smartcast basically allows me to turn on/off and adjust volume with Alexa and not much else.I had issues setting it up. Alexa was simply not working with the tv well at all at first, giving me weird and inconsistent responses, and unable to do much. Eventually I realized the issue was that she was getting confused between the two devices so I disabled the Vizio Smartcast skill and things started working much smoother. Problem is, the most basic function of the tv is apparently not a function of this product at all. While you can turn the TV on and off with the remote, you can't have Alexa do it. This is very disappointing for me as I've become accustomed to using routines involving the TV's power.There are still some other odd glitches. For example sometimes when I ask Alexa to go back, forward, or to the home screen while I'm playing something on Netflix, she responds "Netflix probably very can't do that on" or some weird gibberish.Basically you can't get too far without using the remote, so if you already have a smart TV or another streaming device, this isn't going to improve much. The Chromecast has much better voice control.Edit: after a few days, I've lowered my rating from 2 to 1. I bought this for the advertised voice capabilities but they're nothing like advertised. For example, when I search for my toddler's favorite show, Yo Gabba Gabba, Alexa will respond "getting Yo Gabba Gabba from Fire TV," and the TV will bring me to a screen to buy an episode of Everyone Loves Raymond.Additionally, apps randomly close and go to a screen advertising either CBS All Access or Tubi. This happened 4 times in the last 5 minutes of a football game I was watching

title: If you are legally blind, don’t buy it.
body: Let me start off by saying that I am visually impaired. I do rely on the voice over features of many devices to help me in navigating those devices. I am not a huge fan of smart TV interfaces especially their built-in voice over features. They are laggy and somewhat clunky.In my living room, I have a 4K TV with a 4K Apple TV hooked up to it. I bought another TV for the bedroom. Also 4K. Same brand as the living room TV. However, Apple TV 4K are expensive, so I figured I will go with a cheaper or alternative. The Amazon fire 4K fire stick was only $49.99. The picture quality look good. I’ve also looked at some YouTube videos with tutorials and the build in voice over or as Amazon calls it voice view features.When I bought the Amazon fire 4K stick, I of course understood that there is going to be a disparity as well in the perhaps operational quality of the product. Apple 4K is $179.99 new. Now, with Apple, the accessibility and the voiceover that is built into their devices to assist individuals with visual disabilities is incredible. It is smooth. It is seamless. It is a joy to operate.Well, With the Amazon fire 4K TV stick, it is the complete opposite.It is very clunky and it has a lot of lag. It’s putters all the time. It freezes all the time and randomly turns off or stops the voice view operation of the device because it encounters a technical issue. If I restart it, it’ll start working again. But of course, that’s a pain.I guess Amazon did not think about the fact that the device may be used by individuals with visual disabilities. This is an android-based operating system. I’m not sure why they couldn’t make the voice over or the voice view feature either equal to or better than even what apple has.Overall the build quality and the presentation and the feel of the remote and the device are good. If the voice over or the voice view feature is turned off, the device is great. It’s a great cheap alternative to an Apple TV. And for many people, it will work seamlessly because they are not using voiceover or voice view.No, of course, because I am reliant on voiceover or voice view, for me, the operation of the device is not that smooth. I am very disappointed with the product. I’ve only had it for two days. I’m going to give it time. I will try to get used to it, but I might end up returning it and just spending $179.99 on an Apple TV 4K.

title: Not the best option!
body: Have been using this for months now, and have become very frustrated with it. Yes, you can download plenty of apps to watch free stuff, but they don't work well. The stick will drop the program you're watching over and over again. And once dropped, you have to start from the home page and go all the way back to the app and show you were watching, and sometimes, it doesn't even start where you left off. This same thing happens if you pause a show you are watching. You would expect to be able to just push play and start from where you paused it, but no, you get to go back home and start all over. In addition, several apps don't work with the remote. For example, hit pause on the RedBull App, and you'll find yourself watching a different show. Fast forward and reverse have the same results. Last, but not least, many of the shows play at a low volume, so you have to really crank the speaker volume... until the commercials run; then you're scrambling for the remote to mute so that you don't piss off the neighbors will the loud yelling. In the end, I do not recommend this streaming option, as Roku and others do a much better job. Hope this helps save someone the headache this has given us!Had to add to this review, since I can no longer stand the stupid remote. There is no backlight, and the buttons are too small, the same size, and right next to each other. You will find yourself constantly hitting the wrong button, like hitting Home instead of Pause (they are stacked together), and then you'll find yourself all the way back at the home page and you'll have to navigate back to whatever you were watching. Who thought this was a good idea. They should be fired. With all that space on the remote, you would think they could make bigger buttons of different shapes, and leave plenty of space between them. What a joke!!!UPDATE Jan2021Well after continued use, I have to drop another star. This thing is aweful. Ever want to pause a show to check out what is on the screen? Well, good luck with the Fire Stick. When you pause, it clouds out the upper and lower parts of the screen, and puts up the status a third of the way up. So unless what you want to see just happens to fall in the middle of the screen, you're out of luck. If someone knows of a better option, please let me know as I am ready to trash this garbage.

title: NEVER BEEN SO FRUSTRATED!
body: I hate, hate, hate using this thing. I didn't hate it this much when I first started using it. In fact, to tell the truth, I was relieved when I first started using it after the Roku remote went haywire, to the point where the battery needed to be removed after every use. And this remote.. I grew to hate it more and more and more.First, the shape, terrible. I can't tell which side is facing the TV and which side is facing me when I'm not looking at it. A remote, by definition, should be used without looking. Something simple, yet so frustrating. Every single button basically blend in with the next and it doesn't matter if the remote is facing south or north, both ends feel the same.Second, after daily use for about 4 months, the remote just dropped dead. Changed the batteries out 3 times, frustrated so much I almost threw the remote into the center of the earth, pouring gasoline and setting it on fire, back to where it came from, which is hell. Matching. Fire TV stick, it is indeed from the place of flames.Third, the user interface, idiotic. Terrible.Fourth, apps within the stick, say YouTube, had tons of flaws after less than 2 months. The audio and the video started going out of sync and rarely, if ever, got back on sync. Which was something an old 1960s TV never had a problem with.Fifth, there's NO Volume control on this thing. What the heck is this?! Is this even a remote?! Even my wireless earphones have volume buttons and it's freaking TINY!I HATE HATE this thing. How can they get the basics so wrong?! I wouldn't have used this for 4 months if I had any other choice. But now, I'm just going to buy a bunch of DVDs and stop using these streaming services all together. I cannot believe how hard and complicated watching TV has become.Thank you, Amazon, for making my life so much more difficult. If I go bald, it is your fault. Your terrible remote is going to make my hair fall out with its awfulness.Oh, and thanks for the ads, plastered on the home page, even though I paid for this device.AND thanks for making your Fire TV remote mobile app even worse. And by worse, I mean, completely unusable, after downloading it on my phone. I don't know how they managed to screw this up SO badly.

title: Stay Away From This; All it does is buffer
body: All right most people will say issues with WifI. Nope, and that's verified by Amazon Tech Support multiple times. Got this device hooked up and ready to go. Spent the time to get all the user names and passwords in. Wasn't expecting any issues. I have a 5 year old device that has never had an issue and still works great so was expecting this latest generation to be so much better. Specifically bought so I could use Apple TV, I have all the other apps on my TV. Fired up Apple TV. Takes about 30 seconds to start a show. OK, can live with that. Then 30 seconds into the show starts buffering. After the third pause screen comes up saying this show isn't available. Now I'm thinking there's an issue with Apple TV so I go to Prime and start to run a show from Amazon. Yep, starts buffering, so I go to Netflix, same buffering issue. I know my internet service provider doesn't throttle Amazon because my other Fire works fine. Thing is, it's so old it I can't get the Apple App from Amazon. Well finally restart and now can actually get about 5 mins into a show and then it buffers again. Next day call Amazon Tech support. Spend an hour with them rebooting my router, rebooting the firestick, uninstalling and reinstalling apps etc. Tech is monitoring and checking my wifi signal and it is strong with no issues; plus tech uses a screen share so they can directly see what is going on and they can see my other fire. After about an hour, the tech suggest resetting the device to factory default. This is going to take at least 45 minutes so device can set it's self to factory, then start fresh and install all updates. Then I need to re-register with Amazon, passwords etc. Tech and I end our call. I then reset to factory default, go through all the setup again. Finally the device is up and running and now starting to buffer even faster than before. Back to Tech support, spend another 45 minutes with them and finally they ask if they can replace the device. So now waiting for a new device that will take 3 days for delivery and have to go through the entire setup process again. Save yourself the grief and buy an older generation. The bugs are not worked out on this thing. In the meantime, have ordered an older generation device and will see how it works. All the while, my 5 year old box is still working perfectly.

title: Poor app selection, no customer support
body: the only thing I really like about this is the price. but if it doesn't offer what you want, price is irrelevant. let's walk through the shortcomings of this Fire 4K stick and, pretty much, all Fire devices:1. DO NOT PURCHASE ANY SUBSCRIPTIONS USING AMAZON PAY - AMAZON DOES NOT HAVE YOUR BACK SHOULD YOU RUN INTO ANY BILLING ISSUES WITH A SERVICE PROVIDER. My point: on 10/19 I cancelled a subscription to sling tv that I set up using Amazon Pay. On 10/26 I was billed again for sling tv; ONE WEEK AFTER CANCELLING IT. I contact Amazon customer service (a nightmare in itself unless you have 3 hours to kill speaking to someone who has no clue what you're saying because they're in Pakistan!) and finally went to the top. I sent an email to Jeff Bezos and received a response from Jason Kayne of Amazon.com’ s Executive Customer Relations. His response:"We’re able to see that our Customer Service team contacted Sling but to date, there hasn’t been a response yet. Unfortunately, we don’t have the ability to initiate the refund on our end, in this case you’ll need to contact Sling directly to have this refund processed."THIS IS WHAT HAPPENS IF YOU USE AMAZON PAY AND RUN INTO A PROBLEM. I make a purchase using Amazon Pay and they don’t have the ability to initiate a refund? What a cop out. This worthless excuse for customer service offered me no help and no recourse. I have still yet to receive any kind of retribution for being billed a week after I clearly cancelled. Remember when Amazon has such great customer service? It is non-existent today.2. I've been using Roku for a number of years. When I received my fire stick and went to check out the app’s they offered, I was extremely disappointed. I was surprised by the lack of apps they offer. I’m not talking about obscure, never heard of apps; I’m talking mainstream apps suck as Comcast’s streaming, Fandango, etc.3. lastly, just the layout of the platform left much to be desired. Taking into consideration that I was used to the Roku platform for so long I was still uneasy with Firestick’s. It was awkward, cumbersome and not very responsive.All in all, this was a big disappointment. If you are considering purchasing a new streaming device, buy the Roku product. You’ll be so much more happier

title: Not worth a Dime since they introduced...
body: I did not purchase this product for DISNEY+, however, since DISNEY+ was launched this 4k Fire Stick (as well as my older 2nd Gen Amazon Fire Stick) has done nothing but kick me out of my streaming apps (although now the issue isn't just limited to streaming apps but ALL apps and even Settings) in order to play commercials for DISNEY+; when I contacted Amazon's Customer Service Team, I was told that they have never heard of such a thing, they did promptly open a work ticket and told me that the normal turn around time for technical support is greater than 48 Hours, so I'll be stuck waiting for at least another day (although, since I suspect that this has something to do with advertisement revenue between Disney+ and Amazon, I am not hopeful that this issue will ever be resolved.)I will post an update to this assuming that Technical Support from Amazon ever responds to my issue [Amazon if you are reading this review, it is Ticket # "V155964475".]Update (11/23/2019) After not getting a response back from Amazon; I chatted with them again, it appears that they did not log my issue, and therefore could not contact me back. I "chatted" with 4 individuals before being told that they could not assist me further a phone call, which we did 45 minutes into that, all that we accomplished was a factory reset, and updates... The issue persists, and now I am told that normal turn around for Technical Support issues is 3-5 business days, so since today is Saturday, I should not expect a call back until Monday December 2nd at the earliest. This is disappointing to say the very least.Update (12/12/2019) Contacted Amazon.com yesterday, since Amazon yet again failed to contact me regarding the issue I reported over a month ago. I was told that Mary Ann L., from Amazon's technical customer service would be contacting me, however, I have yet again waited over 24 hours and Amazon has failed to call.Update (12/26/2019) Reached out to Amazon yet again, and was told that they had no record of my work tickets, and that they could open a new work ticket if I would like to do that. I have a feeling that this is just their normal work policy and that they don't actually do any product support whatsoever! Amazon's technical team is a complete waste of space and pay.

title: Amazon Support is the Worst ***See UPDATE***
body: Someone else wrote about some of these same issues. Firestick 4K kept restarting by it's self and kept loosing server connection both hardwired and via wifi not to mention it was signing me out of my account so I had to sign in every time it restarted. Plain and simple is it just crapped out but here is the fun part; I spent 2 hours on chat with support in a good faith effort to try and fix the issue. When everything failed and it was clear it was unrepairable here is where I was left; I bought the Firestick 4K 1 year and 29 days ago so 29 days out of warranty. The rep was good (so I thought) at first he offered me 15% off a new one but when asked if he could do something more because it was only 29 days out of warranty. He told me he escalated the issue and requested I be sent a new Firestick 4k and I would get an email in 2 to 3 days. Great! right? Hold on....After 4 days and no word I contacted chat again and was told it was being addressed it was just taking longer then usual and I would get an email in a day or 2 and even sent me an email confirming the 1 to 2 days. Well here it is a week later, I have heard nothing from them and here is a kicker for you...I pleaded for them to do whatever they could to get it resolved sooner then later because I am disabled, live alone and hardly leave the house so tv is a big part of my outside contact. Well that's my story so I will just say "good luck if you have to get help from Amazon support" it is clear they are just saying what they have to in hopes you go away.***Update***2 weeks after I first contacted support with an issue with my Firestick 4K I received a responds. Not from the person that said he escalated my issue and would email me or the one that sent me an email saying I would hear something in 1 to 2 days; this was from someone new but not because of my contact with support but because I wrote this review. They apologized for the previous reps that pretty much blow me off and took immediate action to rectify the situation. While I am very pleased that the issue is now resolved and I am once again able to enjoy watching TV the fact it took a bad review to get someone's attention is a bit disheartening but it is good to know at least one person at Amazon support is willing to step up and help a customer.

title: Don't waste your money
body: I bought this device to stream Amazon Prime Video through a new TCL 6-series Roku TV and I could never get it to connect to the TV through the TV's HDMI connector. When I first connected the stick to one of the HDMI ports and powered on the stick, I could get to the first Fire TV screen but then it disappeared. Despite recycling power on the stick three times, the TV kept saying there was no signal (from the stick). Thinking that maybe the stick wouldn't provide the required signal to the TV until the stick was set up properly, I took it to an Insignia TV I have and connected it to that TV's HDMI connector. This time I could see the stick and connect it to my WiFi network and download updates. However, when I went to sign into my Prime account, the stick just "disappeared" from the TV's HDMI connector three times. The fourth time I recycled power on the stick the TV recognized it but made me sign into my WiFi network again. This time I was able to sign into my Prime account and everything seemed OK. So I took the stick back to my TCL TV and the TV appeared to see the newly-configured Fire TV stick but after one minute it said that the remote needed to be updated and to not touch anything for a couple of minutes. So while the remote was updating, my entire TV screen turned orange. After waiting for the update to finish for about four minutes, I changed the TV back to the HDMI input that was connected to my cable box to watch TV while the update finished. However, after waiting 20 minutes and then going back to the stick's HDMI input, I could no longer see the stick. I recycled power on the stick but it still wasn't being detected on the HDMI input. Finally, I just gave up and I'll be sending the stick back tomorrow.Besides the stick not working, the other really bad thing about this Fire TV Stick is the remote. I've never seen a TV remote that had a back cover that was so difficult to remove! I struggled with it for over fifteen minutes until I finally managed to get it off. It's so difficult to remote that people have posted videos on YouTube showing how to get the darned thing off!All in all, I'm really disappointed. Amazon doesn't seem to know how to make hardware, at least not hardware that will connect to some TCL Roku TVs.

title: Very Annoying problems
body: Updated review after three years:I finally gave up on these and switched to a Roku Ultra and a Roku streaming stick 4k. I just got tired of the device stability issues and the annoying Amazon ads all over the home screen. I wish I could give them zero stars but one is as low as I can go. They're not even big enough to use as doorstops, useless waste of money. I will miss the bluetooth keyboard support, it's the only reason I kept using one of the two I purchased. That's not enough to keep dealing with all of the issues.Updated review after one year:I wish I had good things to say about them. They are used every day for hours and that might be the issue.Here are the issues so far:1) The home screen is HEAVY with Amazon content and you don't get a choice in the layout2) I have to reset one of the nearly every day. Things just stop working, apps won't start when selected, etc.3) Ads, ads, ads, and more ads. New movie coming out? Ad on the home screen, I just want to see my apps!4) The latest screen saver cuts in while you're watching a video through several apps.5) No Google Play Store so you're limited to what's available on Amazon.6) The remote is tiny, easy to lose and it doesn't have a backlight option. I purchased a third party keyboard that functions as the remote.7) A wired ethernet adapter isn't included and must be purchased separately.8) The reset shortcut on the remote doesn't work most of the time, I have to go to settings to do the reset9) You used to be able to hit the play button to resume content. Now the content may have switched to something Amazon is pushing on the home screen!!! Your app is quit and it goes back to the screen saver, why?I can't recommend this to anyone unless they had really light usage. Unfortunately I have now invested in other Amazon products to go along with these 4k Sticks. I probably should have purchased the 4K TV cube but back when I made my purchase the Cube's remote did not have the same features as the Fire TV Stick 4k. (It does now.)I have switched one of them out for an OLD Roku Express. The menu is clean, apps start much faster. no ads. I just wish Roku had bluetooth so I could ditch both of my 4k sticks.

title: The internet connection on this thing is terrible! Do not buy!
body: Updated review: Now that I have been using this thing for a while, I am updating my review. The internet connection on this thing is terrible! I bought the Ethernet adapter and it did not help. I primarily use it to stream Amazon prime Video. Whether I use the Wi-Fi or Ethernet, the shows often stall when I begin to watch them, then go to a very low resolution so it looks all blocky and blurry. I tested my internet connection, and it is plenty fast enough, so the problem is with the Stick. The TV is only 1080p, not 4k, so that is not an issue. I even tried changing settings to reduce the bandwidth needed (changed audio to basic stereo, etc.) and it did not help. My 10+ year old Blu-ray player streams amazon prime better than this stick. This stick cannot even satisfactorily perform its primary purpose: to stream Amazon Prime Video. It's pathetic. Do not buy.This Fire TV stick works OK, but has some annoying design flaws that could have been easily avoided. 1) The volume control is poorly implemented. Even though the Fire TV sticks all (theoretically) can use the HDMI Consumer Electronics Control (CEC) to control the device they are plugged into, this stick does not control the volume in that way. Instead this has an infrared emitter in the remote that controls the TV directly. That's fine if the stick is plugged into a TV, but most monitors can't be controlled by remote, so it will not control the volume on a monitor. By contrast, my tablet will easily control the volume on my monitor through the HDMI port, so I can watch Prime TV on my monitor from my tablet, but not from this stick. 2) The stick is just a tiny bit wider than the standard spacing between HDMI ports, so if you want to plug this in next to another occupied HDMI port, it won't fit - you'll have to use the little HDMI extension cord included in the box. If they had simply chosen to make the stick just a tiny fraction of an inch less wide, it would fit. Another poor design choice. -- So to wrap it up -- this stick does sort of work, but not if you want to use it to control the volume on a monitor or plug it straight into a TV. This should have been a simple, inexpensive way to turn a monitor into a TV, but because of design flaws it doesn't really allow that.

title: Another Amazon streamer that misses the mark
body: Three in a row now that have had various problems (pendant fire tv, fire cube, fire stick 4k). Not sure why I keep trying new ones. Still hoping Amazon will come through.The good about this is adding the dolby vision throughout including in menus is a very big picture improvement. Even non-4k apps like playstation vue which normally don't look all that great get a huge visual improvement with this stick. In addition it's finally nice to have a remote that controls the tv and volume. They also did a good job of improving the speed which was a huge blunder on the fire cube. For those reasons we are keeping this one instead of sending back.The bad is that once again Amazon has fallen short of what the product is supposed to to. This time it's in the sound. It is supposed to have dolby atmos, and it does but only works with prime from what I can see so far. And the only prime show I know about that has atmos at this time is jack ryan. The atmos does not work with netflix and you can't download vudu unless you side load it but the side loaded version is not 4k so it's useless anyway. Hoping they will fix the netflix problem with a firmware update soon. And would be really great if they'd play nice with vudu and get a proper app for that.The only other streamer I know of that does everything I want (dolby vision and atmos plus decent remote) is the apple tv but while I haven't tried it my understanding is it has some annoyances like not being able to rent or buy through apps like vudu for example. You either have to buy things separately through a website or just use itunes. In addition it's $100 more expensive. However, with what looks like another Amazon streamer that misses the mark as soon as a streamer comes out that does everything I want I'm done with Amazon ones.Update: Since first review we've had multiple sound issues where the sound will drop out for a second. Also, apparently it was trying to do an update however the update would never finish and it just stayed on the white fire tv splash screen and would never come back. I tried multiple times for about 30 minutes. I give up. We're done. It's being returned. Rating adjusted to one star.

title: hate it, but amazon stopping support of their video app on my tv, so forced to get an alternative
body: I only bought this because amazon sent me an email saying they were soon discontinuing support of their video app on my Panasonic smart tv, and it's already been doing strange things, like saying I don't have an internet connection when I do or that I'm not a prime member when I am. I don't care much for American tv, or tv in general, and only watch youtube and free prime member videos on the tv and use a vpn to watch the iplayers in my own country on my tablet or watch my own dvds. I'm planning to return there as soon as I can anyway, and I got enough of a credit that this cost me under $17, so I'm making do with this until then. I don't know a lot about what apps are available and care even less.I've only had this for about a week, and these are some of the things that I hate about it. The home screen is cluttered with all sorts of things I don't want,including things I've already watched or decided against, and I haven't found a way to delete them like I can on my tv app, which has always been uncluttered by unnecessary things. There's no search on it that you can type into like there is on my tv app. Talking to it often gets the wrong thing or it doesn't understand what I want at all.Even going into the prime video app where I know what I'm looking for exists, telling it what I want more often than not takes me to some other obscure app that I've never heard of that I have to instal and almost everything has to be paid for, or if not, there's advertising. There's an episode list in my tv app that is much easier to read, find, and navigate than big photos with small hard to read words at the top like on this fire stick. That's one of the reasons why I cancelled netflix. One of the biggest things is that my tv has a no signal shutoff that works with my tv apps and with my dvd player, but not with the fire stick, so it would stay on forever if I didn't switch it off myself. That's how I get to sleep when I do, to fall asleep watching something. The only improvement to my smart tv is that I could watch something on my web browser that wasn't supported on my tv. If this wasn't a cheap temporary thing for me, I would have probably looked into alternatives more than I did.

title: Worst Device Ever, Even Worse Customer Service
body: So I have had this devise for 2 years now. It's primarily in the bedroom and hardly utilized. But lately it was utilized to its full extend and we noticed that in the middle of watching a TV show, or Movie it does a reboot (does not matter which provider, Prime Video, HBOMax, Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, it just randomly reboots).So I finally got around to calling customer service, after various trouble shooting advice (clear cache, restart, change HDMI port on TV, unplug...wait...replug, and finally factory reset) the problem did not go away. After the factory reset, the problem (rebooting happened while I was on the phone with customer service). The representative told me that it was "normal." Over the years I have had at least a half a dozen fire TV sticks (including a newer model than this one) and they never reboot randomly. She said she will follow up the following day to make sure the device was working properly. She never did. The device still keeps rebooting and the next time I called customer service (this time from work), the customer service representative said he could not do anything but for me to make sure the device was using its original power brick, it is.The device keeps rebooting, so I called customer service again tonight, this time they wanted me to try all of the previous options again, on top of that they started blaming my wifi connection.I told them it's not the wifi, the wifi does not control the device. They keep apologizing but offering the same redundant trouble shooting advice that did not does not work. I asked to speak to a supervisor, things escalate even more toward the wifi. Mind you, I never even hinted I had connection issues. But they insisted its the wifi and for me to reboot my router. How in the world would that be the cause? Plus I actually have my router automatically reboot every other day at a specific time, so I know it's not an issue. After getting frustrated, I hung up on the supervisor, but received an email regarding trouble shooting my wifi connection (attached screen shot). Save yourself the headache buy another brand, I have a Roku as well and I think I'm going to stick to that from now on.

title: poor quality getting worse
body: I was forced to buy a new 4k model when my old firestick stopped working, and couldn't finish the boot process. That 4k firestick along with a second 4k model purchased in the last month are now $100 worth of trash. They garbage compared to the previous, model that I owned, which at least lasted for a little over a year before it died. I bought the one for the bedroom to replace the dead one, and decided to get another for the living room. Now neither one works. Both stuck in an infinite loop, continuously powering on and off. Won't even stay on long enough to do a factory reset. Looking online people suggest changing out the power block which I have done. I've tried 4 different Samsung fast charging blocks as well as 3 different Firestick blocks, and a couple Apple iPad 2.1 amp blocks. Nothing changes. The price went up from under $30 each to over $50 but neither unit made it through a month. I get it, the old units didn't do 4K, but neither do these new ones, now. I'll be calling Amazon support tomorrow, but I bet they won't fix the problem. They'll just do like they did last month and tell me they're out of warranty and all they can do is sell me a new one. What ever happened to quality manufacturers, and companies standing behind their products. From talking to friends and co-workers they sell an awful lot of these Firesticks. Everyone I know has at least one. Can't they talk to their Chinese supplier to get them to improve product reliability, or is the low quality of these new models Amazon's fault, because they've pushed their suppliers to use cheaper components to meet a lower price point? They're just little android bootable drives when it comes down to it. We don't really need to buy Firesticks. There are a million Android tv boxes sold online. There must be at least one of them that uses quality parts, even if it costs a little bit more, it's worth it, in my opinion, not to have to deal with all the time and aggravation of trying to find and then testing out all these supposed "fixes". My blood is just boiling at the moment. To angry to talk to tech support now. It just wouldn't be good for either of us.

title: Buy this if you need encouragement to by a roku or chromecast (with tv)
body: I like Amazon, just not some of their products.I have had two of their HD tablets. I would have returned them, but they were gifted to me (new in box). After playing with one, I decided it was a useless Android wanna be. Gave the unopened one away, and the other is actually being used as a wedge to keep an old computer table level. (seriously, that is the best use I could find for it.)But this review is about the Amazon Firestick 4K. I bought this product, along with a regular Firestick, on Amazon Prime Day 2019. I configured it, and played with it a little. My TV has an Amazon Prime Video app built in, so I probably didn't need this thing at the time and hence, it was left plugged into the back of the 4K TV (unused).I tried to use the Firestick in the late spring/early summer. It would not boot up all the way. It was in a reboot loop. An attempt to do a factory reset with the remote failed, probably because it would not start up properly and rebooted before the 10 second window. Who knows. I read that changing out the power supply and cable might help. I tried that, it didn't help. I shelved the issue.In October 2020, I tried calling Amazon support to see if they could get this POS working again. Was walked through the same steps above. Was told it must be a hardware failure.My Chromecast generation 1, that still works. My Roku, that still works. Hey, they have reset buttons. Amazon Firestick, no reset button. I think it would be common sense to put a button on the device itself to do a factory reset. Why the Firestick doesn't have this is a mystery.I think I was going to be offered a small discount on a new FireStick, but I didn't wait long enough to hear it. The issues I had with this thing was the encouragement I needed for me to buy the new Chromecast with Live TV.In early 2020, I changed internet providers. New router. IMO, this problem started as a result of it. And the Amazon device had no way to recover from it. But I guess it would be a hardware issue.

title: SUCKS
body: This is by far the worst experience I've had with any firestick. I have purchased at least 8 for myself and as gifts. I've had issues with it since day 1.1. It wouldn't mirror from any other device.2. It resets itself constantly but much more when using the 5ghz wifi rather than the 2.4.3. It doesn't respond to the remote without pushing the buttons twice.4. Loses the password for my VPN regularly and have to reentering.5. Through put is much slower than on any other device on my network. Have done side by side comparisons and it will be running 30mhz down when all others are getting 170mhz.Customer service is lacking to say the least. I was sent 2 replacements that were actually worse than the original.The 1st wouldn't connect to my network at all. I'm talking about scanning for signal and never seeing anything. All devices in my home will see both my wifi signals and several of my neighbors. While on the phone with customer service, she's asking me to go through all the steps to make sure that I'm not blind, stupid, mentally retarded, etc. I guess that's what she was doing because I certainly felt that way by the time it was done. She finally agreed that it didn't work. I was told that they were going to send me a new replacement that was a complete kit with remote,cable and charger. That didn't happen either. The 1st was obviously a refurb or return. Not sure which. When it arrived, it was just the stick, nothing else. The new replacement device was basically worse than the original. Same issues. No mirroring, resets constantly, drops the higher through put band much more than the original, which I didn't think was even possible. Finally gave up, sent them both back and am now using the original that still sucks to say the least. I've tried the original on 3 different TVs. The 1st was an old Samsung 52" plasma. The 2nd was a Samsung 58" 2017 that was also purchased from Amazon. The 3rd is a Samsung 75" 4k UHD TV. Do your research. I love my other firesticks but I'm leery to try another.

title: NO DOLBY ATMOS ON NETFLIX; INTERMITTENT ON AMAZON PRIME
body: I'm looking for a streaming device that will consistently give me Dolby Atmos sound when it's available.Basically, there are four streaming sources for this: Vudu, Prime, Netflix and Apple TV. Currently, no streaming device that I know of will give you Atmos on all four.If the stick had been more consistent, I would have given it a higher rating. However, from the beginning I noticed two things: 1. no support for Dolby Atmos on Netflix, and 2. inconsistent 4K pass-through (and Atmos), even on Amazon Prime.Setup: I installed the fire stick directly into one of the HDMI ports on my Onkyo TX-NR686. The Onkyo recognized it immediately, and the fire stick guided me through the set up, which was easy. My Alexa dot worked with the system immediately (but on a later attempt, could not find the TV or the fire stick).Netflix (even if you pay extra for their so-called highest quality, which I do) only produces Atmos on a few devices, it's true, but the Fire Stick 4K claims (right on the Amazon sales page) to give you Dolby Atmos on Netflix and it doesn't. After trying that, I queued up Jack Ryan on Prime and immediately got UHD and Dolby Atmos. However, when I went back to Prime a few minutes later and queued up Jack Ryan, I got only Dolby Surround and a note that the program material was playing in 1080p (not 4K), saying that my network connection was too slow (I get a download of over 85 mbps, so that should never be a problem).The search for Dolby Atmos content continues. I will update this review when I know more.NOTE: I had a 6-series TCL 65" Roku TV and got Netflix Dolby Atmos perfectly with that rig. When I replaced it with a 4-series TCL 75" Roku TV, I could no longer get Atmos on Netflix. However, everything on Disney+ that claims to be Dolby Atmos comes through loud and clear in Atmos. Amazon Prime does NOT give me Dolby Atmos through my TV's system. It looks as if I'll have to shell out almost $200 for an Apple TV 4K, or do without Atmos (except on Disney+, of course).

title: Epic fail.
body: Disappointed in this device. I got the 4K stick with tv control remote. If you don’t use it for a while and the stick goes into sleep mode, it won’t turn the tv on. You have to turn the tv back on manually or with another remote. After that ,the remote works, turning the tv on and off again. Then you shut it off and go back an hour later and it won’t turn the tv on again. Also the sound keeps cutting out/braking up. The picture is not shuttering but the sound is. Rap that up with the unrelenting Amazon add based content that you can’t get away from and this pos is an epic fail. I have an older fire stick non 4K Alexa remote that has been flawless for years. I also have roku devices. The roku device has a way better interface but the fire stick always out preformed as far as not buffering and keeping signal strength. This 4K stick is a mess. It seemed pretty good the first day. Then the next day we realize it won’t turn the tv on. Ok not a deal breaker. It’s in the bedroom and is only going to be used at night when I go to bed. The third day it won’t turn back on ok, other remote turns it on. Then the interface has been updated (on its own). Now the picture is so big I can’t see the bottom of the screen. It was fine until this update. I go into settings and calibrated the screen to fit my tv. After calibration I still can’t see the very bottom. Oh well. Then I put a movie on and the sound is braking up. Changed the program and same problems. Chang the app, same problem. Unplugged the stick reboot it same problem. I should of read the 1 star reviews before I ordered this. Because the sound problems seem to be a common thing going back to 2018. You would think by 2021 they would have that fixed , but they don’t. Do to the interface not being customizable and now this epic fail. I will not be using any fires tick/Alexa/Amazon anything. Oh did I tell you the only reason I had the older fire stick was because it was given to me. I got it because the owner couldn’t get apps that he wanted on it so he went to a roku. Guess what I’m going to do.

title: I want to give it a 5 Star review, but cant.  here is why
body: Love 4k fire tv. Works most of the time with alexia, but sometimes has issues. Might be user error that is causing some control issues. There are lots of setup options that I might have configured incorrectly.The main reason that I do not give it a 5 star rating, which it should clearly deserves, is that there is no "Native Youtube" app, though access is there, but pretty poor implemented. No Google play music app, No Google play Movie app, and rampant commercialism.These companies, Amazon and Google, should become "Big Boys" and get over themselves in terms of cooperation. I don't fault Amazon for not allowing Google play Movie. That is a direct competitor; understood. But come on, "youtube"? No google play music: understood that is a competitor as well, but then give us tools to export playlists, titles. I spent years creating my lists and don't want to lose them.I work around the "Youtube issue", by going to the app on my Smart TV. But that seems silly. They know we are going to do that so why?I also spend a far amount of effort to be a "Cord cutter". I may be an extremist here, but there is enough commercialism in the world that I just want to enjoy my entertainment without being bombarded with non-targeted, adverts. I consider it noise, and there is way to much noise in our lives as it is. I used to watch sports but the endless commercials got way way out of hand. Like I said, I am a bit of an extremist on the subject. That being said, we do not need to be bombarded with banner ads in the 4k fire TV stick IMHO. If the subject is content that we can get on amazon prime, fine. But pay channel promotions? ALL OVER THE CONTENT PAGE, not.Google Play 4k streamer, is a much less commercialized tool, and I love it. No Alexia, but there are other options for voice to content out in the world for us to choose from.Like I said, I love 4k fire TV and use with Alexa, just to much superfluous stuff going on here. One star till things change.

title: Experience has been ruined by after purchase add-ons.
body: I have 3 Fire TV Stick 4K's. And I initially loved them when I first ordered them. But in the last few months, I have grown tired of them & I'm actively searching for a new brand of streaming player. Why you may ask? Because, after never having issues, I have been recently bombarded with messages that tell me memory is low & prompting me to delete apps. Usually in the middle of watching something, that message will pop up & stop all streaming. And I believe it is because Amazon decided to add something called FreeForm, IMBd, #AtHome & news: All apps i can't delete or remove. I never asked for these, I never downloaded them. But there they are, using valuable memory. And I can't get rid of them. I have never & will never use those apps. I checked them out & find zero value in them. But I'm stuck with them. It's disappointing to say the least. Even though it's more expensive, I may look into the NVIDIA shield media player. It's $150, but I believe they don't add bloatware to your device AFTER you've purchased it to completely ruin your streaming experience.Update: 8/27/2020: The memory issues have gotten progressively worse. I've had to delete even more of my apps and I'm constantly being asked to delete more. If I had known when I purchased these sticks that Amazon would add their own apps post-purchase and interfere with my streaming preferences, I would not have bought them. I have 3 of them and a recast. I am always getting the "recast off line" message, even though it isn't, but that's the least of my gripes. I am heavily invested in the amazon ecosystem, but I am finally gonna uninvest myself. I will still use prime, prime video & prime music, because I have no issues with their services. I am just looking for another way to stream. Do yourself a favor, before you buy this product, decide how many apps you really need. If it's reaching double digits, don't bother with this stick. If you're fine with 4 or 5, it should suit your needs just fine.

title: No search function, don’t like what amazon thinks you should watch, well too bad
body: Original Review - Frequent updates cause lock ups creating need to restart device.Updated - 07/27/2019, received call from Amazon support regarding my review and concerns. Gentleman was very professional and sought to remedy issues. Helped with some issues though nothing regarding my original concerns were resolved. To be fair, I did not have time to work with him on an extensive troubleshooting process but I changed my review from 2 to 3 stars based on concern and outreach by Amazon. If my issues don’t continue, I will update again to improve score but right now, too many updates and was very frustrating in that I had to basically unplug it and re plug it in to get it to come back to life. Amazon support was top notch. He knew he caught me at a bad time and followed up via email with a direct line to troubleshoot the issue further. That being said, when I buy a product, especially because Amazon pulled support for my Sony dream system, I am not happy to spend any of my personal time “fixing” issues I never had before I was forced to switch to a new device, I had to pay for so I could continue to access a service I was already paying for, with the added hindered effort of yet another weak link to accessing my home network.09/23/2019 UpdateDropped another star. Found out this device I had to “buy” to continue to use my paid service and allow them access to my home network, does have a search function. So if you don’t like what they suggest you are screwed. I tried using voice commands to get Alexa to search for the tv series “heroes”. Several attempts yielded a screen that popped up with heroes on it, then the system changes video inputs on my tv and loses all information from look up. Yep gotta love the “improved” device I had to again pay for access my paid service that works like crap. Never had a problem using my old Sony system. Could search using keypad but nothing on this garbage device. Next issue it drops to one star.

title: This is why this has a 1 star review...the remote control device
body: I bought this 4K Fire Stick to use on the other tv in our bedroom even though the Vizio itself isn't a 4K. We had planned to get another tv with 4K so I thought I'd purchase anyway since I had an Amazon credit.Today (11 April) I tried to install it and here is the problem:The ridiculous remote will NOT open!! Will. Not. Open. !!! It's impossible. By the way, I didn't have any problem whatsoever with the first Fire Stick I purchased. Everything about installation was easy-peasy including opening the remote control device to install the batteries.Often I think "Well, this has to be me..." but nope. Not this time. Go to YouTube and look at the videos about getting the remote open. The suggestions range from using rubber gloves that have the raised bumps on them to give a better grip to using those rubber little gems that open a stubborn jar lid. Nope. still couldn't open it nor could my husband and we do not have particularly weak thumbs!I have no idea why Amazon would create a product that is so extraordinarily difficult to use. Mind you, I'm finally attaching it to the bedroom tv because we are still in quarantine mode and anything that can help dispel the stir crazies is welcome. The frustration we are experiencing is not what either had in mind as a diversionary tactic.Fortunately, it's under warranty and I'll email Amazon and send it back. They're not answering the phones at Customer Service b/c of the current situation. And might I add, I think Amazon is doing an INCREDIBLE job of delivering products to customers - especially now - and they deserve a round of applause for their service.This review is only for the remote control, of course. I can't speak to the device itself or device performance because I can't get to it. Will update as soon as I can get another one.Be safe everyone!

title: Amazon: heartless, soulless, greed
body: I bought this stick a year ago. It was ok up until about spring of this year when I started hearing an “electroniky” sound. At first I thought it was the speaker hooked up to the tv, so I replaced the speaker. Problem still occurred. So I switched the sound to come from the tv instead of the speaker. Same issue. Now I knew it was the fire stick. I called in to Amazon and did some troubleshooting with the representative. Unplugged everything, replugged, updated, etc. Now it worked fine... for the rest of that day, and then the pesky “electronky” sound was back, sounded distorted, disrupting me from being able to understand the tv almost entirely. Called in another time regarding the issue. Same thing, unplug, replug, update. Still problems. My third call in now had me perform a factory reset on the device. Again, it worked for the rest of the day, then back to the same problem. Fast forward to today, I call in frustrated to have the device replaced, as other representatives have told me would be the last option. I’m told that I will have to buy a new device myself as I am 3 days out of warranty, and the best they can do is give me 15% off a new one. I have spent a total of 5+ hours on the phone with Amazon trying to get this problem fixed over 4 different phone calls. I, like most people, buy $1000s of stuff from Amazon. It’s covid. It’s Christmas Eve. I am told there is literally no type of customer service gesture they can do to replace my defective-for-months device because it is now out of warranty. Incredibly unbelievable! I’m so sick of this company making billions of dollars and not having more compassion for its loyal customers. Moving forward I’ll definitely make Amazon my last option for buying new things, and just purchase from the other stores directly. I’d recommend everyone else doing the same, as this company stands behind nothing except getting richer!

title: Not YouTube Compatable
body: If I had a dollar for everytime I had to restart this thing, I could have bought a whole big screen smart TV. Every other Internet connected device that I own has no problem except this one. At this point Amazon should pay me hourly for troubleshooting.Hey Lex Luthor, grow up and drop your little feud with Google. It's time to start actually integrating a functional YouTube app, not just a web browser-based YouTube shortcut that ~kinda sorta works sometimes~. I've been through YouTube's own troubleshooting so many times and they were as helpful as they could be. At this point it's Amazon's problem. They refuse to actually develop a 4k Fire TV Stick app for YouTube -- though the package clearly states, and it is widely advertised as, "YouTube compatible". Which, if you were still wondering, is a lie. It seemed fine at first but stopped working. Aside from showing long unskippable ads (on an ad-free premium subscription), Amazon's browser-based YouTube constantly buffers and can only successfully cast from any device about 30% of the time (if you're lucky).Prime video does not replace YouTube. Nothing you have can replace YouTube. Everybody uses YouTube for its vast library of largely user created content that spans the past 14 years. It's 2019, get over it. YouTube's not going anywhere and you just look stupid.Alexa voice works okayish as you'd expect, but not great. Often have to repeat myself and I speak pretty slowly to begin with. Also she won't always search YouTube. Only when she feels like it I guess. I'd rather just be able to cast from my phone and type my search in, but the Fire Stick's native casting function is hit-or-miss too.Friends, I've been trying to make it work for a few months, so hear me out. Just get a Chromecast. Roku sucks too. Learn from my mistakes. Love y'all.

title: very disappointed, Pluto and IMDB app do NOT work, pictures don't load
body: bought this thinking i was getting an upgrade... none of the live tv options work... try to run pluto and it says check connection, the connection is obviously fine as the fire tv has linked me to something it thinks i want to watch. try to look for my watchlist or mystuff which was easy to get to on my older device and it is nowhere to be found; just a jumbled mess of mixed tv and movies when i say watchlist into the mic. i found out you have use the prime app to find these things, the base os doesn't access them. None of the pictures load for almost any movie or tv show listing in my watchlist that does come up... there is no search function, you have to say everything you are looking for .... NO way to search without voice. later found out the search by text function was just not loading the icon... the skip add has been turned into a three button pressing instead of just clicking enter to skip; now i have to press up right and then enter. that's deplorable ... not happy with this purchase even at a discount, but tubi worked and doesn't on my older device so i guess im keeping it. The moment i find something else that has smart funtions and working live tv like pluto or imdb im getting that to replace this... maybe my next smart tv will solve this.. amazon prime day was pretty lame this time around i'd have to say. * updated review: IMDB and PLUTO worked after many months of trying them, but the picfures for movie or show is almost always broken so you just see empty black squares which is really annoying to have to scroll through that, just being able to see the names as you move over them.... 50% of all pictures for anything in the base app or prime video app just don't load, even the blue picture for the prime app itself doesn't load many times... (using wired internet, never used this device with wifi)

title: Wouldn’t Play Amazon-Purchased Content; Customer Service was No Help
body: I’d tried a Fire Stick from a couple generations ago years back and found it a bit glitchy, but when a deal popped up for this I figured I’d try it again. The interface has been sped up, and for the most part things seemed to work well, well enough that I ordered a second unit. Days later, an odd issue with the device popped up that soured me on the device and the customer service attached to it: neither device would give me the option to play episodes of a television season that I’d previously purchased from Amazon Instant Video; instead, it suggested that I play the episode via two streaming services to which I do not subscribe.This was some strange bug at the intersection of various divisions of Amazon, and when I contacted customer service I was bounced between them. I interacted with SIX different representatives whose entire arsenal of solutions consisted of restarting the device and de registering/reregistering the device. We tried lots of things, and it became clear that there was some persistent bug in the software or corruption in my specific instance of the show—the problem was somewhere on Amazon’s end. After an hour of being bounced between people, I lost faith that Amazon would be able to solve the problem with the devices and lost trust in the devices themselves. Representative Number Six was quite friendly as he processed a return so I could switch back to my old Roku, where my Amazon content plays just fine.Amazon’s Customer Service used to shine when something went wrong—a number of problems with orders in the past have been resolved beyond my satisfaction. Now, though, their customer service system appears unequipped to deal with rare instances in which something is genuinely not working, and as a result, a product that seemed great at first is on the way back to them.

title: Somewhat dissappointed
body: I own several Fire TV products, a Cube, 2 Fire Sticks, and now a new 4K Stick. I also own two Echos, three Dots, a Fire 10" , a Fire 8”, two 7" Kindles, a Ring Doorbell... probably a couple more things I can't think of now. There is good and bad things about the new 4K Stick that I want to tell you about.The Good1) The new remote with IR support will control our fairly new 4K TV with ease. We like the power button and the volume and mute buttons. That remote almost makes the product worth it.2) Of course the 4k streaming makes for a better picture with UHD being 4 times the resolution than 1080p.3) it is faster and has more memory then The older generations.The Bad1) Amazon seems to have even further locked down the environment. Why can't they understand that Android users appreciate open source software? We want to use any streaming service and rental base out there. The device forces you into their "Amazon Universe" where they control everything. If you feel like me, skip this and get an Android TV box or a Roku stick2) You can't rearrange the Apps on your App bar. This is just annoying because you can on their other TV products. Big step backwards. Unbelievable that this is not something that Amazon is not rushing to fix.3) USB support is abismisal. With the older Fire Sticks you can add a memory card and extend the internal memory for a seamless storage solution. Indeed you can do this with the Fire tablets. But not with their newest TV product. Again, why the step backwards? One would naturally expect an "upgraded" product to have added capabilities rather than subtract. I had higher expectations for this newer TV Stick, but so far I have been somewhat disappointed. Pretty sad showing here Amazon.

title: ruined by recent update STAY AWAY
body: Used to be worth owning until the most recent update that looks like the UX/UI designers were just plain out of ideas. The top half banner ad remains, but instead of having your most recently used apps directly beneath that (what used to be 1 down click on the remote), there are a bunch of tiny-fied app tiles to the side (it will take you 3 right clicks on your remote to get to them) for apps that you don't even use. To see tiny-fied tiles for apps that you actually use, they have to be MANUALLY changed by clicking on a tile to the far right of the tiny tiles - from the icon it's not intuitive at all. To get to your recently used apps, you now have to click past two useless rows of content tiles - Next up for you and Sponsored. All apps used to be directly under recently used apps, but now they are off 9 clicks to the right, at the end of the tiny tiles. It's cluttered, it's NOT user friendly and it's just plain UGLY. And on top of that, each time you turn on your fire stick, you'll see a lovely FULL SCREEN message about it not being able to detect your fire stick remote. Your remote can literally be sitting inches from the device and it will show you this message. It may even show it to you in the middle of a show you're watching - how fun! Anyone who watches TV and enjoys watching TV knows that a full screen message interrupting whatever you're watching is a nuisance. But I guess that's of no matter to these clueless UX/UI designers. It's almost as if they've never watched tv or used a fire stick themselves! Stay far far away from this device and go with the competitors. This device is no longer worth owning in its current state. I went from 2nd generation ATV to the original fire stick to the 4k fire stick and now I will be going back to ATV with their nice 4k device. Forget this trash.

title: Does not work with Firestick and Audio does not synch
body: I was hoping to get a better streaming device than my Roku stick that I purchased about a year ago. The installation was simple, but when I tried to find the YouTube TV app, I realized that it doesn't exist because of a spat between Amazon and Google. After a couple of hours trying different suggestions I found on the web, I found one that worked and was relieved that I wouldn't have to return this. It's important to know that it took me months to find a good, working streaming service and the only one that really worked reliably for me was YouTube TV.So after using the hack version of the YouTube TV app, I found that the audio was not synchronized with the video and even after trying to adjust the synchronization on my soundbar and TV, I couldn't get it to synch. Looks like the adjustments are to slow down the audio due to slow video, but in my case, the video was actually ahead of the audio and there appears to be no adjustment for that. At first I though this was because of the hack version of YouTube TV I installed, but I saw similar issues with Netflix, although not as bad.I was almost willing to live with the audio synch issues, but then another issue popped up. The app just froze out of the blue. After closing and reopening the app, and waiting, it eventually came back to life. This happened multiple times and I decided it wasn't worth it, so I returned the device.Lastly, I tried the Alexa integration with my echo dot. I couldn't find any skills to control my firestick. What's the point Amazon, if I have to press a button on the remote to use voice commands? I thought for sure I found something that I could use the worthless echo device with, but I guess Amazon is still trying to catch up with itself on all of the new technology it's releasing.

title: Great stream quality, but voice control integration with Alexa suddenly broken - removing 2 stars
body: Update: Alexa integration with YouTube TV on totally broken. Suddenly it doesn't understand what "YouTube TV" is, and tries to find things on YouTube. All Alexa commands to YouTube TV now end up with displaying the page to download YouTube, so any voice command or other triggers to tune to specific channels on YouTube TV are now totally useless. Whatever 'update' was deployed today needs to be fixed. It's rather silly to have a remote with voice control that doesn't let you do a simple thing like changing channels.Original review: I recently switched from a different vendor’s streaming device to the Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K. Extremely happy with the easy setup, the streaming quality (no buffering like the previous device), and the Alexa voice integration. Installing applications was very simple, and using voice control to tune to different channels or tv shows is very user friendly and made it very easy to use. The remote comes with voice control, but if you don’t want to reach for the remote, you can use the voice commands on any other Alexa enabled devices too.The remaining part of this review is specifically for those who are considering additional Fire TV devices. If you’re only going to get one, there’s no need to read the rest.After I had used the Fire TV device a while I liked it enough to buy another one for a TV in another room. And that's when I discovered some unexpected and strange limitations in the Alexa integration. In the setup of my second Fire TV Stick 4K I gave the my first and second devices different names, and tried to tune my second device to a different tv channel with Alexa voice commands. The channel was changed - but on the first device, not the second device. There was a clue in the 'conversation' with Alexa though - "Did you mean Fire TV?".It turns out each Alexa device (Echo Plus, Echo Dot, dimmer switches with Alexa built in, or whatever devices you may have) can only control ONE Fire TV device, and only recognize that device as "Fire TV". (See my workaround for this issue for this below). The name I gave the device (you can set this in https://www.amazon.com/mycd) is not recognized by Alexa. Using the name I gave the Fire TV device in an Alexa command, gave me this response: "I couldn't find a group or device named so-and-so". Using "Fire TV" in the command on the Echo device near the second Fire TV was accepted, and the command was executed - but on the first Fire TV Stick 4K device I had installed, not the second.In order to control the Fire TV device with a specific Echo device I had to go to in my account online and locate the Echo device, deregister the first Fire TV device and register the second one, and then switch to the Alexa app to link the new Fire TV device to my Echo device.I proceeded to install Youtube TV on the new Fire TV Stick 4K. The Echo device didn’t find any TV channels when I tried for instance ‘tune to ABC on Fire TV’. Again I had to deregister and register the new device in my online account, and link the device in the Alexa app again. And I had to move all my apps and channels around again, which doesn’t take long, it’s just a lot of unnecessary clicks.As mentioned above, one Echo device can be linked to only one Fire TV device, even though the Fire TV devices have distinct names and Alexa should know how to identify which device you want to command. This may be sufficient for most users, but there are some use cases where it’s not. For instance, if you decide to move from one room to the other, you may want to tell the Echo in the first room to tune the Fire TV in the second room to the right channel before you get there. Or leave the first TV on so you can listen while moving to the second TV and turn it off remotely. Or turn off the TV after you left a room because you decide you’re not going back to watch anymore. As long as each Echo is only controlling one Fire TV device, you can’t do this easily by voice control.What’s my solution to this? Create Alexa routines using voice as the trigger, and the specific Fire TV device as the target for the command. Btw, the Fire TV devices used in Alexa routines are not listed under the “Smart Home” in the Alexa App, but a few lines above under “Fire TV”. I can turn the Fire TVs on to a specific channel (for instance ABC), or turn them off, from any Echo device now. However, adding multiple voice control commands to turn the tv on remotely to specific channels (NBC, CBS etc) seems to confuse Alexa: Only the first routine works across Echo devices, additional routines seem to be device specific again.Whether you really need to create a bunch of routines to use voice commands to control your Fire TV devices from other rooms is entirely up to you. I would like the voice commands for my devices’ Alexa integration to be for specific actions and available with any Alexa device, and not be limited by location because the Alexa device closest to me can only control a specific Fire TV device.Lots of extra work, and given that the devices have distinct names in the Alexa app this workaround shouldn’t be necessary. Disappointed that the integration is so tricky, plenty of room for improvement there, so removing one star.

title: It doesn't work with my LG WebOS TV. Also, Alexa sucks.
body: I feel like 2 stars is a fair rating for this product. It does a lot of things right and I am glad for the voice control capabilities, but there are still so many capabilities that were promised that is sadly *missing* from the product that I received. FYI, I bought this on a sale about 6 months ago, and while it is definitely better than nothing, it is still lackluster and unfulfilling in some departments. I will very briefly go over the negative points below (only the negatives, because others can vouch for the positives easily).Rating:Voice Recognition - 3 star (borderline mediocre, but that's understandable as voice recognition is still a novel area)Stream quality - 5 star (not sure how anyone can have an issue with that, it usually pertains to the media provider)Alexa integration - 2 star (Alexa is definitely a janky AI, and ofttimes frustrating to interact with)Explanation:- Fire TV stick (more specifically, Alexa) is sadly not compatible with my older LG Web OS TV. This is an excellent HD TV and the following two solutions work to change inputs or power off my TV: (1) LG ThinQ app on my phone. (2) Power button on Fire TV stick. To turn ON my TV, only the 2nd solution works. Long story short, Alexa on Echo Dot is borderline useless as she can only piggyback on the Fire TV's IR signals. An example, after I press down the voice button on my Fire TV stick for a second and then release, I can command Alexa via Echo Dot to change inputs, but she is unable to manage this on her own.What Alexa is able to do: change volume, play a show on Netflix/Hulu, media controls (play/pause)What Alexa can't do: power on/off LG TV, change inputs on TV- Often frustrating and a hit-or-miss experience when telling Alexa to play a particular show. Examples, "Alexa, play the Good Doctor" will direct me to a generic Prime video page where I have to click further 2-3 times, first into Viewing Options, next to open Hulu, next to scroll to "Keep Watching" and resume the show where I left off. Sometimes she opens Hulu directly, but often times she does not behave as expected. Often times I will have to repeat myself 2+ times as Alexa misinterprets me and starts to play a song on Spotify. This also works the same for shows on other media providers like Netflix. She simply is not consistent with her behavior. And then, there's not to mention the various bugs I've seen with this command. When just booting up the TV, She says "OK" but does not even open Hulu or anything. I have to repeat it again before she is able to proceed as normal. Supposedly this is because she sends the signal to Fire TV stick, but Fire TV fails to perform the action as it's still loading in. I'm assuming here that Fire TV doesn't send a failure response back, so Alexa thinks she succeeded and that's why she still says "OK".- Harder to dynamically change volume on Fire TV. I figured out the shortest way I can phrase this is as "Alexa, volume up/down Fire TV" - admittedly that's a little broken english, but Alexa rolls with that just fine. The problem is that the volume only changes by a little, so sometimes you want to change it by a lot, but there's no easy way to configure this apart from changing the Fire TV settings in the menu.- Voice recognition needs a lot of work to be considered "normal". I noticed sometimes Alexa perks up when no one says anything remotely like "Alexa". This is perhaps due to how Alexa behaves - apparently she listens to voice frequencies rather than specific words that you might say, which is both a blessing and a curse. I did a funny experiment where I was watching TV, and on one of the shows they said something (not even remotely related to the name) and she got activated. Then I rewinded and played the recording on the tv back, and without fail it had the same effect again. Definitely touch and go voice recognition, but looking forward to when that can improve in the future.Overall, my main complaints around Fire TV are in regards to Alexa integration with it. You would expect it to be seamlessly integrated with their flagship AI, as they are both tried and tested Amazon brand products. But Alexa does not integrate well at all with Fire TV stick, in my experience. It is unable to power on/off TV or switch inputs, which would have REALLY been nice and it's a huge bummer that it *cannot* do this with my LG TV. The voice recognition is also borderline mediocre and needs a lot of work, at least from personal observation. It will often times misfire, misinterpret, or just fail to perform as expected. It's definitely a janky solution that needs a lot of work before it's able to perform as originally advertised. But for right now, I suppose that it's passable for me.

title: Think before you buy, your Amazon Pay account can be easily hacked through this Fire Stick
body: I was delighted with my Fire Stick when I first bought it. I had to buy a replacement remote but then soon after had to purchase a whole new television. The Smart TV's make the Fire Stick obsolete, so I disconnected the Fire Stick and left it next to the cable box which was now also outdated. Fast forward about a year and a half.I was going through my checking account and noticed some hefty charges from Amazon all in the same month that I did not recognize. I begin searching through my Amazon account for hours unable to find where the costs are coming from. I am a Prime member and need to use Amazon frequently because I am often unable to leave the house. After a great deal of searching, I realize that these charges are going through Amazon Pay! There is no record of the orders anywhere on Amazon, but the fees are going through my Amazon Pay account. As I'm sure you can imagine, I was upset and rattled because I always thought Amazon was safe.My first call was to the merchant that was charging Amazon Pay, which was Hulu LLC. After talking with the gentleman for quite a while, he explained that I had set up a trial account with Hulu LLC about a year and a half ago, so it turned into one of those memberships. I had never given anyone my card number so I didn't think it would become a membership and just cancel but oh wait . . . the fire stick is through Amazon and THEY have my card number. Ok, a sucker once again, oh well. Then he tells me that someone is watching cartoons on the Hulu account as we are talking. He cancels the account and does some more looking. Apparently, this person had stolen my account, changed my email so that I would not get notifications of purchases, changed the name on my account, passwords, everything. He even checked my IP address to be sure that it was not the IP address on the account. In the long run, he refunded me $26.29 out of over $200.00 in charges above and beyond just plain membership charges and told me that the rest was considered fraud and I needed to contact Amazon since it was allowed to go through Amazon pay even though the merchant agreement had been closed at the end of the trial period. [What!] He assured me that Amazon would work with me on this because not only was the person able to get my Hulu account and password but they were able to get my Amazon Pay digital password as well. I felt a sigh of relief and began to relax . . . until I talked to Amazon.When I spoke with Amazon they explained to me that my Fire Stick must have been stolen because IT SAVES ALL THE PASSWORDS INCLUDING THE DIGITAL AMAZON PAY PASSWORD. I did discover that I was no longer in possession of the Fire Stick so I believe they were correct. I have nurses, doctors, aides, OT, PT, ST, etc. coming in and out of the house every day. The person put me on hold and another man came on to talk with me. Thinking it was management, I began to go over the story with him about how my Fire Stick had been stolen, hacked and the person had fraudulently hacked through to my Amazon Pay account as well. I was shocked when the man began talking in circles and not answering my questions in a very smug manner. He eventually, after some pressing, told me that Amazon was not responsible for the fraud and I should contact my bank. I did contact the bank but they are going after Hulu and Hulu has since deleted the account. I have been with Amazon since they started so I was very disappointed with them as a multi-billion dollar company not addressing the issues of hacking and fraud on their equipment. One option I did consider was their return/refund policy since I never received the items purchased, but we will see.

title: Buyer beware not an easy product with too many buffering issues
body: [[VIDEOID:c7aaebaa750f9ae3cff6f257aecb0398]] Let me first start by saying I am tech savvy and I know about streaming issues in ethernet and your ISP addresses and majority of my house as Wi-Fi connected to smart items so this was beyond insufferable and dealing with this. I had a Roku I decided I would get the fire stick since I had other Amazon products and it would be an easy connect but before you buy the first thing they should tell you which I haven’t done yet as you most likely need an Ethernet adapter. And stay tuned because that’s my next step after all of the The trouble shooting I have done over the last four hours to try to get this thing to work. Last week I ordered or two weeks ago during one of the Black Friday or cyber Monday deals to get this new 4K fire stick so when I received it last week I figured no problem easy Peezy. But what ensued was an evening of frustration and reading dozens of blogs on how to fix issues with fire stick. Nothing worked I called Xfinity thinking maybe that’s an issue definitely not an issue I have superspeed. Called Amazon they said all it’s probably defective so return it so I returned it and then I just received this new one today. And guess what? I have the same problems! I was on my fourth hour including doing this review and I have doneEvery single troubleshooting that can be found on this from restarting unplugging it uninstalling clearing cache is clearing data moving the modem and moving the fire stick to another part using an extender blah blah blah and I am just through with this! title: Love it, but DON'T buy if you want to stream without buffering, stuttering, or frequent interruption
body: I love this device. I hate this device. OK, now that's out of the way.
