I actually loved the Fire Stick TV 4K so much when I saw it that I bought 3 of them for throughout my house, with the intent to cut the cord, so to speak. It's fast, responsive, and the picture quality is amazing, even on a non-4K HDTV. But there's a very serious problem.I have an Eero (Gen. 2) mesh wifi network (Eero + 2 Beacons) at home that covers my home phenomenally from top to bottom, front to back, side to side. The Eero and Beacons are positioned so that coverage throughout the house is amazing, and there's never a problem with hand-off as I walk through the house. The Eero wifi network transmits both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz with the same SSID.I have a couple of devices that are ethernet-wired into the Eero via a smart ethernet switch, and they are blazing fast and work perfectly. Every wifi device on my network – smartphones, tablets, desktop and laptop computers, smart TVs, Chromecasts, Chromebook, other smart home devices, etc. – connects beautifully and performs flawlessly. Everything that is 5 GHz compatible (about 16 devices) connects at 5 GHz just as you would expect. Except for the Fire Stick TV 4K.The Fire Stick TV 4K will only connect on the 2.4 GHz frequency, and not on the 5 GHz frequency. This is a big deal, because the 2.4 GHz spectrum is congested and crowded, and in my neighborhood can be very slow as a result. That means my Fire Sticks don't stream without frequent buffering and interruption; sometimes so bad that movies or TV shows are simply just unwatchable. They have become nearly unusable to me. And as you can imagine, it's gotten worse since Christmas (all of the new wifi Christmas presents).I have googled this problem to death over the past two days. Amazon's best response to the problem is that the product is flawed, and that the user must log in to his/her wifi router and manually change the 5 GHz wifi channel to a low channel number within the 5GHz spectrum. This is a nonsense answer. First, the low 5GHz channels are becoming more and more crowded. Second, my Eero automatically seeks out the strongest, least crowded channel to ensure my wifi network is operating as fast and efficiently as possible. Not only is it not possible on this router (and on some others, too), but Amazon should not expect its home users to have the technical expertise to know how and to force them to change the 5 GHz wifi channel just so that their flawed product will work. I happen to know how to do it, except that it's not even possible with my current equipment (with which I am not willing to part).Unfortunately, because of this problem, I can't recommend the First Stick TV 4K at this time, and would advise anyone considering a streaming device to look seriously at other options.

title: Unreliable /  New Update / LAST Update
body: So...It has been a year since my last update. Amazon was really great & replaced the unit for the 3rd time. I was really happy when I looked at the box & it said it was a refurbished unit. I figured this one HAS to work. Wrong. It has the same problems in that maybe it will turn on, or (most of the time), I will have to pull the power & then it will work properly. No rhyme or reason as to when or why it may or may not decide to work. It may come on with a scrambled, static filled screen, a totally blank screen or a "Home not Available" message, or, the most popular...No Signal. I have 3 ROKU units & they all work fine. I have moved the FIRE TV Stick to various TVs in the house & it does the same on all of them. I cannot believe I received 3 "lemons", but I have had it with the aggravation of messing with this thing every time I want to watch TV. I am buying one more ROKU & relegating the FIRE TV stick to the junk pile.*******************************************************************************************************************************I have to reboot / restart it about every third time I try to use it. Select the proper TV input, turn it on.....and.....no signal. I have to kill the power & re-power the unit. It may work the next 3x I use it...or not. Very easy to use & setup. When it works. Maybe I got a lemon.Amazon C.S. / tech support reached out to me after seeing this review to try & fix the problem. They walked me through several procedures but none of them worked. They then agreed to send a replacement. It arrived quickly & I returned the original. This one works far better than the original. I have only experienced the issue once & that may be due to me pressing the button on the remote too quickly as the TV turned on but the unit did not. I have upgraded my rating to 4 stars from 2. I would have made it 5 stars but I am waiting to be sure the problem does not recur. I can't say enough about how helpful the customer service & tech support was in this matter.Update....The new unit is now doing exactly what the original was doing....& more. Now, when & if it comes on, the home screen showing your preferences & visited sites pulses like a disco display, or, it goes to a wild display of static. I need to disconnect power & reconnect. Then it will work for a couple of days. I applaud Amazon for their efforts, but I feel this item has serious problems. I cannot return it for a refund as I received it for a gift, so I am stuck with it. My 2 ROKU units are working flawlessly & I have swapped these units out with the Fire TV on the different TVs with the same result.I am lowering my rating to 3 stars as when it works, it's great....but....

title: Too little internal memory and storgae
body: I like the stick, processing is fast. Memory could be better but most of all there is no SD card slot and the memory is insufficient. After the Amazon bloatware there isn't much left.Most people are forced to buy additional devices to try and address low storage issues which are problematic as well. So, to begin with you need to install a SD Card or a thumb drive attached to the devices using a special 2 way cable OTG and USB. USB devices or the thumb drive can be attached to one available USB 2.0 but most people use a USB hub. Please note the stick uses USB 2.0 so even if you get a USB 3.0 your speed is limited.Result is poor performance due to USB 2.0 limitation. It is a hack after all to address the storage issue.Given the memory is too low, if you are using something like Google YouTube TV and the channel is on for several hours, you run into an issue with the cache and the app starts to buffer but never recovers, the only option is to close the app (exit from it) and start all over again. You can blame Amazon or Google or both for the limitations of their offerings.Overall the device is good, a bit better than the older gen Amazon Fire TV Box which had the SD card slot and made it easier to move apps around. Most app developers do not enable storage on the SD Card as a result they have to be moved using a ADB shell onto the SD Card. Some apps still continue to use the storage available on the card despite the app being hosted on the SD Card.It sucks, Amazon could have charged $10 more and offered a SD Card slot on the stick itself. Cost of buying a USB 3.0 hub runs anywhere between $10-$30 and the cord required to power the Stick is an additional $3-$10.It would be ideal if Amazon could charge $10-$20 more and just made 32GB or 64GB memory on the stick itself.Penny pinching to keep the cost lower and make the product competitive has resulted in a poor device and viewing experience. Its like uninstall apps and reinstall them again just to make memory available. Or keep adding junk apps to remove running applications and clear the cache.Over all it sucks with the way it is. No issue with the software or the Fire OS or the device itself. Amazon didn't design it with the intention of using it only for Amazon Video did it?Short sighted Amazon you can do a lot better. It's the same thing with Amazon they come up with good devices and then leave a lot to be desired. Remember the Amazon Phone? I loved it but then no support and the product died. They would rather kill a product than improve its quality and experience. From my POV Amazon Fire Phone was great but lack of support and no updates or newer models they forced themselves out of the cellphone market. Why?

title: Great device undermined by Amazon
body: I have both an old Amazon Fire Stick and a Roku Ultra, so for starters, lets compare to those two devices:Fire Stick - It is an upgrade in every way to the older Fire Stick... better remote, much more responsive interface, and overall just better (I don't have a 4k TV, so that is not a part of the review).Roku Ultra - It seems faster than the Roku Ultra and the remote is miles better (just not having the 4 "quick" buttons with accidentally launch streaming apps I may or may not even use at times when I definitely don't want to use (or install) them is a VAST improvement). The only thing the remote does not have that I miss is the headphone jack; otherwise, it is no contest and Amazon wins hands down. The software is less cut and dry... Both have features I like and dislike, both pretty much have all the streaming services I'd use, and they both stream content about equally. The Fire Stick is much more "Amazon" focused and nowhere near as clean an interface as the Roku one, Roku's search seems to do a much better job of searching across apps (although I don't use Search/Alexa on either, so cannot speak to that), and it seems the Roku makes for an overall less intrusive experience. All things being equal, I would prefer the Roku Ultra over the Fire TV Stick 4k if only I didn't hate the Roku remote so much.A few notes about the Amazon Fire Stick on its own:After having it for a couple of days, it downloaded and installed "Free Time", "IMDB TV", and "NEWS" apps. I have zero use or desire for any of these, but they cannot be uninstalled or hidden. This crapware would be bad enough if preinstalled, but installing it after the fact is particularly offensive.Amazon's Prime Video has always been a mess. Free for Prime, Rentals, For Purchase, and Prime Channels all intermingled can make for a frustrating experience when trying to find something to watch. The Fire Stick manages to make it worse by also mixing in apps you don't really want, streaming movies from other services (e.g. IMDB, Tubi, etc.), and other such junk. Fortunately, you can skip all this and go straight into the Prime Video app; however, the joy is likely to be short lived, since you cannot turn of auto video previews on the Prime Video app on this Fire Stick. Considering I canceled my Netflix account until they allowed for opting out of this foul practice, I just will not browser the Prime Video App. That make for a nice bit of irony... I am back to using my Roku Ultra (with a cheap point and click $6 remote w/o and extra features or buttons) whenever I am thinking of playing a video on Prime Video. For everything but Prime Video, I pretty much prefer to use Fire TV Stick 4k.

title: Dolby Vision and Dolby Atmos..!  (Atmos on only one show)
body: UPDATE: I lowered the rating to two stars because it appears the only content available on Amazon Prime with Dolby Atmos audio is the Tom Clancy series, "Jack Ryan". We were hoping to stop buying 4K Blu-ray disks and start streaming UHD HDR movies with Dolby Atmos through the new Fire Stick 4K. We were surprised and disappointed when we purchased the online/streaming version of "The Incredibles 2", it only had Dolby 5.1 audio available..!I was even more disappointed after searching through the Netflix App, none of their 4K or Dolby Vision movies/shows were available with Dolby Atmos audio though the new Fire Stick 4K either.Come on Amazon, this otherwise great device has "incredible" potential, but needs more than one show to have Dolby Atmos audio... When will more Amazon content and Netflix content include Dolby Atmos..?I purchased this 4K Ultra HD HDR Streaming device specifically for the Dolby Vision and Dolby Atmos technology. I pre-ordered it a couple weeks ago, it arrived as promised on Oct. 31, I installed it Nov. 1. The installation was easier than our previous Fire TV Stick and Fire TV Cube models. I was surprised (and a little spooked) that after the firmware update the system recognized I was the owner before I inputted any information. The Fire TV Stick 4K also recognized I own a Sony TV and Denon AVR, so setting up the remote was easy. The new remote now allows control of the Denon volume and powers on/off both the TV and AVR. But to access the Fire Stick, I still need to change the input on the Denon AVR.I did a mental happy dance when the Fire Stick booted up and the “Dolby Vision” logo appeared on the Sony TV. The first Amazon series I accessed was “Tom Clancy’s, Jack Ryan”, I did another mental happy dance when “Dolby Atmos” appeared on the Denon AVR. Having your eye pupils constrict when a dark screen lights up with bright fiery imagery while the sound of helicopters radiates from the ceiling accompanied by gun shots from behind your head is the only way to experience an action show. The best visual technology with the best audio technology enhances the total entertainment experience, I’m happy and recommend the Fire TV Stick 4K..!Now that the system is up and running and if all goes as I hope, I won’t need to buy 4K HDR Blu-ray disks to play on my Oppo UDP anymore. The plan is to stream 4K Ultra HD HDR Dolby Vision/Atmos movies and shows through the Fire TV Stick 4K. I also hope HBO gets their act together and the 8th season of “Game of Thrones” streams in Dolby Vision and Atmos so I won’t need to wait for the disks to be released…

title: For the first time in a long time, I've had a poor experience with Amazon.
body: Check it out:I bought an Amazon Fire TV 4K on Black Friday. I got it for a price of $24.99 (before tax) a stupid good deal, which is why I bought it. Next day it's on my porch, gotta love that one-day shipping. Package in perfect condition, Fire TV stick in perfect condition. Setup was a breeze, and with Google Fiber gigabit internet, I be streaming flawlessly. Disney+, Netflix, Hulu, IMDB, the whole shibang. I can irresponsibly binge for days with this thing. Love the Alexa functionality, love the speed, love the 4K, love the ability to control my TV and receiver with the Fire remote. UNTIL:I start noticing a problem. If I leave the Fire TV stick idle for more than a few hours, it becomes completely unresponsive, and I mean like completely. Still sends a signal to my TV, but just a black screen. The only way to get it to work again is to power cycle it. For those of you that are tech illiterate, that means unplug it and plug it back in. Then it boots up and works just fine. But I have to do this EVERY GOSH DANG time. Just imagine an incredibly attractive hunk of man meat having to put down his bowl of ramen noodles to go rummage around behind his TV for like 15 seconds to unplug the power cable and plug it back in. What is this, the stone age? SUPER ANNOYING, I'm sure you can imagine.Me: No problem, Amazon makes replacements a breeze, I'll just get a new one.Amazon: I'm about to end this man's whole career.I WAS DEAD WRONG. They have this new system in place where you chat with a robot, and the robot is incompetent. I'll just call him Donald. Seems like a fitting name. So I type what's going on in the comments of my replacement, and it takes me to talk to ol' Donald boy, who then asks what is going on. I then politely inform Donald what is going on, and it redirects me to THE FIRST PAGE AGAIN, where I am again asked to tell what's happening, which then takes me back to Donald in an endless cycle of me trying to explain that my Fire TV is being stupid.Me: No worries, Amazon's customer support over the phone is top notch.Amazon: Guess again, mfCall this guy up, and he has no idea what he's talking about. He had to talk to his manager 3 times just to tell me that they can only offer me a refund and I'll have to buy it again myself. Which would be fine, but guess what Amazon? It's now TWICE as expensive! So I immediately came here to vent to all y'all like a full-grown-American-man-child.Anyways. Amazon, I'm disappointed in you. How am I gonna watch Baby Yoda if I have to get off my couch to do it?

title: A device with great features and interface with horrible audio sync problems
body: After years of Roku use and reading several positive reviews about the Fire Stick 4k I decided to take the plunge.The Good:- Great Interface- Extremely responsive- Alexa functionality and integration is really useful and not just a novelty.- Support for the major “apps” / stream providers.- Lots of advanced features and room for customization and tweaking.The Bad (and quite frankly horrible):- All apps and streams eventually exhibit audio delay / sync issues wherein the audio and video will be out of sync sometimes by as much as 2 seconds making it impossible to watch.Normally one flaw wouldn’t be enough to completely invalidate something but the audio sync issues cannot be understated. Almost daily all streams across all apps will eventually develop the aforementioned 1-2 second lag making it completely impossible to watch. Frankly even if it was 500 millisecond delay it would be noticeable enough to make watching impossible. This device and its interface and features are better than the Roku every way that’s why it so disappointing that the main and primary feature and purpose this device exists for - playing videos/streams - is broken. Notice I said broken and not defective, a streaming device that can’t stream consistently is broken as product and the value proposition mute. This would be akin to a car with every bell and whistle that can’t drive a mile without loosing steering control or stalling - that wouldn’t be acceptable.The only way to temporarily fix the audio sync is to restart your device (stopping whatever you were watching).This Fire Stick 4k was my test that if successful would have made it a no-brainer to buy 4 more to replace all the Rokus in my house. Until these issues are properly admitted to and addressed (which they haven’t been by Amazon) there will be no replacement if Rokus.One note to add - people have been talking about this issue since the device came out and Amazon has yet to formally admit to it and address it which has caused me to second guess their commitment to engineering quality and even has me second-guessing my use and promotion of AWS...if making a streaming that has an obvious flaw with streaming that hasn’t been addressed in 2 years, what will happen when EC2 infrastructure runs into critical issues - ignore and deflect?I digress...anyways if Amazon fixes this critical issue then this is by far the best device on the market, if it doesn’t then it’s the most useless. Hopefully this review will aid others in making a decision.

title: Didn't Work For Us
body: After getting robbed by DirecTV, I signed up for Hulu and a couple of extras. The built in app on our (not so) smart TV responded slowly, usually took 2-3 tries before connecting to the router and didn't have all of our services, meaning I had to have them on my already stretched thin phone and "cast" them. So based on the recommendation at a local brick and mortar, I got the Fire Stick.First, the syntax wasn't difficult, but not that great. Unless I missed something, to go from one service to another, you have to go to the home screen, then scroll through recommendations, then to the row of apps. Not difficult, just too many button presses. THAT we would have been willing to accept. The reason that I say that it doesn't work for us? The first one had weird motion issues. But only on some channels. Anything moving, even just normal gestures while speaking, would make the moving object, (the speaker's arm, background objects, etc.) disappear but have several "ghost" images. Again, only some channels, and the built in app on the TV didn't do that, nor did the two other devices we have. But I was going to have to wait until the next weekend to exchange it. A couple days in, it locked up and not only was frozen, it was sending reset commands to the TV, so switching inputs would go right back to the Fire TV and its frozen picture. Power cycling would work for about 2 minutes. OK. A bad one can slip through the cracks for any product. (Edit: Or a review! I noticed after it went live that I originally wrote "... a bad on..." Seems a little ironic that it was that particular line.) Exchanged for a second, and no motion issues. That one lasted about a week before it also froze the same way. I didn't even bother trying to power cycle that one. I exchanged for a different brand; one with dedicated buttons for Hulu, Disney + and two assignable buttons (and two others for services that we don't have) and like the control syntax much better. To be fair, the new one isn't in the stick format, and we may have had heat issues, so maybe the module version of the Fire TV wouldn't lock up like that. Because my Wife and I aren't working the same shift at the moment, the TV is on pretty much 24/7. But this review is on the stick.Again, they sell a bazillion of these, and most people are happy with them, so I'm guessing it's our installation, not a bad design. But I still didn't like the control syntax, even if it would've worked perfectly. FWIW, we have neither Alexa nor Prime.

title: As a Chromecast user...
body: This is not for me. I bought this for a TV that was already smart but doesn't support casting. I have a chromecast for another TV, which I have been very happy with, and was going to buy another. However, I thought I should diversify and try another product this time around. Overall my two main problems are the extra steps needed to use it and the lack of casting support (that I just could not find any references to any where). Chromecast is very straightforward to use. Boot it up, sign in to your wifi, do something with a code or something, and start casting from any device on the same wifi network. In comparison, this Firestick required me to sign in to Amazon, set up some Amazon Prime Video accounts, wait for software to install, etc. It nust felt like so many more steps to set up the Amazon eco system that makes Chromecast feel like plug and play in comparison. Even casting Amazon Prime Video to Chromecast is way faster. I feel like the wait for things to install took really long. Also, my Chromecast lets me plug the USB power cable directly into the TV. The firestick works this way too, but every time I turn it on it warns that there is not enough power coming from the USB source. I do not need that much power overhead if all I will be doing is casting.Speaking of casting, the only app I could cast from my phone was Amazon Prime video. I read only that the Firestick supports casting, but I was not expecting that it would be so limited. I also tried casting from Youtube and DC Universe, but saw no option to cast to the Firestick. Pretty disappointing since that was my main motivation for buying this. It looks like this device is for signing in to all your apps and navigating with the remote controller and Alexa. However, since I put this in a smart TV I was already signed in to these apps on the TV. I really did not want to sign in again for the same TV.I will most likely be returning this and doubling up on Chromecast devices.To end on some positive notes, my TVs have video consoles indented into the TV where the Firestick alone would not fit. Thankfully Amazon includes an adapter to let the Firestick plug in and dangle perfectly for my situation. Thank you for the customer friendly forward thinking! Also, the video quality was amazing for the Amazon originals I watched.I think this product would definitely work for some people, but not for me. Probably not for you if you were looking for the same thing I was.

title: A great streaming device with caveats
body: We bought the Fire Stick to replace our old Air Tv Player (Sling TV). According to reviews, the Fire TV Stick had the widest compatibility with apps so we gave it a try. So far the player has worked well. Installation was easy. We picked up an RJ-45 converter to connect directly to our router so streaming signal is great. Controls are simple and the menus are easy to understand. Alexa is a big help when searching and makes it easier to find movies and shows. So why only 2 stars? The high hopes we had for app integration ended up being a huge let down. Before I get into it, I freely admit to the fact that I may not have done “enough” research on this device and how Amazon has it set up. My opinion however, hasn’t changed.1. Vudu is not an available app (We have 400+ movies on Vudu). Despite the fact that Vudu has been available on every streaming box we have ever owned, Amazon refuses to add it. An “altered” version of Vudu is available for the Fire TV Stick. installation requires a specific third party app for installation, The process is tedious and is too complicated for most users. The app you eventually get will allow you to stream your media but nothing more.2. No third party purchases are available on the Fire TV Stick. Amazon will not allow their users to make any “third party” purchases on their device. In our opinion, this is a deliberate attempt to force users to purchase media via Amazons services.3. You are forced to visit your favorite streaming sites externally, purchase your media and then come back to your app on the Fire TV Stick to view what you purchased. As stated in #2, Amazon seems determined to make you shop with them. The Fire TV Stick is the only streaming box we have tested so far, that works in this manner.Conclusions - if you are looking for a simple, easy to use streaming service, the Fire TV Stick is a good choice. If you do not make online purchases for media, the Fire TV stick is perfect. If you Like to purchase movies, tv shows or movies via third party app, avoid the Fire TV Stick. If you are a Vudu user, avoid the Fire TV Stick.Recommendation - Get something else to fill your streaming needs. As a household we have already given our Fire TV Stick to a family member and have gone back to an “Android” based streaming box. We now have all of the functionality and features we were hoping for without the draconian limitations.

title: So Unreliable
body: We have an LG 4k smart tv and were hoping that we wouldn't need an extra device to access all the apps. In reality, we don't. But the menu is so crappy, and the remote is so touchy that it's a royal pain in the tukas to use. We had an older Fire Stick that went to live on another (less used) tv, so about a year ago I got the latest and greatest one for the LG tv. Although still very clunky, the Fire Stick menu is way better than the LG (although I really do NOT like the latest Amazon update). About two months into using it we started to notice that it was having buffering issues. We thought it was the internet and upgraded everything; the router, the modem, carrier, etc. We are downloading speeds wirelessly at almost 600mps even with other stuff running in the background. Still, it would freeze up and buffer all the time. We upgraded everything and still the same problems.After troubleshooting and changing a few settings it seemed to be doing ok again. Then, all of a sudden it would just stop working altogether and we would have to unplug everything and reboot everything. It would happen at least a couple times a week. Very random. That one took more time to troubleshoot, but eventually we got it working again. Now the remote keeps losing it's connection. Not as big a deal...we can get it back pretty easily, but still, it's a pain to have to do it all the time.Now the latest. I started noticing about a month ago that we are hardly ever getting a 4k picture and HD pops in and out every few seconds. It will go down to 240, then come up to 1080 for a minute or so, then maybe 720 for a few seconds, then back to 1080, etc. It's driving me crazy and so far none of my internet searches have turned up a solution other than buying a new one. Also, I know it's the Fire Stick because the picture using the LG platform is flawless and never goes out. This is our third Fire Stick and each one has had issues at one point or another. How can it be that Bezos can send the Shat to space without killing him, but can't make a reliable tv device?Not sure what I'm going to do here, but I don't think I will be sticking with the Fire Stick anymore. Oh, and don't get me started on setting it up to use with Alexa. The least intuitive setup I've ever worked with. I used to love the Fire Stick, but I expect things to work reliably. I know...first world problem, but c'mon.

title: Skip this and save up for a better model
body: I was very excited to have my first voice responsive streaming device. I had always used either a blu-ray or my laptop with a cable to stream. I got a "good deal" on this and I can see why. I have a lower priced Kindle Fire. All the same offerings but with ads. This is kind of the same. I was watching a commercial and it prompts me to click for a discount. While that's good for some, I found it a bit invasive, but hey, it's Alexa!Speaking of Alexa. It does not work nearly as well as my Kindle of phone app. It's a watered down version or "new employee" version, because it seems confused! lol If you ask for one thing you might get told "she" doesn't understand or you might get a completely different result. It connects then disconnects to the tv. It kept turning my fan on rather than changing the volume. My fan isn't a "smart fan" it was just on the same extension cord. If Weird. First off, I couldn't get the remote to work. I thought maybe the batteries were dead so I bought more and it turned out the remote is just fickle. As typical, as soon as I showed it to someone else it worked lol.As for the streaming performance- It crashes and freezes often. It wouldn't connect to mirroring or bluetooth. If you ask for a tv show it just goes to automatically play it from the first episode not where you left off and getting to the actual area with episodes is done manually. The apps are decent. I was happy it accommodated the less popular ones like Shudder. It's a bit confusing when it comes to a show that is offered on multiple streaming locations. It would be much easier if it would just say free or paid from the get go. I still haven't figured out how to easily go from the device to regular cable tv. I was however, able to connect a lot of channels I usually watch on TV through our cable service with the option to watch live. (Note- CBS is only available with their individual streaming plan). To sum it up, I'd say, splurge on a better model. For me it's one with voice control and remote finder because as anyone can tell you, I can lose a remote in 5 min flat! ;p I'm going to have to save up for it because I don't have $100 bucks to throw around, but I've put the word out to my family so maybe they can pitch in for Christmas? :DUnless you're fine with mediocre results and are just desperate for a streaming device thats compatible with your tv's technology, it's a hard pass.

title: 4K Disabled/Blocked 3rd party app downloads!!
body: I I was sooo excited, to get my new stick! Until I began setting it up. I appreciate the beautiful new remote functionality, look and the speed, however I am not satisfied with the upgrade completely. Sorry Amazon, but I have to give my honest feedback so you’ll know how to make things better for your loyal customers.I have tried using multiple sources to download as I normally would, but it keeps blocking me ( as many reviews from numerous customers will tell you). Although all settings had been ENABLED in the Firestick settings menu and Downloder settings... The “Downloader” app keeps stating, “Unknown error has occurred. Check URL and connection” after trying to download the actual app of choice and/OR just trying to get to the URL to get the App. It appears that Amazon has sleft out some IMPORTANT info with this updated 4K Firestick version because it is not allowing us to download ANY 3rd party apps, including, Vudu, google apps, Youtube & etc. This is false advertising, as this should have been mentioned in the other feature descriptions. Just be honest with your customers upfront. amazon is forcing us to use their AMAZON apps ONLY, as if Amazon is not making enough money already by taking business from actual shopping food, toys, retail and clothing centers and vendor competitors and cable Tv providers. That’s GREED in my opinion. Leave something on the table., you’re already winning the game in 1st place compared to all vendors and competitors.One more thing to add, if Amazon Prime Movie options weren’t so outdated, we wouldn’t be as upset. We don’t want to watch all old movies, which is the mass majority of your movie options. Furthermore, we might as well purchase a cable package with a cable ya provider if we have to subscribe to each individual application you’re offering us to buy per month. That money adds up $$$.The Firestick has been a lifesaver and an amazing addition to many families to save money on irate cable bills...so to take that function away from us with this new upgraded version is very very upsetting to your loyal customers which helped to build your brand and business through our shopping experience and you all may lose MAJOR financial profits on this and lots of returned 4k Firestick from unhappy customers. I may be one of them and I’ve always been a huge supporter of amazon for years. This is HORRIBLE.

title: Not as good as previous fire tv sticks.... seem to be many issues with this particular model.
body: Let me start by saying, I’m a fan. I generally like these little units. For the price, you cannot beat the function. BUT - There seems to be an issue with these 4K sticks. I had most of the previous firetv sticks and never had an issue. Received this 4K stick and nothing but problems until unit froze yesterday.First, the remote unpaired almost daily. It’s PITA to have to pair the unit every day. Second, there were Wi-Fi issues multiple times a day, where it would drop the Wi-Fi signal and I’d have to either go in and disconnect/reconnect or reboot the stick to get it functioning.Lastly , this unit froze on a white boot screen with Firetv in orange and 3 pulsing dots in the lower right corner. I’ve tried everything including leaving it totally disconnected from power and hdmi for 2 hours. Still boots to this white screen. I saw many questions about this particular problem on a community website but there were no answers other than ‘call customer care’. Unfortunately that usually does not resolve the issue.Now, let me point out a real inconvenience with the firetvs. Wifi passwords do not seem to save on your amazon account from one device to another, also firmware updates of entry wipe out Wi-Fi passwords. There is also no way to save a configuration from one stick to a new stick, or to batch download apps that were on your previous stick. If you have already configured one stick, you should be Presented a list of apps from your previous stick and Che k them off of a list so the can be batch downloaded. As it stands, one most open each app link, click download, back out and then repeat for most apps.02/05/19 - update. After going through the horrible of chat support with techs that obviously do not know the product, I was able to get Amazon to replace the unit. Amazon should have dedicated support teams for their product families,I.i., Kindle’s, firetvs, etc.... and users have direct access to these teams, not a generalized chat session with a tech that has no clue. Hopefully the replace,ent lasts longer than the first one. I still have gen1 and gen 2 firetv sticks that work, albeit very slowly, but they still work.I upgraded my review by one star to two because they were willing to replace it. Reading through all the reviews and comments there seems to be issues with this model. Many users have the same issues.

title: Sound Drops off, started mildly. Now it happens Every day
body: [[VIDEOID:52d60172a89e4b325d695d6ae2fe0135]] I have to say that when i got this new fire stick i was very excited. I've owned the previous one and never had one issue. I was happy the new remote included volume and ability to power off/on TV, the sound even worked through my soundbar. My TV was already a smart Samsung TV but I was so happy with the Amazon fire stick i bought a second one for my second TV.Well from the Day one I experienced the sound drop off. Thinking it was my internet service or my soundbar. But as time went by and the sound drop off completely blacks out my TV and freezes it, and after so much troubleshooting every time, first thinking it was my internet. Then cable connections i finally came to the conclusion that restarting the fire stick would fix it. But even when doing that it is now happening every day. First it was every other month very mild symptoms. A quick power off would fix it. But now even if i unplug restart though settings or just disconnect from TV it does not work. I keep everything up to date and i have to admit i should have exchanged since i first got it, but i had faith it was never really the sticks issue. After all troubleshooting which i am very well aware of how to troubleshoot, i have tried different tvs, internet sources, hdmis, sound systems and it's the damn stick. That really sucks. My first fire stick is still running without a single issue. It just sucks because that's the one I have in my bedroom and it doesn't have the same remote as the new one. Anyways finally got fed up and decided to write a proper review. Sucks that i love the Amazon interface and that i am so used to it, because i been thinking of just testing some other streaming service.Also the Hulu app it's absolutely horrible in this app. That is where most of the sound drop off happens and no amount of troubleshooting that app has made a single difference, clear cache, clear data, restart, uninstall, update, yadda yadda yadda. It all does not workAttached is the video of the 4th time of me restarting this damn stick and it always stay stuck in the same spot. Before you question my internet connection i can guarantee you that my internet is fineI just finally caved in and going to do an FDR, after working in tech support for years, i know it will help, maybe a day, maybe a month but the problem will come back !

title: Woefully glitchy, especially when paired with Alexa devices
body: I have spent an insane number of hours on the phone with Amazon Alexa tech support, trying to troubleshoot the legion of HW/SW issues that we originally thought were Echo device based, but have since extrapolated are likely originating in the FireTV Stick. Regardless, the problems persist.1 - when two 3rd gen Echo speakers are paired, I have a nice stereo sound system.2 - when these speakers are paired with the FireTV stick, everyone goes to pieces: a) if I simply want to play music, I must switch the FireTV device control setting to "Manual", so the Echo does not automatically turn the TV on every time and navigate to Amazon Music lyrics sheets! b) if I ask the echo device to play a song or playlist, it responds - as it should - instantly, confirming my selection. Then, very oddly, I have 2 minutes (a long time) of silence. Originally, both I and Alexa tech support thought this was a glitch, and we spent days and days troubleshooting the issue, with no success. I then discovered that the music would begin playing suddenly after this two minutes silence delay, and I am suspecting this is because it is still interfacing with the FireTV stick, even though the TV is not automatically coming on. Very buggy to ask for music and get instant confirmation, followed by two minutes of silence. c) when I actually turn on the TV, the speakers sometimes work, sometimes only one works, sometimes everything mutes. No predicting which! I can be watching a show, with sound only coming out of the right speaker, then everything suddenly mutes. I pause the show, then restart, and sound comes out of the left speaker. I shut off TV and turn back on, and goodness only knows what mysteries await me!So, I have three brand new Alexa-enabled devices, recently added to my Alexa-infested house (please don't ask me how many Echos, Alexa Taps, Dots, EchoPlus and other such devices I now have. It's embarrassing and my wife is very angry with me). I have configured them exactly as directed by no less than 9 separate tech support experts from the Alexa team...and they still won't work. My wife wants me to send it all back, but middle-aged white male with nothing better to do that I am, I refuse to give up. I *cannot* let these little monsters get the better of me!Help.

title: had been a frustrating process.
body: it took a few phone calls and talking to some nice rep some not so nice rep but finally I had to finger it out to get it to work. When I 1st got it, it was like a no brainer to connect. I am no professional but this is not the 1st TV that I have hooked up! For what ever reason the fire stick "shuts down" everytime the TV is off I tried different HDMI port, different outlets on the power strip etc. I had to take the fire stick out and replug it so it would reboot. called support got a lady who gave me two options either 1) send me a new one or 2) update software and call me back to check on me in a couple of days. I didn't want them to wast money so I opted to try the update, noone called me back so I called got a rude support person who talked to me like I was stupid! refusing to send me a replacement but really gave me no other option, everything he wanted me to do I told him I had tried, Finally unwilling he agreed to send me a replacement. well, that didn't work either!!! I finally decided to try plugging the usb power to the back of the TV instead of the power outlet even though it is not recommended because it says it might not have enough power to load the update but low and behold, it worked!!! NOT one single support person suggested this option! To be fair, so the problem may not be the firestick, I still don't understand why. I have the same power Surge protector power strip and DVD player, sound bar, Satellite box. VCR (yes, VCR) all worked but the fire stick didn't. the fact that there is no independent power button for the firestick (it's synced with the TV so the power on the fire stick turns on both the TV and the fire stick) causing it not to restart after the TV was turned off. Now that it's the power of the fire stick is plugged into the TV as soon as I switch the "input" of the TV to HDMI port it's plugged into it fires up! Then there're those channels or apps or what ever you call them. Most of them want you to subscribe for a fee after a trial period. There are a couple free ones and of course there's always Amazon prime stuff to work. I only got this as a secondary source for when there's nothing to watch and didn't want to pay monthly for streaming service. now that it's working It's ok I just can't get over how rude that one support guy was!!!

title: Works great until bricked!
body: I own 2 Fire sticks and 2 4k Fire sticks. Overall have loved these devices aside from the barrage of Amazon-pushed ads and inability to fully control what you want to see up front. However, my most heavily used non-4k stick recently got frozen in a loop of upgrading - stuck mid-way then rebooting and going thru this sequence unending. I found the extremely difficult to find "Chat" section in Amazon where you can chat with a customer service rep. We tried to trouble shoot with all the reset and factory reset settings - without resolution. Ultimately, the rep got back on the line and said my device is broken and cannot be fixed as it is out of warranty. I purchased in Nov 2018 and no Jun 2020 it is worthless! It automatically updates and during this process it died. He offered me a one-time 15% discount off of a single item sold and shipped by Amazon. Now I shop constantly on Amazon, using my Amazon Rewards Visa card as many do. My first thought to replace like for like - but then why would I buy a device which can apparently fail rather easily shortly after the warranty fails? So I looked at a competing Roku device - ultimately deciding to wait a few months as an updated version was due in the fall. I also need a new TV, so I researched and selected one (sold and shipped by Amazon) placed it in the cart and no discount was there.So I opened another chat session (a week later) and armed with transcript from previous chat asked why no discount. This rep stated the discount is only good towards another Fire Stick. Lengthy conversation, but ultimately he said the other rep was incorrect and this is all that can be offered.I am NOT buying another device which I personally experienced failure on despite lack of abuse. I also mentioned to this same rep that I will write this review as well as start to reconsider my loyalty to Amazon site and Visa card. There are other sites and cards out there. Bottom line is this is an AMAZON manufactured product with their name on it and their forced-down-your-throat ads and it seemingly has obsolescence built into it as well. I am not tossing my other 3 but am not confident of their lifetimes and definitely will not replace them with Fire sticks. So much for making the customer happy :( .

title: Easy to install and works fine, updated
body: Installation and set was easy and streaming is solid. So what's not to like?I don't much care for the interface it seems too busy and navigation is a bit touchy until you get used to it. Perhaps it is because I am familiar and comfortable with the Roku user interface. I am sure I'll get used to this one.The downside for me is it seems more like another smart device like a tablet or smartphone that only happens to directly connect to a TV/large screen. This is not what I expect of a device that is supposed to integrate with and expand the capability of my TV. Logically, at least to me, it should look and work much an enhanced television set and interface; that is, turn any TV into a Smart TV rather than extending my small screen to a large one. screen.Alexa Voice remote is not to be confused with Alexa on my echo. In fact, addressing one confuses the other. Worse if there is any integration between them I can't figure out how to make it work. They both show up in my device list but beyond that interaction between them is non existent at worst or just inscrutable, which is almost as bad.I'll keep it because it does seem to work well and does what I needed it to do. On the other hand only time will tell whether I get used it enough to consider another rather than just upgrading my Roku sticks.UPDATE:The more I use it the less I like it. The interface is large and colorful but confusing. Initially I thought the quirkiness was just because it was new to me and just different from my Roku. Wrong! It is quirky and hard to navigate. Response to button presses is slow and sometimes unpredictable!! If I don't power it off when I shut off the TV I never know what ,ode it is going to be in when I turn the TV back on or if it will respond at at all. It seems to get confused when I swith inputs; i.e, switch from its HDMI input to antenna and back. Often afyer doing this it won't respond at all and I have to reset it by removing power.The bottom line is it works but it is painful. I should not have to work so hard to get it to work. I will stick with my Roku sticks instead of buying anymore of these.

title: Netflix is not completely functional on this device
body: Prime Video works fine with this device, but Netflix is sub-par compared to Apple TV 4K.Netflix decides what format to select based on the audio/video capabilities of the player. It will only play 4K/UHD through this device if the 4K Firestick is plugged into an HDMI port that supports 4K at 50/60 Hz. On my TV, a 2015 Vizio M-55C2, there is only one such HDMI port, HDMI 5. This port won't upscale from below 720p, so my SD content and devices are unplayable on my TV without moving the HDMI cable to port 1 or 2. Apple TV 4K is happy with 4K at 30Hz, so no cable moving needed.Netflix will also not play Dolby Atmos content through the Firestick at all. I have a very nice AVR - Denon X4400H - that all my TV devices connect to and it reports its capabilities correctly. I don't know why Netflix can't see Dolby Atmos support on the 4K Firestick, and neither does Netflix. They said it's an Amazon problem.The Bluetooth remote is kind of a joke. Apple uses Bluetooth too, but it also put an IR receiver in the Apple TV so you can use a programmable remote like the Logitech Harmony to control the device. Amazon, unfortunately, doesn't appreciate why people want to use programmable remotes for A/V systems to control all their devices.Amazon's hardware products always suffer from poor execution and bizarre design approaches, so I shouldn't have been surprised: I have a Kindle reader I don't use and an original Fire TV that I got for CalMan that doesn't work all that well either.I only got this thing because Amazon's Prime Video app for the Apple TV 4K didn't support any audio better than 2 channel stereo for the longest time. They finally fixed that, but they still have too little content for Dolby Atmos; the new Jack Ryan series is pretty much the only show they have. I have a Dolby Atmos setup with 12 speakers - four in the ceiling - so I want to be able to play Atmos content whenever it's available.Amazon shuts the door on Atmos through Netflix, so I returned the Firestick 4K. I suspect this is fixable with a firmware update, but I don't have any need to wait for one since I already have a better alternative.

title: Buy a Roku Box instead
body: Apparently, the selling point on the Fire Stick are voice recognition and Alexa integration, but since I haven’t used either of these features, this review evaluates how well the device streams TV and movies (which I believe is it’s actual purpose). The answer is “Ok”, but it’s many, many other problems outweigh the good picture quality and availability o& channels.Let’s start with the pros:-Very good picture quality-Easy sync with our receiver so that we can control volume with the Fire Stick remoteAnd the cons:-Messy, confusing home page-Movies added to “my list” go into a black hole. Why do I add movies to a list that doesn’t seem to exist?-Unpredictable, often inaccurate tracking of “last watched”. Sometimes it’s there, sometimes its not.-Increased marketing of rental and for purchase movies on Prime. You really have to dig around to find movies on Prime that you don’t have to pay for.-Amazon tracking everything I watch. Not cool, didn’t know I was signing up for that.-The remote. It’s a Frankenstein mashup of some of the good features of the Roku remote and some of the bad ones from the Apple TV remote. The “back”, “Select”, and “home” button are one too many choices. I don’t know how many times I’ve accidentally hit home and then have to retrace my steps back to what I was watching (because the “recently watched” feature doesn’t really work consistently). And no free app alternative for phone or iPad just expensive, subscription-based third-party apps. Lame.-Apps don’t always work. Disney+ doesn’t work at all and a Amazon’s non-existent support gives useless instructions for making it work. It would be much better if they just fixed the problem, which, judging from the number of complaints, is widespread.-What time is it? There is no way to make a clock appear on the home page. This is just dumb. I installed a clock app, but you have to start the app to see the time Also dumb.We received the Fire Stick as a gift and decided to use it and gave away our old Roku box. As soon as I can get a new Roku Box, the stick is going in the trash where it belongs.

title: Frequently loses internet connection / won't restart from the menu
body: My overall opinion of the device is not reflective of the categories that Amazon has chosen to display because I have other issues that are not included here including network drops, refusal to reboot, and overall storage space.I've had the current model of the Fire TV 4k for less than 3 months. When it works, it works well. However, the issue is that once every week or two, it will suddenly decide not to connect to my network or will connect to the network but not allow for data. I have had this issue regardless of whether the device is connected wirelessly or via Amazon's official ethernet adapter (my router is also fewer than 10 feet away from the device - I got the ethernet adapter because the 5GHz signal of both this one and the previous would periodically disconnect).This is frequently remedied by a reboot or three, so would not be so bad, except that at those times, the device will also refuse to reboot from the menu. I will choose the option to reboot and it will just freeze, saying "Your Fire TV Stick 4K is Powering Off". I've left it at that state for 20 minutes or more. I've even seen it freeze up like this while trying to factory reset it to attempt to rectify these issues. It's also happened pretty much immediately after my most recent attempt to factory reset it.Finally, there's the storage issue. Aside from the fact that there are only 5GB of usable space, my device is showing that more than 4GB of that 5GB have been used, despite the fact that I have nothing extra downloaded to the device and less than 1GB of applications. Even after resetting the device, it seems that each app added uses multiple times what is being displayed (I added 200 MB of apps and it somehow showed that it was using about an extra 1.5 GB ...).6/26/2020 EDIT: Amazon sent me a replacement, which I installed yesterday. With NO additional apps other than what came preinstalled and the largest one of those showing as slightly over 3 MB in size, I am already down to 4.00 of 5.28 GB of available space. Amazon NEEDS to fix their memory issues or expand the base size because this ends up impacting performance.

title: Registration of device
body: I ordered what I thought was a new firestick (found out later it was refurbished) after a week the remote stuck, they sent a new one but didnt resolve the problem so I was sent a replacement firestick and I returned my original. Imagine my shock when I connect the replacement firestick and it says Hello Robert! Instead of Tammy as the original did. To clarify my shock, Robert is my deceased fiance who passed 2 years ago in my house, I found him. He did not live with me and the laptop I ordered the firestick is not even a year old and my amazon account with the email connected to it was not made until months after he passed. How does this happen? I was traumatized everytime I turned it on and saw hello Robert. I am still dealing with the trauma of his passing in my house which is a different address than I currently live and I contacted chat last night when I felt able to cope with this situation. Everytime I asked to speak to a manager, ask for the general managers name and number, chat disconnected. I was on chat 2 hours repeating myself to 3 different chat connections to be told the replacement was derregistered to me and registered to Bob's account, a dead man. I never got a reason other than someone from his family had to do it. Have I lost my mind? Is his ghost haunting me? No. Amazon messed up and somehow registered my device that is a replacement and came to my house in my name same as original but they registered it to of all people, my dead fiance. So besides the trauma of bringing back the horrible memories of that horrid day, I lost all my free trials, have paid for a device 2 months ago,that I was able to only use full benefits for maybe a week. I would not have knowingly bought a refurbished one to begin with. The issue was finally resolved by resetting to factory reset, which could and should have been told to me in first few minutes of first chat. I never got a managers name, the general manager or ceo name and contact info and lost weeks of services and couldn't watch prime videos I pay for until very early this mo6. This is unacceptable

title: OK product, mediocre Alexa integration,  *terrible* customer service experience
body: When I got this item, I was exited to have integration with my echo devices. All my dreams of laziness whole being under the blankets would finally come to life. Well, setup was tedious, their remote app doesn't quite work (for some reason the keyboard fails to be able to type 's' or 'r' remotely), Alexa integration was practically non-functional, and then the customer service experience was outright laughable and an example of the decaying customer-obsession tenant that characterized Amazon when I first joined.I tried going to different forums and knowledge bases to see what was wrong. The compatibility check passes for my TCL TV, I can use the remote to control my TV and if I use Alexa from the remote it can find all my devices and all. However, if I try using any echo device Alexa will fail to find such a device or it will fail to communicate with the hub. If I try controlling the volume through the Alexa App, I get a glitchy experience that finally errs by saying the device couldn't be reached. Overall it's a waste of time.After giving up on the issue, I decided to contact customer support. I confirmed I had unlinked accounts and relinked, reset the factory settings on both the controller and the stick, fetched updates, changed networks, verified addresses, etc. They finally decided to send a replacement unit after trying to trouble shoot it for over an hour. The unit they sent took 5 days to arrive, and on top of that they sent a refurbished one, even though I paid for a new one, and they saw no issue with it. The refurbished unit also failed to work. I verified network connectivity and other router settings, but nothing worked in the end. Even after talking to manager in customer service and support, my problem was never solved and was told to just return the units for a refund. Complete waste of energy. My Alexa experience hasn't been awesome, but it's usable. I wish I could say the same for this. I don't give it 1 start just because the interaction with the UI and the apps are not half bad.

title: Numerous issues after latest Fire TV OS update.
body: Ever since the last major update that changed the whole appearance of the Home Screen and menus, I’ve had nonstop extremely annoying issues. Almost immediately after the update, I had several random days where my sound would just cut out while watching a show. The Fire TV would then freeze for several minutes and then restart. I would then return to my show only to have it happen again about an hour or so later. I had several separate days when this occurred and when it happened, it would continue throughout the entire day. After that stopped, I then started having issues where I would try to play a show or movie on prime or Netflix and it would just spin and never play the show. Internet connection and bandwidth was tested and fine. Fire TV would show connected and I would even disconnect and reconnect to WiFi without any luck. Restarting the Fire TV seemed to resolve it. This has been happening for about a week and I’ve had to restart it at least 5 times already. Today, I used Alexa to play a movie and after watching it, I tried to watch other shows and movies and my Fire TV continuously kept switching back to the movie I had already finished. I changed it back at least 10 times only to have it randomly switch back at different points of the movie I was trying to watch. No matter what I tried to watch or where I tried to watch it, it would just keep switching back to prime to that one movie. I restarted the Fire tv and it seemed to resolve it. Let’s just say I am not a fan of this latest Fire tv OS version. It’s always worked flawlessly in the past and I never had issues. Now it’s become a headache and I’ve switched to an Apple TV and a Roku. I wish current tech companies would stop “updating” their devices. There seems to be a lack of good coders and software designers these days and every update is just creating more and more problems and destroying what used to work just fine. Either leave your devices alone and simply release new security updates, or invest in better software engineers!

title: Creepy and prime video is bad
body: I purchased one of these after receiving one as a gift. At first, I really liked it. I loved being able to add apps, unlike my DVD player and TV, and was able to get all the ones I wanted and can download new ones as they come out (even ones not many people have, like national theatre at home). However, I have two issues with it that are very annoying and make me wish I didn't purchase this.I was very disappointed in the prime video app itself. The prime video app on any other device allows me to see what is free with amazon prime and what is not and I ignore anything that is not. However, with this device, it is not clear. There is no ribbon on the thumbnail of the show to indicate it is free. Instead, I have to click on each thumbnail to find out. There is a tab called free to me, but that tab also includes imbd and other items that are only free on a trial basis or with ads. It makes it very, very difficult to find out what I can watch for free and it annoys the crap out of me. I feel like they make it confusing just so you purchase things without knowing it. It is very deceptive, especially because they are only doing this to make you pay for something you don't want to pay for.In addition, there was a recent update to the software that is very creepy. If you are streaming something with ads, the device recognizes there is an add, minimizes it, then has an outside banner that tells you something to the effect of "ask alexa to add product name to my shopping list". This means that it activity tracks what you watch when you watch it and tries to amplify the ad. This is unacceptable. I pay annually for amazon prime and I paid for this device. But apparently, I am not the customer, but the product. This is nothing but a money grubbing act by amazon that is creepy and invasive. As of today, there is no way to turn this off and disable it. I would recommend anyone who also feels this is creepy not to purchase this product.

title: Abysmal redesign for 2021
body: I switched over to the Amazon Fire Tv Sticks from Roku about 2 years ago on all of my TVs. I was initially very impressed with the voice commands, particularly the one to change HDMI inputs. The interface was simple, the app selection was decent, and the UI was responsive.However, a few months ago, the interface received a complete redesign. No longer can I see a list of all my services as soon as I turn it on. The majority of screen real estate is taken up by ads. As you can see in my attached image, this is the main dashboard now for Fire TV. I cannot control the placement nor the content on this screen anymore. To see my installed services (apps) I must navigate to the tiny square icon; the one with 3 little squares and a plus sign.Search was also completely removed from the app store. The available apps are displayed in what I assume is the order in which Amazon wants you to see them displayed. It's not 100% clear whether or not the voice commands are searching the app store. For example, I tried to find the Peacock app. I navigated through the list of apps and couldn't find it. I then used the voice comman feature by saying the word "Peacock." It pulled me out of the app store and to a generic list of various things with that word instead of searching just the app store. The list of things did not include the app I was looking for so I press the back button. That brought me back to the main dashboard.In conclusion, I greatly despise the new "catered experience" and much prefer an interface that allows me to place and reorder icons the way I want. I have no interest in any of the ads and I will never click on them, not just out of spite, but because I already know which apps and services I subscribe to our want to subscribe to. Feeding me ads for content is why I cut the cord over a decade ago to begin with. I am now actively on the research hunt for a new streaming box.

title: Works sometimes, mostly just junk
body: I bought this because my somewhat older Smart TV would not support live streaming TV News on Hulu and I was not able to upgrade the software to do so. Hulu recommended a device like the Fire Stick, or Roku, or Chromecast to solve my issue. The Fire Stick arrived and was easy to hook up. However, my 4K Fire Stick only sees my 2.4 Ghz wireless (not my 5 Ghz) and often drops the connection altogether. Once this dropping of the connection begins, I have to reboot the Fire Stick, forget my wireless connection, and then reconnect to the wireless network again. It takes two or three minutes each time. It will do this over and over, every five to ten minutes, until I finally throw my hands up and swear and turn the TV off. Other times it will work for days without doing this, but eventually it always starts up again. I'm an IT guy, so I'm savvy on this stuff. The issue is not my wireless network. My wife and I watch TV together, and don't run anything off wireless other than the Fire Stick. Everything else is hardwired to the router, which is always preferable anyways. I've noted the very helpful "help tips" from Amazon where they tell me to plug in an Ethernet cable to the Fire Stick, but unfortunately, Amazon forgot to put an Ethernet port on the Fire Stick (so they could save about 30 cents in manufacturing). My only option now is to buy Amazon's Ethernet port adapter for an additional $15, but the reviews on the adapters are stupendously bad considering they're just an adapter. Apparently the Ethernet adapters are as hit and miss as the Fire Sticks themselves. I may have to go this route if I can't find another solution. As it is right now, most of the time the Fire Stick works as it should, but when it starts dropping the wireless signal, it's worse than worthless to me. So the two stars are barely earned considering that this thing cost me 40 bucks and it's often worth zero bucks.

title: Has potential for elderly and disabled but is currently a frustrating failure
body: I’m sure at this point in 2021 most people understand what this product is and what it can do. However it potentially has some extra utility for the elderly and disabled who are having a hard time watching TV in the current software-based streaming ecosystems.My mom has early-stage dementia, and for a couple years now has had increasing difficulty using a remote control to watch TV. This has had a negative impact on her life in her later years, as she needs someone there to help simply so she can watch TV. It’s reminds me of the misguided car design we see nowadays, where something as simple as turning on the headlights can be quite challenging for even the tech savvy among us. I am of the opinion that many software designers are clueless about normal human behavior, and a trail of carnage is following in their wake that should be investigated.So, I thought a voice controlled Alexa device might be helpful, and linked this with an echo dot I already had, but we’re not quite there yet. Like many corporate products managed by people with only a short-sighted concern for the immediate bottom line, the elderly and disabled are deliberately, with full intention, not given a product that could be quite helpful to their lives.FireTV and Alexa is close to being a functioning voice controlled TV ecosystem, but is too inconsistent, unreliable and not thought through in a considerate way. For instance, consider the FireTV remote app installed on my iOS device. It would be a revelation if I could use this app to control my mom’s TV while away from the house. The technology is there and everything is in place. But forever whatever reason, the decision was made to not allow that functionality. The iOS app is nearly useless. And along with it the entire fire TV ecosystem. Perhaps they will have this figured out in 20 years when I’m real old and will need help as well.

title: This is one of the most ridiculous devices
body: So I was sold on the voice integration with Alexa and was unfortunately convinced to buy this device to replace my Roku.I made a big mistake. I have tried to make this work for some time, but the list of foolishness grows:1) The remote often "backs up" and becomes unresponsive for 10's of seconds and then freaks out and clicks a thousand things repeatedly. It happens ALL THE TIME. Yes I have read forums about replacing batteries and what not, but to no avail. Step back and think about - in order to effectively use this device I am told to replace batteries every day to "fix it", which doesn't work in the end anyways. Its ridiculous.2) The network regularly disconnects from the wireless network, even when other devices on the network are still active. I am told to reboot or reconnect to the WIFI. In order to use a device designed to operate on wireless. Again, why I am doing all these workarounds? As usual, it is ridiculous.3) The streaming is no better or worse than the Roku device. All the hype around stream rates and what not is marketing nonsense. If you can REALLY detect the difference in these situations, then god bless your world of micro-managing complexity to cure your itch.4) And finally, the Alexa voice integration. It only really works when what you want to watch is on the Amazon "Domain". If it is buried in Netflix, or some other app, then you are at the mercy of the effort put forth by the application to integrate with the Alexa stream. This is HIGHLY hit or miss, even for something as popular as Netflix, where it just doesn't QUITE do what you want, despite the obvious marketing nonsense.And I am suffering through this world of idiocy for a year now, and finally realized that I am doing it only to save $100 to by my new Roku. Which I will now proceed to do.

title: Missing too many features. No fast forward accel. No Miracast/Chromecast. App market is shady.
body: Missing lots of features. Alexa always gets confused. For ex i ask whats the volume level and it asks me for what device? I dont even know what you mean by device and dont know what 2 refers to when she answers. Missing lots of basic features like a cursor. For ex cannot skip or scrub time in the amazon music app. You can only fast forward and that feature doesnt have an acceleration feature so you spend 30 minutes fast forwarding like this is a cassette player.No chromecast or equivalent. No Miracast (open standard supported by all Windows and Android devices, even Fire tablets). App market is shady apps that are adware, crippleware, upsellware - timer-based hidden ads/pop ups, etc.Youtube casting is unofficially somewhat sometimes working through Silk Browser as a built-in hacky feature but doesn't work/is always breaking, e.g. device isn't detected in castable devices on network or casting videos doesn't start (stays at the YouTube home screen if you've manually opened YT on Silk Browser but stopping the cast exits the YouTube app.Amazon either needs to learn to work well with others (as Jeff B. says, being kind is harder than being clever) or put in mechanisms (not good intentions) to make sustainable and high quality ecosystems where every component is high quality.The UX is terrible. The APP screen doesn't even show the name of the Apps and 99.5% of App Icons are cryptic abstract art. Lots of hard to find/hidden menus that are hard to discover and get to. Lots of fundamental mistakes in design and usability.Desperately needs a built-in Task Manager/Killer App to force restart Apps because many Apps are buggy and only work the first time it starts up and a bunch of features stop working as you use it so you can't wake it up from memory.

title: Was not expecting such a jump in performance from the old firestick! (EDITED REVIEW!!!!)
body: A little background:I stream everything and do not have cable. Have been running two Nvidia Shield (2015) boxed for a few years now. They were so impressive I tossed the old Firestick in the junk drawer in the kitchen. The only turn off with those is the inflated price. I think no streaming device should be $100+++. I have also played with a few chinese boxes, they all SUCK! They claim 4-8gb ram and quad-octa core processors, and they ALL lie about this! With those specs, they "should" out perform the shield with a meager 3gb ram, however there is no comparison in performance. I keep them for spares or to loan out, but refuse to use them myself.Sorry, not trying to bore you. On to the Firestick 4k review.This thing packs a big punch in a small package. It is on par with the Nvidia Shield as far as performance (maybe a tiny bit slower), and for 1/3 the price these are worth every penny. Super easy to set up with all the same apps I use on the Shield, at a fraction of the price. The picture is just as good, sound is the same quality (maybe a little lower in output), and the thing is FAST. Probably the best bang for the buck streaming device out there. I did buy an OTG cable so I could sideload apps not in the Amazon Appstore ($7 shipped), these can also double as a way to have an external USB storage connected. I have been contemplating on selling the Shields and buying a few more Firestick 4k's, seriously they're that good.one last note, Alexa actually listens better than Google on the Shield, and responds way faster. "Alexa, turn on porch light"..... :-)EDIT:The Wifi drops out constantly, and this is plugged into my Pioneer AV receiver less than 1 foot away from the router. Had to but the ethernet adapter

title: Do not expect fast connection and do not blame your broadband
body: I am a broadband technician and I will tell you that I come across Trouble calls for slow internet connection and many times when its a streaming device I see it to be these Firesticks. I saw this one on sale for $24 and decided to give it a try and look deeper into the settings. I have 450mbps off a Nighthawk 1900, 4k Sony Bravia Tv and it is flawless when I stream on my Xbox one and other devices, but when I used this firestick (5G) it was soo slow and kept loosing connection. I was streaming "Band of Brothers" series and it kept buffering or loosing picture quality. I understand that like any products you buy there will always be "Lemons" but I have been seeing these cause issues frequently at work. As broad band techs If we go out on a trouble call and see this device as the cause of slow streaming there will be a charge of between $35 to $50 for the visit because we can prove that our service provided equipment works just fine in most cases. I went ahead and returned this Firestick 4k and bought a Roku Ultra and immediately it was like night and day, if you have your router right next to the Tv and you want to use a streaming device then I highly recommend making sure your Roku has the Ethernet port included which the Roku Ultra has. If you have an Xbox one then that would be your best bet because it comparably is a beast of a processor but if not the Roku Ultra is cheaper and will do well. FYI the PS4 limits you on what apps you can use like the Cable provider apps, if you decide to go use a console for streaming the Xbox one does not have app limitations. Sorry if I offended some Firestick fans out there but "It is what it is", maybe its the quality control or maybe the processor on those Firesticks but like they say "you get what you pay for".

title: Works great at first, then becomes frustrating
body: After a little of a month of use, I’m providing my review of the fire stick.First two weeks, the device was flawless. No buffering or dropped network connections. So nice to ditch cable and move to streaming. Big savings.However, after two weeks the honeymoon was over and the frustrations with the device’s ability to maintain a persistent connection have progressively gotten worse. Requiring me to have to disconnect and reconnect the device to reestablish a network connections and wait for a variable amount of time for the device to continuing playing content. Very aggravating.Worse part is that the channels will lose your place in a movie. So you’ll get through part of a movie and the device will hang or drop the connection all together, requiring a reboot. And bam, you have to fast forward a movie to continue watching the content. This happens across all the channels I use, so it’s the device not the channel.Of course, many will blame the WIFI or network bandwidth. However, that is not the case as all my other devices don’t have this problem and the WIFI is so strong my neighbors use it. I can watch the network connection settings and see the device repeatedly drop and connect to WIFI. Again with no hiccup on any other WIFI enabled devices. Of course, after the reboot of the device the problem goes away for a while. Sadly, the problem gets progressively worse. At first, only needed to reboot every 1-3 days. Now, I often have to reboot up to 3 times a day. Problem exists with or without the extender with my WIFI router less than 15’ from the device.So right now, while I was first ecstatic to use the device I’m rather frustrated with the frequent choppy and broken content.

title: Didn't do what it is supposed to do
body: The Fire TV Stick 4K is clunky for being a "stick" when you add on ethernet adapter, the Mission USB power cable, and the extension dongle. I knew this when I ordered, but it is far from a streamlined product and it is more of a rats nest setup than I envisioned. However, being it is the only Amazon streaming device which is supposed to do HDR, HDR10+ 4K, Dolby Vision, and Dolby Atmos, I bought it despite the dongly, wired mess that it becomes. Yes, yes, I could skip the ethernet adapter, but I wanted to ensure the most reliable data connection as possible since streaming at high resolution content requires some bandwidth, not to mention I have a LAN drop already in place.I tried multiple times to watch Amazon's just about only 4K Dolby Vision, Dolby Atmos program Jack Ryan, and it would only show in 1080p and Dolby Digital +, not 4k Ultra HD Dolby Atmos it's supposed to show using this Stick 4K. Moreover, it's annoying that there's a scan little incoherent blip about "Dolby Atmos is not currently supported on Netflix titles". That is patently untrue, as Netflix has far and away the most Dolby Atmos streaming content, so i took it as old information in the product description. Amazon should be very clear that Fire Stick 4K does not support Dolby Atmos on Netflix, not Dolby Atmos is not currently supported on Netflix titles. Two entirely different meanings.I do not like the UI format of how Amazon presents titles through the Stick 4K, since it lacks the ability to customize things like removing unwanted things like Amazon photos, etc, or the inability to rearrange the pinned apps. I also dislike the constant last used app, since it is redundant to the pinned line.

title: Overrated
body: Been using a Roku Ultra Premiere for 2 years. Bought this because it's inexpensive and is newer technology--or should be--than the Roku Ultra and there is an app I really wanted to try. I also kept hearing how great this thing is, etc. So I gave it a shot.Can't say I'm thrilled with it. The interface Amazon uses--I'm familiar with it from the Amazon App on Roku--just plain sucks compared to the Roku interface. But ok, I can work with it.The Roku is still faster launching apps, etc. than the FS4k.
