This allows the single power button to power on (or off) all devices at once.One huge reason I left Roku is the lack of Bluetooth connectivity. I despise entering in information (like user credentials for streaming apps) using onscreen keyboards. Fire TV allows me to connect my Logitech keyboard wirelessly in those situations.While I'm very happy with this purchase, I'm subtracting a star for two reasons:1. The Fire TV user interface, while very modern and polished, can be cluttered and redundant. Understandably, it tries to push Amazon content. I sometimes miss Roku's simpler (but dated) design and layout.2. The microphone on my remote was defective out of the box. I cannot get a response from Alexa. (And when I search my voice command history on Amazon, the log does indeed indicate that my commands were not understood.) Amazon is sending me a replacement remote once they are back in stock. Normally I wouldn't penalize a product based on one defect, but based on what I'm reading on the Amazon forums, other people have the same problem. So there are clearly some QC issues here.

title: Better than Apple TV?
body: I am a die hard Apple fan, and have used their products from the very beginning. Once you build the ecosystem, it’s difficult to try something else that’s not compatible. With that out of the way, let’s talk about the Fire TV Stick.Unboxing the Fire Stick wasn’t anything special. Packed nicely, but not even close to the Apple experience. Maybe that’s where the higher cost is?Very simple to set up. Plugged it in, went through a few easy prompts on the screen, a couple restarts while updating, and voila! There were some suggested apps to download, which I did.Something was missing right away. YouTube! How can there not be a native YouTube app? Amazon and Google please make up! YouTube has become a very large portion of my tv entertainment. I am able to get to YouTube via the web browser.Another big miss(potentially). Being an Apple TV user, I was accustomed to speaking through my remote versus typing. Putting in text with a remote is horrible! When I started to log into each of my accounts, I thought I’d just speak into the fancy remote. When I pressed the microphone button on the remote while my cursor was in the text field, it just exited the app and prompted Alexa. A little confused, I went back into the Netflix app to try again. Before pushing the microphone button again, I tried to see if there was something onscreen to tell me how to do this, but no such luck. Tried the button again with the same result. It’s possible I’m doing something wrong, but it is very unintuitive.Overall, even with the negative points above, I am pleased with the Fire Stick. It’s pretty fast! The apps seem pretty well optimized with the system. I am going to continue to use this as I work on expanding my smart home devices.

title: Decent video quality & control options, some troublesome design flaws.
body: I am coming from using a Roku Ultra as my main streaming device. I bought this because I needed access to a streaming service that is not yet available on Roku.The stick unfortunately is designed too wide interfering with other HDMI cables/audio output on my set. The USB power port is on the side of the stick further creating cable placement issues. I will have to buy an HDMI extender to fit it in properly.Adding channels is not as entirely convenient as I had hoped. Unless I missed a hidden search function, adding a channel that is not listed in the suggestions requires going to the website. I tried using Alexa, but it only put the channel in my shopping list instead of on the stick. Being able to search for services on the device itself and install from there is important to me as a user.What really matters: Video quality is decent and on par with the Roku Ultra if not a little better. It certainly has more video options than Roku offers allowing full HDR, color type, and adjustable bit depth. I like the fact I now have regained basic remote control over my 24 year old stereo receiver that I had long since lost the original remote to. The Firestick also lets you set audio devices to turn on with the TV automatically.Other thoughts: It would be nice if our preferred streaming services were first to be listed instead of recently used or Amazon program advertising. One thing Roku really does well compared to FireTV is give the user full control on how to organize their favorite apps making navigation painless.There is a bit of a learning curve if you come from other streaming options, but once you get past that, this seems to be a decent option for adding advanced streaming capability to your 4k TV.

title: Nice picture and convenient TV controls.
body: I bought this as an upgrade from a FireStick Lite that is still working well, because this has TV on/off and volume, as well as 4K (vs. the Lite's 1080P). Setup is easy, provided you wait a while for system updates to download (there were 2 of them). Remote paired itself and TV controls somehow worked with my LG TV without any actions on my part. A couple of minor issues: 1) getting the remote's back off to insert the provided AAA batteries requires more strength and leverage than my two thumbs could exert, but a slim blade on my pocket knife did the trick; 2) only 6 Apps are visible on the home screen, and you're required to click on the 7th icon in order to view & scroll to the rest of your Apps; 3) the Firestick freezes up while scrolling through a list of available movies in Apps such as Starz and ShowtimeAnytime, requiring you to hit the back arrow and start over in order to view the rest of the selections (don't know if this is due to Firestick's software or the Apps, but it is a bit frustrating); 4) there is no slot for plugging in media you want to play, requiring you to use the Cloud; 5) no earphone jack for quiet listening, but for $24.99 you get a lot for your $ during Prime Days (for the $49.99 list price, not so much, compared to Roku's similarly priced units); 6) the 8GB capacity cannot be augmented by a micro-SD (unlike Rokus), thus requiring you to clear data and caches periodically in order to update Apps when there is insufficient space to install them to the Stick; 7) there is no Spectrum App available, making a cable box or Roku (which has the App) necessary if you don't want to rent a cable box from Spectrum.None of these are "deal-breakers" in my opinion, but it may help buyers to know about them if they are important to them.

title: Pretty Good Streaming Device
body: Update Oct 2020: Still absolutely HATE the fact that when you pause a show the device times out and goes back to the Home Screen so fast. This is a flaw.I recently upgraded to the FireStick 4K from the original FireStick. I don't have a 4K TV but I liked the new features offered on this product. Shipping was a bit slow due to COVID issues but it arrived fast enough. The batteries shipped with the device were dead but luckily I had some AAA batteries at the house.Setup was a breeze. I wish there was a way for the new stick to automatically pull in all the apps I had on the old stick but instead I had to download manually. I could immediately tell a difference in speed. The old stick lagged when I would choose a show or an app but this new one is smooth and fast. I really love that the new remote has volume control for my soundbar. That's one less remote to worry about. There's also a power button on the new remote that will turn everything off for me when I'm done. That's a little thing but nice to not have to power down each device separately.Streaming has been great. Again, no lag and quick loading. So far so good. I also have an AppleTV device and as much as I like Apple products I feel like the AppleTV loses points against FireStick. The setup, use, and controls of FireStick are much more intuitive than the AppleTV.There is one little thing I can't stand about either the old or the new FireStick and that is when you pause a stream it will hold for a very short time before defaulting back to the home screen. I have two toddlers and we often have to pause a show to take care of something and by the time I get back I have to get all the way back into whatever show was on for them. It's annoying.

title: Put aside plenty of time to get the back off your remote - not kidding - find a pry bar
body: I have bought two amazon firetv stick 4K. Both of them I have been unable to take the back off the remote myself. You cannot set this up until you have access to the remote and you need supernatural hand strength to do so. Amazon needs to leave the back off and allow us to put the batteries in ourselves for the first time, provide a body builder in the order, or they need to fix this issue. I've never had a remote with such a ridiculous design. The first one I ordered from a shopping network and the remote packaging didn't have the directions so I looked up youtube videos which were helpful, but I still wasn't able to do it myself. I had to find a "strong man" to help me. UGH!The firestick remote I received from amazon had a picture on the plastic, but honestly without prying it off or having the death grip you won't get this off. (I can't even say prying will help because I have tried that without success because I didn't want to break it - scissors, metal objects to pry, you probably know the drill if you're reading this looking for help.)Once you finally get the batteries in and set this up it's quick and easy. Just search for the apps you want or log in to the most common ones (Netflix, Prime Video, Hulu, etc). Just be sure you've set aside a couple of days to work on that remote.Really, Amazon, I received this on March 30th and today is April 12 and I'm still waiting for someone to take the battery cover off the remote of my second firetv stick 4k. You need to take it one step further in your quality control check and have someone remove the back from the remote. Have them do it multiple times so that maybe, just maybe, when we try to install the batteries we can do it without needing a vice and pry bar.

title: Improvement over older Android TV OS
body: I've got a 2015 Sony TV with Android TV built in. I liked the Android OS, but Sony hadn't updated the software in a few years and it was noticeably slow to do some things (changing volume would cause Netflix to stutter). A month ago I got the Fire TV Stick 4k on sale for $35. So far I've been very happy with it and haven't used the TV's native Android interface at all.Good:* Alexa integration is nice. After linking the fire stick to my existing Alexa dots, I can tell Alexa to "open youtube on fire tv" from the kitchen, and by the time I'm in the TV room, the TV is ready and waiting for me. I can even ask Alexa to pause, change volume, navigate menus. It's handy if your hands are full or you're too lazy to look for the remote.* Performance is good. Menus are quick and streaming works fine. I haven't noticed any problems with the apps I use regularly (youtube, netflix, plex, twitch)* I can use the fire tv to switch tv inputs (to xbox, for example) or turn on the soundbar when ARC fails to turn it on automatically.Bad:* The fire stick didn't come linked to my Amazon account and I had to enter my email and password using the remote, which was annoying. All other apps since then are smart enough to have me login on my phone, then link the account with a short code.* I watched one episode of Ted Lasso on the Apple TV app and it had some video distortion the entire time. It was minor little glitches, but it was noticeable.* Amazon prime video interface is still not great. If I click on "The Boys", it will prompt me to re-watch the last episode I already watched instead of the next one that just became available. Hopefully the next version of their interface will improve things.

title: Amazon TV is great, but, settle disagreements with HBO & Peacock or I add a different device.
body: The firestick works great. The streaming quality is great. The selection of material is so vast, it would take me multiple years to watch through everything I'm interested in.Which brings me design-wise to a few suggested design improvements for Amazon. Mainly, that if like me, you find a great many series & movies you're interested in, and flag them to watch later, you end up with a ridiculously long single row of flagged items. It would really, really help if Amazon would allow customers to create their own categorizations and choose which of those self-created categories to place their flagged watch items in. If Amazon allowed people to do that, it would see how its customers categorize various movies & series, which in turn would help tell Amazon how to best do that themselves. Hint. Hint.The other design complaint, is the inability to remove watched items from the Recently watched list. Such as, apps I experimented with, decided I didn't like, uninstalled, yet still show up as recent watches. Or movies I quit out of without watching to the end of the credits, or simply didn't like after a few minutes.Finally, here is the Huge complaint. Whatever is the disagreement between HBO Max and Peacock that has resulted in Amazon currently not offering either, please settle it. After almost a year of experimenting with streaming, I finally made the complete cord cut this month, believing I would get my access to CNN via HBO Max, and to MSNBC via Peacock. Except that Amazon has not added either app, due to whatever the disagreements between the parties. If this is not settled by the end of this month, I'll resolve it for myself by adding a rival device which is not engaged in such disputes.

title: Most reliable Android TV device so far.
body: I bought this device for $25 on a Black Friday deal to be able to watch foreign live news and media channels using Kodi. Installing Kodi was a breeze. Unlike its predecessor, this Fire TV version also supports VPN which is a must if you want to get around geo-restricted channels. The processor is fast to handle VPN encryption at 5-10Mbps that gives you the ability to watch channels in HD. If you want higher VPN bandwidth, you can use a dedicated VPN router that will offload the stick from this chore.This device also comes with a cool remote control but what's cooler is that it integrates with my Samsung TV's remote control very nicely, so that only one remote is needed to control TV and stick.I gave it one star shy of perfection because, well, it's not perfect. For starters, it doesn't support a vast majority of Android software out there mostly because they require Goggle Play to function. So, if you want to install your favorite app that runs just fine on your smartphone, you may be disappointed to find out that it will not run on this device even if you installed it manually. Secondly, the volume control on the remote doesn't integrate well with monitors that support HDMI and as such, you may not be able to adjust the volume if paired with such a display. Third, there is tight integration with the build-in application manager that, unlike other Android devices, it cannot be swapped. As a result, you don't have any other choice but to live with a user interface solution that may not be ideal for you. Curiously, the application manager seems to "dislike" several applications, and will "hide" them from view. Luckily, this can be adjusted, at least on the OS version at the time of this writing.

title: Great Video & Sound Quailty, Awful New Interface!
body: My husband and I purchased a couple Fire Sticks to replace my Roku sticks that at the time did not support HBO Max. We also wanted to upgrade to an option that supported 4K video and Dolby Atmos. After switching to the Fire Sticks, we immediately noticed a better picture quality and loved the option to watch 4K video. My husband, who is an audio enthusiast, really enjoyed the better sound quality options. We also liked all the app options available on the Fire Stick.One of the cons of the Fire Stick is that we were not fond of the interface, and even more so since it was just "upgraded". We found the Roku device interfaces to be much more visually appealing, well laid out, and easy to use. Even before Amazon upgraded the Fire Stick interface, we found it to be a bit confusing, cluttered and inefficient to access apps. Since the upgrade, we find the interface to be exceedingly confusing, cluttered and cumbersome to use. They made the home screen into an array of recommendations that I'm not interested in, significantly reduced the size of my saved apps menu (the main function of a Fire Stick that should be easily accessible), put the "Recently Watched" further down on the menu and it only shows recently watched Amazon products (previously showed recently watched from various apps), and overall the appearance just seems chaotic, distracting and difficult to navigate. The home screen would be much improved by not displaying various menus with unwanted recommendations that make the screen look messy. I would prefer to clearly see and easily access my apps, see less menu bars and recommendations, and have everything laid out in a more organized, less cluttered display.

title: Great Device after Audio Works
body: Arrived blazing fast and is just what I expected it to be, though it had a few glitches... here's a fix for the main one.IF YOU EXPERIENCE NO AUDIO DURING SETUP: (this only is for those using audio from their TV) I searched online and in the reviews and did not find a solution that worked for me. After a great deal of troubleshooting I found the problem on my own, with a solution. FYI, this is an older model LED Insignia TV and I am only using the audio from the TV (no external speakers).STEP 1: Complete the wireless set up, parental controls, Amazon account, etc...STEP 2: Say NO to the first pairing test and then select that you are using only your TV for sound when that option comes up.STEP 3: Select the first available pairing test that shows the audio increasing and decreasing via the pop up on the screen(after trying all else, I did this even though I still did not hear audio).STEP 4: Complete any other required set up steps, and initial welcome video(for your convenience, if you hit the button with the three horizontal lines on it, the text for the video pops up on the screen)STEP 5: Using the menu options at the top of the screen (Home, Live, Your Videos, etc) select Settings.STEP 6: Go to Display & Sounds.STEP 7: Go to Audio.STEP 8: Change the surround sound option to stereo. Now it should work! Hit "back" twice and try moving left and right through the other options. You'll know if it worked when you hear a clicking sound as you move through those options.Hopefully this helps someone!(Shout out to Insignia as this TV is 9 years old and still works flawlessly, even after several moves!)

title: Much quicker but
body: Redux. Received a new firestick from support. Better. Upped review to 4 stars.Completely rewriting a review.Arrived. Loved the quicker response of this latest stick. Volume control etc handy touches. Alexa expanded but I do not use that much.But was having serious problems with 4k streams. Would all start at 1080....sometimes move to 4k and back again. Most of the time at 1080. Checked TV, and of course my wirless strength , and possibility of a data cap effect etc.. All good. Problem seemed to be with the stick.Then finally the stick just went bonkers. For instance I had it connected to the HDML 2 port, but when I searched Alexafor a title, it sent results and switched the TV to the HDML 3 port....which of course had no signal.Had done many power off resets but had been reluctant to do a factory reset (LONG passwords), but finally tried that..It could not find the stick. Talked to a good tech who advised me to do the power off componenet..that worked reset went through. I responded too quickly to an amazon email asking if it solved my problem. In helping me the tech had solved my immediate problem which was inability to do a full reset, but the initial problems remained...but when I had the time to check...….on some 4k content the resolution jumped back and forth...on some it was there most of the time. On some (like the amazon original Bosch) it is never there.In comments I have not seen anyone with the same problem, so it looks like a defective sample.Hate to go through the rigmarole again, but will try to get a replacement. 5 star potential. 2 star experience.

title: When Fire TV Kicks up to 11
body: Fire TV Stick 4K does all the bells and whistles that you want it to, while supporting bigger video and HDR. The big difference between this and its predecessors is that it doesn't have a button attached to the device, so you risk suffering technical difficulties without DIY troubleshooting or at least without an appropriate cable or device to help with it. It still carries good power and performance for getting my streaming services and little games that I like playing here and there.Apart from that, it still impresses with Alexa on the side. Now if only its remote can recognize my words better...For things outside of Amazon, it's not all sunshine and bunnies as you'd expect. Anything involving rooting or using a third-party service like Google would put the device in a dangerously slow or subconscious state, so it's not worth it until ambitious hackers and devs make it worthwhile. You're still better off putting your Kodi on something bigger.As a big Android TV fan, I recommend this device to get the best of the current generation of media that you will be able to stream video and music from. It goes without saying, but if you plan on packing some of your own content, it's better off elsewhere like in a cloud or a network storage device. The majority of what you do with this is dependent on everything that happens on the Internet.I myself got this to play exclusive apps on the Amazon AppStore, such as IMDB FreeDive and Flappy Birds Family. But for what it does that others don't, I don't see as many downsides to compare unless you're feeling ambitious enough to do things outside of its scope (rooting, sideloading, etc.).

title: Love It! - Battery Cover Access, not so...
body: I only ordered this because there was an offer I saw and thought that this would be a great enhancement to my aging Samsung Smart TV that is getting to be very slow on the apps to move around due to all the constant updates that are pushed to my TV. Since getting the Fire TV Stick, I use the same APPS I used to use, Netflix, Pandora, YouTube etc but with this FireTV Stick 4K version, everything is supper fast the way it should be. This is much faster than my Samsung Apps were when my TV was brand new. I never use the Samsung Smart Part of the TV, now all I use is the Fire TV Stick 4K version even though I do not have a 4K TV. I would only buy the Fire TV 4K version as it has a faster CPU inside and overall speed is faster. Since my purchase, I have subscribed to Prime so there you have it. Oh and I bought a 2nd one for my other TV.If you travel, take it with you and you have access to all your apps and shows wherever you go as long as you have an HDMI input in the TV and WiFi.One complaint I have and ONLY ONE. Amazon, you need to re-design the battery cover. This is the most difficult thing to remove even for a techie such as myself. I needed a tiny screw driver to pry the door off on both of my remotes. It needs to be slid down and then pried off. This is one of those things our aging society will most likely not be able to do themselves. Kids, get ready to do this for your grandparents and aging parents! That is my only complaint. Luckily I will only have to deal with that ever 1-2 years when the battery needs replacement, however long that is.Would have rated 5 Stars if it weren't for the battery cover access Issue.

title: Amazing little Fire TV streaming stick
body: I bought this stick recently after months of struggling to get our supposedly high-end Fire TV Cube to show 4k Prime Video content on our LG OLED TV (model LG OLED55C8P), and eventually was forced to conclude they just weren't properly compatible with each other. I'd turn on the system and the TV would report no video from the Cube. I had some workarounds but all of them required me to downgrade the stream for viewing.Anyway, I waited until the Stick 4k was on sale, swapped that in, and no more problems. The picture is super-sharp and there's not a single glitch or hiccup when I watch 4k content—both Prime video, and even with the Netflix app. I turn on the TV and the Fire TV Stick, and the TV immediately says it's receiving a Dolby Vision signal. I hope eventually Amazon finds and fixes the technical issues with the Cube because I did like that device, but for now if I had to pick one or the other, it'd be the Stick, no question. (Meantime, the Cube is going to move to an older non-4k TV elsewhere in our house.)As far as critiques go, one thing I wish they offered along with the Stick is a HDMI 2.0b/DHCP 2.2 high-speed female-female HDMI adapter, so one could use an existing high-speed HDMI cable to extend the distance between the stick and the TV. As it is, even with their little extender, I now have a bunch of dangling gear on the back left side of the TV and it looks a tad unsightly, especially since I also have the Ethernet adapter for the Stick. I'm going to try a short (6ft) extension cable to see if that helps, because I'd prefer the Stick to be a little ways away from the TV, more hidden. This is the only reason I deducted one star.

title: Great for any TV to stay relevant
body: I'd like to remind everyone that we are in a world where technology is always moving forward. Though Technology continues to change the tech we buy can end up obsolete. With that being said I have 3 smart TVs that were meeting it's end of days.The TV's mentioned are a 4K TV bought when 4K and HDR became selling words for Tech giants. The other are smaller 1080p screens with what I believe still have a decent picture. Now for the 4K I bought a Fire TV Cube 4K. The two 1080p sets got a Fire TV Stick 4K.Now all three TVs were reaching a point where all of them were no longer supported by their makers. The built in and downloaded apps were slow and not responsive as they used to be due to lack of hardware like RAM and processor. Firmwares were no longer being updated and some apps were no longer available and new apps were not made for them. Fire TV Stick/Cubes are the answers. With built in Voice recognition and Alexa integration controlling them all is easy. With a push of a button apps are automatically opened in a matter of seconds. Now depending on your TV screen and internet connection stream quality are all on point. Every app is available and more, if unknown developer apps are enable things can be side loaded. Not just movies and TV. It also runs a lot of games really well.Now if you're facing a old smart TV or even a non-no smart TV with HDMI this can be your next upgrade if you're not looking to break the bank but want your TV to stay relevant. Worth the purchase.TL;DR: Get one if you're looking for something new with out having to purchase a brand new TV. Give an old TV life with the Fire TV Stick/Cube 4K.

title: Hooking it up kinda makes a tangled mess, but otherwise, it works very well
body: It's smaller than I expected. So amazing what they can cram into tiny packages today. I wonder about it getting too hot sometimes though, so I never leave it on all day. If it really has such a powerful processor in that tiny little case that doesn't seem to have any air vents in it, how does it stay cool? Setting it up was a bit of a mess. Not difficult, but it just looks like a tangle of cords on the back of my TV. I did my best to twistie tie it up to the base of the TV so that it wouldn't just dangle back there. I was concerned for the cord and plug in that goes to the TV with all that stuff hanging from it. Otherwise, it works well. It's a bit weird that you can't really shut it off, only put it in sleep mode. It does seem to cool off in sleep mode though. The interface is pretty easy to use, even the advanced features. I haven't used the voice recognition nor alexa integration features yet as I don't have alexa and so many other things in the past have had a hard time understanding me in their voice controls that I've given up using voice control. The buttons on the remote are big enough to find, even in a dark room and are plenty easy to use. Don't really need the voice control if your fingers work. The biggest surprise to me is how many streaming services I can get on for free. It's pretty great. I'm only subscribed to Amazon Prime (natch) and BritBox. The rest of the ones I've used so far are free. I've noticed that even more Amazon Prime content shows up on this thing than did on the interface for my WiiU back when that still worked. The main reason why I got this thing is that Amazon discontinued their support for the WiiU sometime last fall.

title: Updates have improved its reliability. Works great.
body: [UPDATE]My original review was a bit long winded and seems so long ago now. Probably through the power of updates, all the problems I had stated in my original review have ceased to exist. And it was quite a long time ago that the problems went away, so long that I forgot I even had so many problems with the Fire TV Sticks originally. I had continued to use them through that trouble period and long past it, I have even purchased a 3rd one. I love using them. We have one for every tv in the house. I no longer have any unwarranted connectivity issues, apps function fine and do not freeze or crash, and I can use them continuously for hours with no problems. I never bothered to replace any of my sticks that I had those problems with, so these are still the same exact ones that had all those issues. Clearly recents updates have improved the reliability and user experience and it was just a software issue, not hardware, that was causing those problems. Also since my last review of this product, they’ve updated the interface so I’ll add a new photo. I think I finally just gotten use to the new interface as well. Also gotten a new phone with a better camera since then so better quality photo for ya.[Original review]I really want to like these. I have 2 Fire TV Sticks 4k that we’re given to me because my grandmother bought them and didn’t want them. They were unused not even opened. I was so excited and really liked them at first. Even bought a brand new 43” Tv for my bedroom because I don’t have cable in my room so I never needed a tv before. But a month later and both Fire Stick seem to have the same problem.Both constantly have issue with the internet, but I’ve check the internet and it is fine. Even tried to restart the router and that doesn’t even help. It’s easier to just restart the Fire Stick. Apps crash. Apps freeze. Apps don’t even open. Home screen crashes and keeps reloading. Both Fire Stick do this. I’ve tried clear cache data. Force stopping apps. I’ve restored to factor default. Deleted extra apps. I’ve not used up all the memory on the Fire Stick anyways. I mainly only use 3 apps. PLEX, Pandora, and YouTube and occasionally Prime Video. And the only fix I have is to constantly restart the thing. I shouldn’t have to restart the Fire Stick every 5 minutes and I should be able to watch an entire video without it freezing or listen to more than 3 songs with out pandora just stopping. I’m really disappointed because I really liked them at first and was going to even recommend them over the Roku. But my mother has a Roku in her room and I never hear her complain. I even know people with Fire Sticks and they’ve never complained to me so I don’t know how I could end up with two bad eggs.I don’t really care for Alex. I don’t have an echo or anything. Voice recognition works until the Fire Stick starts have issues or the internet starts bugging out. It will just say error. If I leave it alone the internet will sometimes sort it self out. It doesn’t disconnect it just claims it’s connect with problem an further says it has no internet connection. But my internet is not out since everything else is working fine. And we don’t use a lot of devices at once in my house.I’ve tried them on multiple TVs. They are currently connect to my ONN and old Toshiba tv. Was on a old Sharp for a while as well when I had it in the living room. I’ve since decided to have them in the office and my bedroom since it wasn’t getting as much use in the living room and that’s my mothers tv not mine. TV set up was easy. No problems connecting he remote.When it is streaming it’s fine. Looks good. But then everything freezes. Maybe I get a good hour of solid play time a day then it will start doing it thing and I constantly need to restart the Fire Sticks again and again. It’s like it needs a break. So maybe it’s over heating. I don’t know why though because it’s not in an enclosed space and it doesn’t feel any warmer than your average electronic that running.To be completely honest I have a gameboy and a ps2 from the early 2000s that are more reliable then these Fire Sticks.I’m embarrassed to even use these to watch a movie with guest around. I rather use my laptop and connect it with HDMI to the tv.Sorry. I would have taken better pictures but my phone camera is broken and vibrates like crazy. I tried to show where they are connected and that they are not confined. The White screen is pandora after it froze and I tried to force stop the app and when I launch it I often just get this white screen after the pandora logo and sometimes in the corner it will says some failed error web address thing. And there’s a picture of the internet not working. As well of some picture of it actually working. And all the photos except the first one where taken with in a 10 mins period and in that order showing that pandora was not working, youtube was not working, Internet stoped working, Internet came back (screensaver) then I got my other tv and stuff. So yea. Fun.

title: Works, but ugh...
body: Error, after error and more importantly more errors and no support (I tried calling and got no responses but more dead air)... I'm going to be using this negative review as a venting post; so buckle up:I got this product for my folks who wanted to watch Netflix on a TV that doesn't have internet. No problem... Kind of Amazon to register this product under my name; but it was for my folks (whom I also addressed it too, not myself) who not only don't shop on Amazon, they also don't have an Amazon account. Figured I could get them signed up through the fire stick that I bought for them...NOPE!!! 'It's easy to sign-up for Amazon...' LIES!!! Maybe for me once upon a time it was for me, but not for my folks and the device isn't registering the new account after screwing around from 45 minutes to an hour and a half!!! Finally got somewhere after repeatedly responding to captchas and other account verifications.I am not submitting a negative review to throw a wrench in the cog works or to make this product look bad (it's impossible anyways with how many positive reviews, including my own 5 stars after I had some time to cool off and take migraine medication; which is no fault of theirs, but this process should've taken less than 45 seconds instead of an hour and a half and repeated errors); but to draw attention to their systems. After finally getting this product settled in, my folks are happily watching their programs and I have a huge sense of achievement for my day. Initially, I visited Wal-Mart and contemplated HARD about buying Roku; but I liked Amazon Fire Stick because their remote would also be able to control the volume and power of the television they were using, I've heard too many bad things about ComCast that I can't put Roku to full use (plus, we're very happy with our local Internet Service Provider to switch to another provider), and Amazon seemed like a clear winner with their capabilities (their streaming services rivaling that of Netflix, and tons of other benefits such as fast shipping and finally Alexa at your disposal). Their TV doesn't have 4k pixels, but it doesn't mean they won't possibly upgrade to that later and I learned that lesson the hard way when I should've bought a Blue Ray player with WiFi capabilities but I have this product instead and just as happy because my folks have STRONG internet and WiFi. $10 extra is worth it from the old version and only having to grab one remote to watch movies and shows online.Overall, I feel pretty happy and satisfied with this product after getting over some unnecessary drama. I set my folks up with an Amazon account without a credit card or debit card (with Roku, you HAVE to have either and then you would delete the payment later; Amazon doesn't ask for a credit/debit card unless you wanted to purchase something); but it doesn't mean they won't decide later to sit down and add one, because they love packages from Amazon because it does feel like an internet genie that if you can't buy it locally then you probably might be able to find it somewhere on Amazon if you can be patient enough to wait a day or two. Shipping on this device was very impressive; especially with the snow storm that we had, I would not be complaining if this or any shipment was a day or week late as long as everybody was safe then a little patience on my end is not that hard to muster up.Overall, I should've set up my folks Amazon account before this product arrived; but I'm still doubtful I would've avoided any issues setting their account up with this product without inputing a debit/credit card... Support staff I know are going to be reading this: Thank you for this product and the improvements from the previous model, but how can I give my folks the best experience on this product? For now, the product seems a little slow to load (probably due to the snow storm that we had) and on their other television it doesn't have this issue with Netflix.With Kindest Appreciation and Respects,JeremyJan. 22nd 2019 Update:Product quickly recognized the account that I set up for my folks and did another update after the 2nd day of usage. Now everything loads so quickly that there's no issues with streaming on Netflix and no waiting. My folks are incredibly happy with this product (also happy to have an account with Amazon under their name instead of using my name). They are very happy to have an old standard LCD TV and turn it into a smart TV.

title: I do NOT use Alexa. No you do NOT have to enable it. Final review from IT guy. FS vs RO__
body: I'm gonna cover some of the "generalized questions" and skip the "this only applies to me" questions.Lets be honest here, there's a lot of those "my particular questions" that involve around a certain model of TV and tech questions, but those type ANNOYING questions (that are only for the ONE person asking) always flood out the generalized questions that review readers such as myself want to know, so here's a few answers FROM MY OPINION that apply to EVERYONE ELSE. By annoying questions I mean stuff like "will this work with my...?" While that may be important to you, the rest of us get to filter through 1 billion of those "only applies to me" questions to find relevant content that applies to everyone. A few of them are fine but they usually make up 95% of the questions and its exhausting trying to filter them out.PRIVACYFirst off, in a day of privacy minded individuals desperately grasping for privacy in a big tech world, this is like anything else. If you use YT then YES anything you do on there, every single video you click is recorded forever. Thats Google. They're never going to stop. Is Amazon as bad? Well Im not touching that question, but I do trust Amazon a LOT more than I trust google!!!That being said, YES you can disable all Alexa functions, and NO you do NOT have to use it if you do not want to. If you want you can run a VPN on your TV or add one into your network separately. Ask your local IT guy.VERSES1) I always thought their largest competitor (starts with RO) was better, but Im not feeling that anymore.Honestly this Fire Stick brings a LOT of punch to the fight. Maybe even defeating my forever loved and always defended RO. There's more that you can do to this than just watch the basic stuff. You can download offsite channels such as LIVE CAMS from Myrtle Beach, Panama City Beach, and "Florida and Alabama Beaches". The latter is at the moment only showing 3 cams (8-15-2021) but in the past they had many. I hope they return as their cams were fun as well. As it is, Myrtle and PCB are my 2 favorite beaches on the planet (Next to Cancun) and they are the only 2 beaches that I personally visit myself. SO LUCKY ME!!! If I want to take a beach trip, or see how a particular beach is faring during a hurricane, Im a few clicks away! Honestly, I LOVE the beach cams!!! It can be quite entertaining to watch people in public from the privacy of your home. Especially when its a Saturday night and the party of 8 on the beach has all had far too much to drink and is stumbling all over each other and hugging lol. And watching people pick their bikinis and briefs out of their.. well.. is always amusing. Those channels also showcase attractions at those said beaches. Restaurants, places to stay, things to do, nature trails etc. ALL the beach cams are great. Im a HUGE fan! (Especially the FUN with the bamboo corridor at PCB).You can download offsite (downloader) and watch anything from this fire stick that you could watch from a computer. YES anything. No need to elaborate. You can also have Alexa read you websites out loud (or so Im told; haven't tried that)The channel selection I honestly think is better than RO. There's more content that I find relevant to watch. The search channels feature is better as well. Lets say you like Yoga. Type YOGA in and about 20 channels pop up (including n u d e yoga 0.0 ) No I didn't add that one, but you can if that's your thing. Im not judging.The remote control feels good in your hands, and the devise doesn't seem to require much wifi (mine is ran through LAN of course for a better connection) Ethernet is ALWAYS better, and anyone who says otherwise doesn't understand basic networking. So if you can, run an ethernet cable. Mine is ran 75 feet and its plenty fast enough for true 4K. Ive watched some 8K videos as well with no issues.Overall I HIGHLY recommend this Fire Stick and wish I had got this long ago.If you found ANY of my review helpful, informative, amusing, or if it in any way helped you make a decision then PLEASE PLEASE hit that HELPFUL button associated with my review! DISCLAIMER: I paid full price for my Fire Stick, and my association with a particular Amazon service has nothing to do with this purchase, but I still enjoy knowing my reviews helped someone.PS I do NOT answer technical questions. Call Amazon! That's what they are there for.

title: Some Trepidation @ 1st - Simplicity Amazing and Setup Quite Easy
body: I've over a half century of working in the world of technology AND I am extremely picky about what I acquire and my expectations. I sometimes return products within a single day. If you get bored reading, just order the damn thing!If you do product research on the web, some say that YouTube TV is not available. IT IS AVAILABLE! You simply go to the website, signup, and download the app to your stick or smart TV.Suggest you search for discount code online. Mine was easy to find and saved a few bucks. . .I did a lot of product comparison and settled on thus 2nd Generation Fire Stick. 3rd Gen offered little - for me - to justify the higher price.We cut the cord with Spectrum and by using YouTube TV and Amazon TV (p/o Prime) for Movies, this couldn't have been a better choice. The learning curve was more of a Mole Hill and my wife learned How To quickly.The Voice activated remote is fantastic and works well with our TV's. NO PROGRAMMING REQUIRED.Our bill for Spectrum TV + Internet was about $202/mo and if we needed to record two programs we could only watch 1 of those 2. I was told the latest set-top box add the ability to record two and watch other channels. What drove the final change decision was when we looked at a bill. Spectrum added another charge for enhanced sports without our permission. Another $3 that I know we neither requested nor approved. I DETEST SPORTS.What we find fabulous about YouTube TV is that you can sign in on the website and UNCHECK all the channels you have no interest in. The system has a Library and you simply add your favorite TV programs or movies to the system and the shows are continuously recorded on the cloud. If you select a show to record the system records it and all future shows. NO equipment needed and storage is unlimited.When I went to local Spectrum store to return the equipment I found that I was being upgraded to from 100 to 200 mbps internet for $45/month for the first year. I was being charged significantly more for the old 100 mbps. link. Guy insisted that my personal modem wouldn't work at 200 mbps. I was sure it did, but not sure if it would work at 400 or 900 mbps. No fee for modem so I took one. Got home and mine was registering 237 Mbps. I did a speed test to a server in Murmansk Russia. Speed was the same. If you need one, the store scans the information and it is automatically set to run when you connect it. It has no wifi...Back to the subject of Fire Stick. I never realized this, but our smart TV was connected to Spectrum via our wifi system. When I installed the Fire Stick I learned that it requires wifi. I was worried about having to pay for more cable to be installed to other rooms in our house. I now have three cables from wall connectors in my spares box. I had over the air TV with a small antenna on our kitchen TV. Ordered another Fire Stick and got it working the following day. Sound is excellent and video is vastly improved on all sets.If you don't have wifi cable modem, that is a minor technical issue. Easiest fix is to buy a wireless router and plug it into the modem connection. If you have a cable provider, there is likely an extra charge for the their wifi addition.Don't plug the power cable into a USB port on the TV. The box comes with a USB charging cable and a SMALL power supply that plugs into the wall. The little stick provides 1,000 milliamps (1 AMP) and it is rare for a TV to accommodate that on a USB port.Now for the best part, at least for me, We have Prime and live in the D/FW area which is likely why we got this service. When I ordered the first unit it was about 9 PM. The screen pops up and tells me if I order within so many minutes, the unit would be delivered the following morning between 8AM and 11AM. It was ! ! !I then ordered the second one, for the OTA kitchen TV, the following evening. Same pop-up. This time delivery is scheduled between 4 AM and 6:30 AM. On the way from the bed to the coffee pot I opened the front door and there was the package.Some who purchased this complained about the stick running hot. Mine doesn't. My philosophy, gained over 55 years of working in electronics and technology, is that if it doesn't burn your fingers, it is ok.

title: LOVE IT! So should have purchased one sooner.
body: I did a lot a LOT of research on this before purchasing as well as the new one and the Ruko as well.Reasons for the purchase:- We are Amazon Prime members. So it would go great with this service and others we already had.- It was between this one and the other newer one. But this one had a discount on it. But still did everything we needed.- For Our Needs and the features it had were better than those of the Ruko.- Also we LOVE LOVE LOVE the Amazing Customer Service we KNOW we get from Amazon. So that in it self had me leaning towards the Amazon Fire Stick.- It would go not only with services we already have but also future ones we plan on getting in the future.- Quality we can trust. I have purchased other Amazon Label products from Fire Tablets to Trash Bags and all have been just great and even more so for the price as well.Now other things since our purchase. One reason I so so LOVE it is that it solved a couple serious problems we were having. #1 We were having issues with our remote working very well that came with our VIZIO Tv that my nephew helped me pick out because I know nothing about smart TVs. I wanted good quality. But didn't get totally what I wanted from a TV (which isn't much because I don't watch TV myself on the TV, more of on my laptop). I would have to point the remote directly at the lower left hand corner of the TV with the remote the TV came with and even then I would have issues. I did contact them about this and even questioned on the Q&A area. and pretty much I was told I could purchase a new remote that would work with the TV but i would have the same issue. Also even if I bought the VIZIO in 2019 but it was a 2015 model there would be no upgrade in the Apps. So we would have to find another way of hooking up Disney+ to the TV. Which was me having to take my Laptop and using a HDMI cord and hookign it up and watching it from the browser. Which is what we did for nearly 3 months. Because the Company VIZIO from what I read online was only going to be updating the apps from the TVs that were 2016 or newer and not until this past January. Which maybe they did. MAn I spent alot on the TV and they not going to send out a update of the apps including Disney + but had the Hulu and ESPN. :0( very heart breaking. So the Amazon Fire Stick fixed both of these issues for us. I dont' have to give up my Laptop to the kids to watch Disney + on the Big TV as a Family even. But also i tested out the Fire stick and i can even point it at the ceiling and it STILL works. I can have the remote backwards as well and still USE IT!!!!!When you got munchkins this is a plus. Or Boys that want to be Monkey Butts. no matter if one of them stands or sits in front of the lower left corner of the TV the channel or volume can still be adjusted. and with these 2 sticker butts there is less arguments of them wanting the other one to move out of the way. hehehehe For this Momma with 2 Handful Boys this is a LOVE LOVE LOVE.Now also I dont' have to give up my Laptop. and can go back to multi tasking alot better. I use my laptop a LOT for Business, but also we are Homeschoolers and I also have my own classes I take online as well. So the Fire Stick came to the rescue to free up my laptop a bit.O.k. nothing is perfect. So there is only one thing I have a issue with but it is workable even if it can be a pain in the butt. and that is the parental control on there so no purchases are made. I keep having to punch in the code just to switch from say Amazon to HULU to Disney + even if we are not trying to purchase any thing. But Also some times even in the same app to go back to the home screen or something. Like Disney +. I'm so worried that i might get so use to just punching the code in so many dang times that I might end up just do it for one of the boys and not knowing and end up purchasing a movie or TV series i totally did not want to pay of it and over withdraw my bank account and get fees or something or a bill might not get paid.This totally needs to be fixed.

title: Ugh, Merry NOT Christmas to the hubbin...
body: So I have anxiously been waiting for the fire stick to come in the mail for the last week and a half. I ordered this for the hubbin for Christmas because I bought the family a new projector for our outdoor every weekend movie, fire, hotdogs, sausage dogs, real homemade hamburgers on the grill and then we do marshmallows in the fire and if it’s cold the kids have hot chocolate while we have hot tamale’s. The hubbin and I were still at work when our package arrived so I stupidly thought I could trust a 16yo to grab our package from outside and put it up until we got home. Instead it got tossed on our bed only for the dogs to think it was something for them and they chewed up the remote and hdmi cord and one of the batteries. Meanwhile the 16yo is so engrossed in her phone and Xbox that she didn’t notice nor hear the dogs shredding the Amazon packaging, and munching on our brand new $40 firestick. Needless to say, she has lost her phone and Xbox for a hot minute because this is a repeated thing that seems to happen simply because the dogs get hungry at some point after the 8-12 hours we are working and the 16yo can manage to feed herself but can’t remember to give the dogs food and water even after reminder calls, texts, and even Xbox message reminders. So far the remote buttons still seem to work as we attempted to plug it all in and hunt down a single aaa battery. Unfortunately we didn’t get to finish updating the firestick before one of the dogs managed to walk by and yank the power cord out the wall so we gave up for the night. Will update this if the microphone part of the remote still works otherwise I will order another firestick after Christmas and the dogs will be given the 16yo’s iPhone, tv remote, Xbox remote, and Xbox headset to munch on as I am constantly picking those things up after she goes to school so they don’t chew them up. This is a gotta teach them the hard way kind of situation because apparently being scolded 4 different times to stop letting the dogs get on our bed and tear up our stuff or they grab something out of the trash and run to our bed and have a field day on our bed. We are in the middle of remodeling otherwise I would be purchasing a bedside commode for her to use and locking my bedroom door so the dogs can’t get in our room. Beings we are down to only our bathroom right now, and our bedroom wall and door is being extended we currently don’t have a wall or door to even be able to put up a gate or anything. The joys of remodeling. Other than this situation, everything would have been absolutely perfect. Thank you Amazon, Amazon Employees from behind the computers to the warehouse workers to the truckers and down to the delivery drivers, for working so hard to continue to ensure us consumers get our products in a timely manner and fashion. I cannot thank you guys enough especially during the holidays and even more so with the coronavirus and risking your health to serve your country and many other countries. We wish you all a Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year, and pray you all stay healthy, happy, and safe for many years to come. Here’s to hoping and praying 2021 brings us back a bit of normalcy, safer covid vaccines, good health, good fortune, and most of all, happiness. I can honestly say from the hundreds of orders I have purchased off Amazon, I have only ever had issues with one product and I wouldn’t even blame Amazon for what happened as much as I blamed the company/seller I bought it from. Amazon can’t eat the cost from a seller shipping me their last Instantpot accessory bundle, and the box only having less than half the items in it and before I could even manage to take pictures and write a review that specific bundle was turned into the, “we don’t know when or if this item will be available again” item. So I kept what did come in the box as back up pieces and will order another accessory kit after the holidays along with another firestick for the hubbin. Thank you again for all you incredible and hardworking people do for us consumers and please keep up the good work.

title: I just cut cable and this think works with Direct TV now
body: this thing works, its done everything I wanted it to do, so my concerns with other reviewers will be addressed in this reviewdue to poor reviews about this with direct tv now i was skeptical about this, I nearly purchased 3 Apple Tv's, my thoughts were give this a shot, if it works I got everything i wanted for less than the price of 1 apple tv.we had considered for some time cutting cable due to the rising costs associated with it and we were streaming more than we were looking for live shows, kids use Prime and Netflix and I watch the news in the morning and at night, and a few shows on discovery network/ food network/ and E for the wifewe swapped cable for fiber and have a 1 gig plan for $65/ month vs our normal cable bill of $200+we still have netflix at $16( 4k)and we got dicrect TV now ($40)after tax were sitting at $130 a monthwe have 3 fire tv's running and none of them have had an issue operating anything on themI read a lot of reviews about the DTVN not working well, in my opinion its gotta be a bandwidth issue for those having issueswere running 2-3 streams at a time and have not had any issues yet (2 weeks in)in order to get the best out of a streaming device you have to have the speed (bandwidth) and the wifi strength to accomplish thisas i said before we have a 1 gig speed package and the google wifi mesh networkwe run 25 of 29 items on the network off of the wireless and none of our TV's are wired only my work stuff/ computers areall three firesticks are within 15 feet of a mesh node in my home networkmy wireless speeds all over the house are consistently 240 mbps + where as my wired speed is 900+mbpsthe closer the item to the mesh node the faster it is, with it peaking around 450 on a wireless networkwe have a sony 4k 65 and a Denon receiver as our main setup, the firestick plugs into the dennon and the remote for the firstick controls the TV, the denon, and the stick... 1 remote to rule them allwere able to use voice search in prime, netflix and direct tv now, yes there are quircks to it, but it worksfor example in DTVN once you click search you can then use voice, if you did voice prior to click search it will only search amazon primeother TV's are a Sharp/roku and a Samsung, I realize the roku tv does not need a firestick but we dont have a voice remote and i wanted to keep it simple so that all tv's had the same functionalitythe fire remote has no problem operating these as well for power and soundPicture quality and sound quality is great, cant tell a difference between it and our old cable companyI did read a few concerns about the firestick and DTVN streaming when the TV is off using data, I have not come across that issue personally as you click the home button and then power off the tv with the firestick remotejust to be sure it wont do it, say all night long, I set up a parental control via the google wifi for the firesticks, were 1 I can limit it to "x" hours a day, but 2 I shut the internet off to them overnight on a timerI must admit i got AM and PM mixed up and the wife said the stick wasnt working, no internet to it and then it dawned on me, a few clicks in my google wifi app and it was resolved and streaming againwere 2 weeks in and its been great so far, we have the same TV shows we watched, the same streaming services we used, with faster internet at a cost thats $70/month less than cableyes i had to buy a new wifi router( was going to do it anyway as the airport was old) and yes i had to buy 3 fire sticks$370 for the items, just shy of 6 months is my break even point, but if we back the wifi router out, as i was going to upgrade it regardless it puts me at just under 2 months to break even as i got the firesticks on sale

title: I love this firestick -- freedom from fios and not looking back ......
body: I LOVE this firestick.I spent about five days online learning about streaming, the various devices and options. I do not have a smart TV, so I was not sure I could even stream video. I learned an HDMI slot would allow you to stream. The website I read had listed another device as its number one choice, but I also read that the company had struck a customer for $65 for a "turn on fee." NOT a good sign. So I focused on Firestick and am I glad I did.Okay, so I'm pretty self-sufficient with most computer stuff, but I'm tech challenged when it comes to cell phones and other technology. All this was new to me. I want to thank everyone who answered what may have seemed like an endless array of dumb questions, but I wanted no ugly surprises after I bought the device and got it set up. The Firestick arrived in two days, well packed and everything inside I did recognize. I do not have a smart TV, but I have two HDMI slots and thought we can do it with one of those. One slot is now where I get the video from the FIOS, tv from cable box. This wonderful device does set itself up, it's easy to follow, I was anxious for no reason. It does everything but tap dance and sing a lullaby. However, I got stuck when I had no audio that would from my stereo speakers. I chose the right option, tried over and over but no audio. A phone call to Amazon and I was transferred to an amazing tech who had me move the device to my stereo HDMI slot. Who'd a thunk? It never occurred to me to use those. My TV I got in 2012, the Sony receiver I got in about 2015 after an electric mishap zapped my wonderful but old Yamaha. Once that was set up in the AV receiver, the firestick worked wonderfully.I have now had it for about six days and the picture is fabulous. I watched two Amazon prime movies, no fee, and also got Netflix, and love that. I even went to bed before on film was over and Amazon kept my place so I could finish it. don't really start watching TV until the evening, but I do like to watch the news, then mostly movies. I feel that nobody should be gouged for a little entertainment. If you are disgusted with the ever increasing cost of cable or fios, go ahead and cut the cord!That said, I want to add that there is a fee of course for the Amazon prime, but you get the free shipping, and a discount at Whole Foods. I stick to what's free on Amazon video, and if there's no fee next to it, I watch. I just can't afford to have that cost creep. Some folks said that the free movies Amazon weren't that good, but I think there is enough freebee films to be worth it. I also like Netflix. Very good selection, compared to what they stick you with on the cable and fios, it's a deal. I have Frontier, and I know they are not the worst. But in the beginning of this year they jacked everyone's bills by at least 10 percent, plus they are forever getting rid of good channels or putting then in with the more expensive bundles. Also, I don't watch sports. Nothing against all the sports fans out there, but I don't think I should pay for a "regional sports fee" for something I don't use. Then, of course, there are the taxes. Taxes on some bills have been upwards of $25. My only complaint is giving up the music channels. I haven't looked but I'm sure there is a fee if you buy any tunes. I'm used to going to the music channel which goes by decade or genre.All in all I think the costs for Firestick and app is fair, the quality of the video is great. The set up was easy, everyone who answers questions is very helpful. Next I am going to either get Hulu or DirecTV for live TV and news, and cut all but the internet cord. If you are on on the fence about this, just do it. The photos show hold old my tech is. I have older speakers (can't afford to update yet) and this sleek firestick amongst all those wires. This little remote replaces almost all the other three. I haven't tried the Alexa yet.

title: Everything I wanted
body: DISCLAIMER: For people looking for a device that lets you access movies for free- this is not one of them. Please do your research before you buy it and write a bad review on this. It’s like a Roku but Amazon. It allows a regular tv essentially become a smart tv. You still need subscriptions for the streaming apps/websites.Anyway to my review.It did take a bit of time to download the updates but it wasn’t too long. Maybe 10 minutes? Anyway. I have a VERY outdated TV that I lost the remote to. The only thing I had to use for streaming was a PS4, and as much as it worked, I didn’t like having to use the controller for it and I had to get up and turn it on which wasn’t a huge problem but when I went to turn the volume up I kept changing the channel. I’ve been wanting a roku but they’re so expensive, so I decided on a fire stick. Now I did this on a whim because the only fire stick I had ever interacted with was a jail broken one so it was very glitchy and you had to power it off a lot, but I didn’t know it was jail broken at the time.It was super easy to set up and it powers off my tv and changed the volume! Logging into streaming services was super easy and the remote is very responsive. I love the fact that the device comes with an “hdmi” extender in case the fire stick itself is too wide to fit into the hdmi port, and if you have an older tv you know it’s like boxed in, in a way so I can still use the fire stick. It has an Alexa component and you can use it by saying “Alexa” or just press and holding the mic button, which works for me because I have an Alexa dot so if I said Alexa it would set all of my devices off lmao. But it’s super easy friendly.Also for the parents: the moment it’s getting set up it has the option to set it up with parental controls if you’re putting it in a child’s room or where a child can access it. I’m not sure what the controls or what it restricts because I didn’t set mine up with it. But it’s there.It even has games!! Simple ones but still really cool (also has jackbox for those who love family games)It has almost everything you can think ofEven tiktok??When it was delivered I was at work so I got the “setup” email (since I bought it and clicked the “connect with this Amazon” account at checking out) and it said you have to put batteries into the remote so I thought “great Im gonna have to find batteries when I get home”. Nope!! Comes with amazons own brand of batteries. I love when batteries are included!!!The cord the fire needs to be plugged into is actually a pretty good length so that was pretty cool.The little three line button on it to change hdmi doesn’t seem to work for me but if I just press and hold the mic button and say “change to HDMI ____” it’ll do it just fine. Maybe it’s user error but it pretty much controls everything about the tv so if you’ve lost your remote or don’t want to have to use two remotes, you only need one.The remote itself is kinda small so I feel like it’ll get lost a time or two but they do make silicone cases and you could put like an AirTag in it or something lolThe microphone picks up a lot and you don’t really need to repeat yourself. You can also control the device through your phone if you do ever lost the remote (through an app Amazon has on the App Store)You can add profiles on the device so people can have their own personalized streaming services they use etc.Overall it’s a very good investment for the price. It’s pretty much a roku but cheaper and it responds faster. Definitely recommend if you’re looking for a quick fix to an older tv or even just a tv that’s not smart. It beats spending an extra $500 on a tv to include streaming services for only the price it’s at right now.Anyway that’s all from me! I hope you found this useful!!!

title: Easy install, setup, except for impossibly stuck remote back panel.
body: Recently purchased the Vizio 55" M-series Qled tv from Amazon. I've been using Roku devices, and even have a 4k version, and it's an excellent streaming solution. However, Amazon has been improving the Fire line of streaming hardware, and since I've always had positive experiences with Amazon products, I decided to give this 4k streaming stick a chance. Of course it has the major streaming apps (with the exception of Spectrum TV app, but that's more Spectrum's decision, I believe). To be honest, the only apps I'm primarily concerned with are Prime Video, Disney +, Apple TV+ and at some point Netflix and some premium channels. But having access to apps like Pluto, Crackle, etc is welcome, too.Anyway, back to the review of the actual product.Easy to install, attach the power cord, plug into the HDMI port, plug in power, set the tv to the respective HDMI port, follow the on screen directions. Quick and easy installation and setup. If you use multiple HDMI devices, you will want to used the HDMI extender because the stick itself is a bit of a space hog, it encroaches the space of neighboring HDMI.And, the instructions say to use the usb power cube that is provided, instead of the tv USB port. So, expect the need of a power outlet, and figuring out another cable management.The one complaint is the difficulty removing the back panel of the remote. It was so tightly attached that I worried I would break the remote. Even after removing it, I am hesitant to fully close that back panel. I left a small gap in case the batteries were low powered and need to be replaced soon.One of the reasons I wanted this model is the inclusion of Dolby Video and other variants of HDR. The new Vizio TV has Dolby Vision, and HDR (10+ and HLG). And, since I updated my living room tv to 4k QLED, I wanted to have compatible formats to achieve the optimum experience.I had to adjust some settings, to match the tv. Some have complained that they couldn't get video from their streaming app. I found this issue with Disney +, and the fix wasn't complicated. I had set the Fire stick to 12 bit, from the default 8 bit. My research said my tv was 10 bit, but was 4:4:4 @ 8 bit for 4K. hahaha, almost sounds like I know what I'm talking about, eh? Anyway, once I reset that to 10 bit, the video arrived in excellent condition. To be safe, I set it back to the default of 8 bit, which might give the best picture for this specific tv. This error was my fault, since I experimented with the Fire stick settings. The same scenario regarding setting of HDR. I put it back to auto, and all is fine now.Is the user interface tilted towards Amazon fare? Of course it is, it is an Amazon product!!! And if you used Amazon Prime Video on the computer or on Roku (or other streaming devices), you'd recognize the user interface.And, to the point, the picture and sound quality from the Fire Stick is amazing. The tv 'info' notes that Dolby Video and Dolby Audio are engaged. The Fire Stick is the perfect complement to the QLED 4K tv.I will probably be replacing my Roku streaming sticks with Fire Stick 4k units, gradually (especially as I update those TV's). hint, hint Amazon....send me a couple Fire Stick 4k streamers. haha. That'll motivate me to buy more 4k tv's.Review: I am impressed with the quality of manufacture and design, more impressed with the quality of video and audio with various HDR compatibility. I might start looking into 4k Fire TV's, to have the Fire interface incorporated into the 4K tv.So, very satisfied with the installation (with exception of the remote back panel), setup and enjoying the viewing experience.

title: BUY THIS NOW!!!🤩Here's why:
body: Have you ever wanted to control your TV with your voice? Not only that, but you get to do so many other things with this product that you just got lucky for discovering. It’s very modern and easy to connect to your TV! What are you still doing here? Go buy it! Anyways, I know some of you are still here so here are some specific reasons why you should definitely buy the product. I tried it because I barely used the TV before I bought it! The reason I barely used the TV was because I barely had any channels on the cable and I don’t watch the news. I knew that the Amazon Fire Stick had many apps included and it’s made by AMAZON, come on, that’s a steal! In addition, I heard it was controlled by your voice and it was true! This product made the TV not only just a screen but actually made me want to use the TV. You can count on this being a valid review because I am a trustworthy or credible consumer. If you know me I will let everyone know if something is a good product or not. Finally, I’ve had the Fire Stick for a while now and still like it! As shown above, now you should know my background story with the spectacular Fire Stick and why you should trust me! I highly recommend this product! One reason I recommend it is because you can watch shows, movies, or videos. You can use Netflix, Disney+, Youtube, Hulu, and many more streaming apps that Amazon provides. title: Fast, easy to use, and smooth presentation of content.
body: For details as to how this item received the given rating, see below:Aside from a Roku, the Fire TV stick nailed one of the best user experiences. In going through various streaming devices, from Apple TV, other brands that have gone to the way side, to using a Chromecast, the Fire brand got it right. Streaming your favorite app for content, or even playing a game, it's just easy.In recently upgrading from an older stick, this one works great with current AC network types (even though it is compatible with previous versions from N even back to B or A, if A is even around anymore). In testing, there is yet to be a moment where there is lag or content jolts due to buffering issues.The built in Alexa module/button is convenient and works really smoothly. The IR device control is an extra bonus that I was not even expecting. On the older TV we can now control the power and audio without having to use extra controllers.There was a slight challenge in getting content from Google's play store, such as our favorite show Friends leaving Netflix. In purchasing the entire series on sale from the play store (sorry, the whole series is no longer on sale), we discovered that there was no way in getting the content to play on Amazon. In chatting with Google's support team, there was no way in getting this content to appear. However, turns out there is! If you use the YouTube app on your Fire stick, and then tie it to you Google account, you now have access to your content!Movies anywhere? Yes, but not TV show's everywhere! It's time for all of these providers to have one general platform/license that talks to everything. In the end, we the consumers feel the pain of going back and forth between different platforms, when we should be on one. The same applies to other digital content, one license for many systems or one license for one system.Apologies for the extra dialog, but it's just a tech dream that maybe will come true one day.One downside was the packaging. Why all the plastic? There was a plastic bag for my plastic controller that also had plastic all around it. It is appreciated that it was not scratched in anyway, however, unnecessary use when I'm sure Amazon can come up with better packaging.Pros: • Fast • Huge environment of apps/games/music. • Great integration with existing Amazon eco system (and other platforms, even if via work arounds for some). • Wireless AC support (Dual-Band and backwards compatible with older wireless technologies). • IR Device Control to control more with less. • Great output 4K/up to 2016p/up to 60 fps. • Alexa microphone. • Volume keys.Cons: • Packaging can use less plastic.*Note, there are two ratings applied to the reviewed item. "Product rating" and "Packaging rating". The actual product will not be impacted by the packaging as these are two separate ratings.In regards to packaging, the following are contributing factors against the "Packaging rating": • In an effort to globally reduce and promote less usage of plastic, any usage of plastic or non-environmentally safe packaging/products/material will be marked negatively. • Single use items that do not biodegrade or cannot be easily recycled (see point above on plastics). • The packaging rating is being introduced to help drive a shift to more sustainable and environmentally friendly resources.

title: Best budget media player on the market? I think so.
body: I have to say: I'm pretty impressed with the new FireStick. I previously owned a FireTV 2nd edition, which to this day no other Fire TV seems to hold a candle to, so I have to admit I wasn't expecting much out of this device.I've pretty much tested it into the ground at this point but I'm still no where near complete as I don't have some of the necessary features like Dolby Atmos and an HDR tv to really see what it can do. Even with that being said, I can attest that it does hit the 4k resolution as promised.I unfortunately could not connect this directly to my receiver(a pioneer vsx 520) as it would not pick up the 4k resolution. I opted to use the ARC function available, and that at least gave me some functionality though it doesn't quite seem to work well with anything other than the regular dolby digital 5.1 format.A few times I found it dropping the ARC connection from the TV but I honestly don't know how much of a role the stick is playing in this occurrence. I was pretty surprised how well the Wifi handles on this device. At close distances it was able to use all of my 300mbit connection down but for some reason it split the upload speed in half.Going much further out, about 40 ft, I definitely saw a huge drop in the connection showing about 40mbit down and up which was still good enough for streaming much of my media. I own an HDHomeRun Connect OTA which ran fine after an update.I'm a big KODI user, and though Amazon doesn't support the application, I found it pretty easy to get around this and install it using the Downloader app. This allowed me to test most of my personal media. Initially, I was having issues playing my DVD iso's but after updating KODI to 18.1 RC1, things started to work. I did, however, have to take off the hardware codec as the movies were choppy for some reason. Apparently, this has been an ongoing issue.If your not into DVD menu's like myself, you can just use the MKV format with the mpeg-2 codec and things will run fine. I also tried a few Blu-Rays as well. The H.264 codec with the MP4 format worked without a problem but using the MKV format with the VC1 had stuttering issues.If you have a good media server, than I just recommend transcoding with Plex or Emby as converting can take forever. The device did a great job with switching resolutions and frame rates for 24fps mode so if you have a TV that has these functions available, breath easy.Lastly I tested out some games. The FireStick unfortunately only supports three controllers at this time the Xbox One, PlayStation 4(ver 2), and the Matricom(I specifically used G-PAD BX). I grabbed RetroArch, a great multi emulation application.It would seem the PlayStation is far as you are going to get for good performance on 3D accelerated games. I tried out the Run-Ahead feature for latency reduction on some of the older titles and it worked pretty well baring a few issues with certain games. I do believe that some N64 and PSP games can work with the FireStick but it is recommend you use the separate emulators rather than RetroArch to run them.I'm no where near done with trying things out but as you can see, the FireStick 4K does hold up pretty well for the price. The interface is snappy and integration seems to be pretty painless so if you're looking for a budget media player you can use for the mean time or for a secondary room this is definitely the one I recommend.

title: Way better than I thought
body: I originally bought the Fire TV stick to watch HBO Max without streaming it from my phone - I have a Roku TV (TCL) and HBO Max was not available on Roku at that time (it is now available). However, I have now switched almost all of my streaming apps to the Fire TV stick instead of directly on the Roku TV.The main reason for this was the Fire TV remote - I was able to set it up so that pressing the ON button on the Fire TV remote turns on the TV, my sound bar, the Fire TV stick, AND switches the input on my TV to HDMI 1 (where Fire TV stick is connected), so basically I can now use just one remote.
