I have not tried it with all yet. I have set it up to allow for alexa integration and at times this has worked well as long as you understand the trigger words you have to use. It is not as easy as asking alexa to play x. You usually have to provide which streaming service you want it streamed from.This works for youtubetv YouTube amazon hulu disney+ HBOgo hbonow espn and many more and the UI is pretty easy to figure out. My mother and grandmother both have managed to figure out the interface to watch shows they want. It also works pretty well in terms of support outside the house (backyard or driveway with good wifi coverage) and with a tv or cable service if you want to as well.It does not seem to support vudu directly however by linking to movies anywhere I have full access to my library. It also does not directly support google play directly though by turning on miracast you can get it to work in lower quality. This is just a battle between these two companies that they will eventually have to work out.Very happy with this. Over the last 2 years I have given several away and purchased multiples.

title: Who needs cable?
body: I just got this thing in today and let me tell you that I am happy with my purchase. Before I continue let me just say that the blue ray player that I am using is an out dated model for Amazon Prime. Amazon will no longer be available for me on my blue ray player after 9/26/19 so they generously sent me a $25 discount on any of their fire sticks available. Even their cube. The Fire TV streaming stick (with Alexa built in, 4K Ultra HD, Dolby Vision, Includes the Alexa Voice Remote) was fairly more expensive (although not by much) and went on sale for $34.99 - $25 comes to $10.69. So I immediately jumped on it and ordered it. For under $11 I am having the time of my life with this thing. It works so damn well and much better than I could imagine or had hoped it would. With a good indoor antenna I can watch my local channels. With this fire stick I complete my television watching experience. I thank Amazon so much for their generosity because they did not have to do that. I can now watch my shows on my television in the clearest of quality. My Sony Bravia never looked so good. And, if I want to know the time, I just hold down the speaker button and say "Time" and Alexa tells me the current time, and the weather if that is what I ask for. It is an easy set up and as long as you have an HDMI input it will work flawlessly. I don't have a 4K and that does not matter. So if you read this and your HDTV is a little out dated, don't worry about it. It'll work for you too. Get it. You will be glad you did. I guarantee it. Besides, my sister has a 4K Visio and my 120 Hz 41" Sony Bravia looks better than hers. But, it is a Sony!Buy this. I have broken away from cable for the second time and now with this, the cable king is dead and buried! Why pay so much for channels I will never watch? And to a company that will raise their prices any time they want and there is nothing you can do about it and they know it. That is why they are so arrogant about it. I come from the time when color TV was new. We did not pay to watch television at that time. The most you had to worry about was someone stealing your roof antenna. Now, with an internet connection - that we need anyway - and a fire stick, you are good to go. So Go!

title: Promethean Fire Stick
body: Prometheus, himself, uses Amazon's Fire Stick as it was born of his making. This is an amazing device. I have been using PS4, AppleTV prior to this and the Fire Stick is absolutely, unequivocally, a far better device than the competitors. Firstly, the Fire Stick integrated with my Onkyo 7.1 surround system with simply allowing the Fire Stick to acquire the connection during set up. The Fire Stick controls my TV (Vizio 85") and the Onkyo sound system seamlessly. The picture and Sound Quality of the Fire Stick exceeds PS4 and Apple TV (and I am a Apple fan as I have all their products), but the Fire Stick out performs these other systems--making the other system look like blunt instruments that lesser apes bang rocks against. I am completely satisfied with the 4k Fire Stick. I wish I had gotten it years ago. Also, the Alexa integration with my bluetooth lights is awesome. Oh, and the voice search is by far better than Siri. Siri is an underdeveloped child that has been locked in a digital closet, unable to develop language and other intelligent functions. Alexa, is intelligent and fast and effective. Alexa even recognizes my own personal voice patterns and can discern if I am talking or if one of my offspring is attempting to hack my system. I love Alexa. She is a digital vixen. :) Honestly, the Fire Stick is a very cool, very low priced, system that, in comparison to Apple TV, is exceptionally better than the Apple TV. Last I check, the appletv was about $200 and the 4K Fire Stick was $50!! One would be a troglodyte if they didn't choose the 4k Fire Stick over an apple tv. No, I am not paid for this review. I have zero affiliation with Amazon or any other company or entity. These are my own, unfettered, non-paid reviews. I would gladly accept payment for this review, however. :) I highly recommend this product to anyone who wants to stream on all platforms (e.g., Curiosity Stream, Vudu, Apple TV, etc.). It's awesome. Did I mention the sound quality? My sound system never sounded better and the 7.1 is far richer in experience than ever! Seriously mind blowing. Prometheus, if he hadn't invented the device, will probably steel it like he stole Fire!

title: Excellent product-- just ordered my second one today. Highly recommended.
body: I bought an Apple TV a couple years ago in hopes of enjoying online streaming. While the device is a nice piece of equipment, in my opinion it lost out to the Fire Stick. Setup for the Fire Stick was easy. I installed the stick next to another HDMI input, and the one thing I noticed about my Sony is that they certainly didn't plan for wide HDMI plugs. I was able to find 'slim' HDMI cables on Amazon which I included with the order.Unboxed, well prepared. Quality packaging. Instructions were top shelf and an 800 number is included because you have to call in to activate your Fire Stick. The serial number is attached to your order number. The whole activation only took 10 minutes. It was so easy to login to our wi-fi router then Amazon Prime. This opened up a whole new world of new apps that I didn't have on our Apple TV. We bought the stick mainly to try out Disney Plus. D+ loaded quickly. I started watching a Disney tv series and compared it to the 2 year old Apple TV. Compared to the Apple, the Fire stick was lightning fast. And it was excellent at displaying the action scenes. On the Apple TV, some action scenes started to pixilate and blur compared to the Amazon stick-- which kept up and played the movies perfectly.About remotes: The Apple TV remote has been uncooperative to say the least. It's hard to apply a light touch to get the commands you want. And if you slide the touchpad too quickly you'll jump through a bunch of apps or movies then have to back up. The Fire Stick uses a round navigation button that goes 4 ways with an enter button in the middle. It is very instinctive to use. I like that when I say "Alexa HDMI 1" she changes the input to HDMI1. On the Apple TV I said SIRI HDMI1 and it said, "sorry we aren't able to process your request at this time."The Fire Stick was affordable yet very well made, packaged, and thought out. Amazon is doing a lot of things right and this is one of their excellent examples. Today, after comparing the Apple to the Fire stick I ordered another Fire stick and will send the Apple TV down to one of the kids who use Apple. I'm sold on the Fire Stick! Highly recommended!

title: Works GREAT with Xfinity Streaming Ap
body: Greetings! My wife and I were paying $22.50 per month ($270.00 per year) for 3 rental boxes for TVs in locations that we did not use that often. The Xfinity Streaming App (it is marked Beta but it works fine!!!) is now available for the Amazon 4K TV Stick and I installed 6 of these recently throughout our house. You have to rent at least one box from Comcast to make their TV service work. We actually have rental boxes on 2 main TVs that we watch 90 plus percent of the time and the these 6 sticks throughout the rest of the house.It was AMAZING how well these sticks work. Plan on about 10 to 15 minutes per stick out of the box to upload the latest version of firmware from the internet. I don't know how it works but the Remotes JUST WORK! I installed these sticks on TVs from at least 4 different manufactures and the remotes worked for EVERY type of TV!!! The remote "knows" what type of TV the stick is connected to, configures the remote to work with that TV, and it JUST WORKS! Sometimes my remotes had to update and it did that automatically and just worked!!! (The Amazon Remote for the sticks works MUCH BETTER than a Sony Remote for a Blue Ray player that will NOT control a Sanyo TV AT ALL.)If you are thinking of using these sticks to save rental money from Comcast do note that the Xfinity Streaming APP works MUCH better with Xfinity Broadband than it does with another broadband service, such as DSL from AT&T. There are licensing issues on some channels when using the APP with other broadband services that you don't have when you have Xfinity Broadband service. The Xfinity App WILL WORK with other broadband service but NOT all of the channels that are available on the Xfinity App will work when you are NOT using Xfinity Broadband (but most of them do!!)I wish I had bought these sticks YEARS ago! It was have saved me $270 per year on Comcast box rental fees!! My wife does NOT like change, but she has gotten used to the slightly different interface that the Xfinity App has that is slightly different from the rental boxes from Comcast.WE ARE VERY PLEASED with these products!!!!

title: NON TECHY OLD GUY Impressed
body: My techy son asked why I don't watch Amazon Prime movies when I have Prime. I said "cause it's more tech crap that I don't wanna have to deal with. I'm winding down from tech and wanting to live more simply is why!" My 55" smart TV is a about 4 years old , didn't have Prime built in to the apps, and was not downloadable internally. He ordered the 4K Fire Stick for me, sent it to me, and told me to call when it came and he would help me set it up via phone. He lives in LA and I am in Iowa. As hard as this is to say, I'm a little older (64), have run a business for almost 40 years where I had to deal with some tech stuff, and lately I have strived to get away from all the tech crap in this world and my life. I bought a big fancy Keystone Sprinter travel trailer to get away from everything, including modern technology that I had to deal with it for many years. I don't want learning curves for it anymore, BUT.... the 4K Fire Stick with Alexa remote was so incredibly simple to setup that I was very much impressed. Plug this in, plug that in, wait for the TV screen instructions (short), listen and watch a promotion video while waiting for setup (kinda boring, but oh well), and BAM........it's setup and running. I tried the voice remote and was actually thrilled not to have to roam around on the remote to search for a movie or music artist. I said "play Earth Wind and Fire" and it immediately went to the best hits album and started playing it. Wow, SWEET! Same with movies. How simple and sweet is THAT?! I guess Alexa got mad at me for asking a stupid, totally non related question (yeah, being the grumpy old smarta$$ that I am), and she quit working with voice command within 10 minutes. Yeah well kiss my a-- Alexa, ya b----! I took the batteries out for about a minute, put them back in, and Alexa works again. That is the only issue I had with everything. So....it rocks. Simple, easy setup, simple functions........I recommend it for those in my position - don't like tech anymore, stubborn old fart that doesn't want anything complicated anymore, and simple setup and use. Just buy it. SIGNED: Grumpy old stubborn non-techy guy.

title: Easy to set up and works well
body: I bought this device to take with my while traveling to hook up to hotel televisions so that I could watch streaming content on the road. This device was cheaper than the competition, and reviews also said that its support for captive portal was excellent, which is really important since I wanted to use it in hotels.Everything worked as expected, and I was able to watch streaming content from Hulu, Prime, and Netflix in my hotel. Picture quality is outstanding, and the feature set for such a small device is impressive.I set up the device at home before going on the road, downloading streaming clients and logging in. Since I have long passwords, I used the smart phone app integration to enter those instead of using the remote. Setting up the integration required turning on an obscure feature in the settings menu (as described in the remote app), and it sometimes dropped characters when I used copy/paste. So, it wasn't as seamless as I was hoping. Fortunately it worked barely well enough to get my passwords entered, at which point I went back to the remote.I had a small issue connecting to the hotel's captive portal because I couldn't figure out how to click the "Accept" button at first. It turned out I had to put the browser into "cursor" mode, and then I was able to move the pointer over the button and click it.The other small issue I had was that at first I was disappointed to have to hook up the Fire Stick to USB power because the hotel's television was far from available outlets, but the obvious solution (to people other than me) was available on the internet: The television itself had a USB port, and I was able to power the Fire TV Stick from the TV. In other words, I was able to hookup the Fire TV Stick to the television only by using the TV's HDMI and USB ports, which is what I wanted.Having Alexa and Prime directly integrated into the Fire TV Stick is really convenient, and although my son had a field day with Alexa, I haven't had a chance to explore that aspect of the device yet.I am looking forward to using this on upcoming trips.
